Other ground-based studies of NGC 1399 by Forte, Faifer, Geisler (2007) and Ostrov, Forte, Geisler (1998) suggest a similar lack of a mass-metallicity relationship over a large luminosity range.

sources.summary: The paper reports velocity broadening for NGC 1399, including 280-500 broadening with systematic uncertainty and a 90 percent confidence detection among selected targets.

quotes.quote: HCG 62, NGC 1399 and A 3112 show evidence for 400 velocity broadening.
Although there are significant systematic errors associated with the technique ( 150 ), we find broadening at the level of 280 to 500 in A 3112, NGC 1399 and NGC 4636.
There are some targets for which we detect velocity broadening with 90 per cent confidence between 300 to 500 , including A 3112, HCG 62 and NGC 1399.

sources.summary: The paper describes a bridge-like low-surface-brightness structure connecting NGC1399 and NGC1387, traced by globular-cluster contours and interpreted as ongoing interaction with possible stripping by massive NGC1399.

quotes.quote: In this figure is shown the connection between NGC1399 and NGC1387 which reflects the ``bridge-like'' stellar steam between the two galaxies made by several filamentary structures, detected by , whose existence had initially been proposed by and confirmed by . This low surface brightness structure seems to be confirmed by the GCs distribution, whose iso-density contour forms a connection between the two galaxies. This suggests an ongoing interaction between the two galaxies where a fraction of GCs, originally belonging to NGC1387 , may have been stripped by the more massive NGC1399 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1399 as the central Fornax cD galaxy anchoring its mock tracer simulations, modeling its light with a de Vaucouleurs-like profile and random-motion-dominated outskirts.

quotes.quote: We simulate only the region covered by the current discrete tracer surveys (FVSS, P+18 and S+18), covering about 1.8 deg ^2 around the cD, NGC 1399.
Indeed, for NGC 1399, which gathers most of the light in the cluster core, iodice-2016 found a Sersic index n = 4.5 , i.e. very close to n = 4 , describing the de Vaucouleurs law.

We assume no-rotation Although some rotation of blue GCs in the radial range between 4 and 8 arcminutes was measured, the kinematics of the outskirt of NGC 1399 is dominated by the random motion schuberth-2010,coccato-2013 , and an isotropic velocity dispersion tensor, and solve the radial Jeans equation d( ^2) dr = -G M_ tot () () ^2 to derive the 3D velocity dispersion ^2 along the three directions in the velocity space, and generate a full 3D phase space.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1399 as the Fornax-cluster CDG, showing two collimated radio jets, diffuse lobes, and AGN-hot-gas interaction through X-ray cavities.

quotes.quote: NGC 1399* The radio emission of NGC 1399, the CDG of the Fornax cluster, has a two-sided extended radio morphology in the form of two well-collimated radio jets terminating in two mildly diffuse radio lobes (Figure fig:chandra_vla_b b). Similar morphological features were previously discussed in and . \ Moreover, , , and found signatures of the interaction between the AGN and the hot gas, in the form of X-ray cavities, with bright rims coincident with the outer edges of both radio lobes.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 1399 with the spiked nuclei in NGC 4552 and NGC 2681, noting its smooth core and a possible far-UV nuclear point source.

quotes.quote: On 1993 FOC images the central 20'' of NGC 1399 appear as smooth as silk , i.e. no spike similar to either that of NGC 4552 or that of NGC 2681 is present. Indeed, NGC 1399 is well fit by a Nuker law of the core type, and its central surface brightness is much lower than that of NGC 2681. However, a STIS far UV spectrum of NGC 1399 recently obtained (R.W. O'Connell private communication) appear to contain a distinct nuclear point source --definitely above the background light of the galaxy's core-- which may be even brighter than the spike in NGC 4552.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes Ginga observations of NGC 1399, treating it as a non-active elliptical whose earlier 2-10 keV spectra suggested a significant extended hard X-ray component.

quotes.quote: Three non-active ellipticals -- NGC 1399, NGC 4472, and NGC 4636 Ik92,Aw91 - were observed with the Ginga LAC that has a larger >2 keV collecting area. Initial (pre- ASCA / ROSAT ) studies fit the 2-10 keV spectra with one-component models, and the higher temperatures and 2-10 fluxes as compared to subsequent ASCA and ROSAT results are suggestive of the presence of a significant extended hard component. We re-analyzed the background-subtracted Ginga top-layer spectra for these galaxies, extracted from the LEDAS data base.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1399 as a Fornax central galaxy whose surface-brightness profile, color gradient, and globular-cluster frequencies fit two diffuse stellar populations tied to blue and red globular clusters.

quotes.quote: The particular case of NGC 1399, one of the dominant central galaxies in the Fornax cluster, for which a new B surface brightness profile and (B-R_ kc ) colours are presented, is discussed taking advantage of a recently published wide field study of its GCS. The results show that the galaxy brightness profile and colour gradient as well as the behaviour of the cumulative globular cluster specific frequency, are compatible with the presence of two dominant stellar populations, associated with the so called ``blue'' and ``red'' globular cluster families. These globular families are characterized by different intrinsic specific frequencies (defined in terms of each stellar population): Sn=3.3 0.3 in the case of the red globulars and Sn=14.3 2.5 for the blue ones.

sources.summary: NGC 1399 is treated as the central Fornax galaxy whose extended globular-cluster system contaminates the fields and may have stripped blue clusters from nearby galaxies.

quotes.quote: 4 a smooth background distribution of GC candidates with a projected density decreasing from East to West, representing the GCS of NGC 1399, the central galaxy of the Fornax cluster.
We should take into account that blue clusters from the GCS of NGC 1399 extend up to 40 -- 45 arcmin from the Fornax cluster centre (Bassino et al.

The backgrounds for NGC 1379 and NGC 1387 present larger blue populations, demonstrating the large extent of the NGC 1399 system. ^ a Number of blue / red GC candidates without background corrections and Poisson errors. ^ b Number of blue / red GC candidates background-corrected and Poisson errors including the background errors.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC1399 initially stays below its limit, later has modeled luminosity about an order too high, and shows Bondi-rate sensitivity to assumed gas constants.

quotes.quote: Three limitation values in case of NGC821, NGC1399, NGC4636 are not exceeded.
In case of NGC1399 modeled luminosity is order of magnitude too high than limitation value.

E.g. for NGC1399 the mass accretion rate assuming =5/3 and =0.5 is M _ Bondi =4.6 10^ 3 M_ /yr , but if we assume different =1.4 (which is typical for the partially ionized ISM and usually assumed in ADAF models) and different =1.0 we obtain an order of magnitude higher mass accretion rate M _ Bondi =4.2 10^ 2 M_ /yr .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1399's globular-cluster system, finding blue-cluster tails toward neighbors and extended blue and red cluster radial profiles consistent with tidal stripping.

quotes.quote: For the blue GCs, we find tails between NGC 1399 and neighbouring galaxies in the azimuthal projected distribution, and the three low-luminosity galaxies show low specific frequencies and a low proportion of blue GCs. These behaviours support the idea that galaxies closer to NGC 1399, in projected distance, might be losing their blue GCs as a result of some interaction process, like tidal stripping of clusters by the giant elliptical NGC 1399 (e.g. , , , etc.). The large-scale study of the projected radial distribution of GCs around NGC 1399 shows that the blue clusters extend up to 250 kpc from the galaxy centre (45 5 arcmin) while the red ones show a steeper radial profile that reaches a radius of 140 kpc (25 5 arcmin).

sources.summary: The paper adds NGC 1399 to the SMBH-Sersic sample, adopting a previously modeled light profile and using an external direct SMBH mass estimate.

quotes.quote: We have also been able to include NGC 1399 (Houghton et al. \ 2006), which previously had no direct SMBH mass estimate. This galaxy's light-profile has been modeled in D'Onofrio, Capaccioli, Caon (1994) and we adopt their S ersic index having obtained the same value from our own fitting of their data.

The SMBH mass for NGC 3031 and NGC 1399 are from Merritt Ferrarese (2001a) and Houghton et al. \ (2006), respectively, and the mass for NGC 4374 is from Maciejewski Binney (2001, with updated errors taken from Kormendy Gebhardt 2001).

sources.summary: The paper uses HST globular-cluster luminosity functions for NGC 1399 to derive Fornax distance moduli and says those values confirm earlier TOM estimates.

quotes.quote: Recently, Grillmair and co-authors used the Hubble Space Telescope to obtain luminosity functions in B for NGC 1399, NGC 1404 and NGC 1316 (which is a special case and will be discussed shortly). The resulting distance modulus values (using our Milky Way TOMs) are 31.41 0.11 mag ( B ) and 31.34 0.11 mag ( V ) for NGC 1399, and 31.69 0.23 mag ( B ) for NGC 1404. While the values for NGC 1399 confirm the TOMs from Table tab:kohle , the value for NGC 1404 is also in agreement with earlier work by Richtler et al. .

gmw_00000329.name: NGC 7217
gmw_00000329.overview: NGC 7217 is an isolated, unbarred early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0004203, arXiv:1103.1692] classified as a LINER/Seyfert [arXiv:astro-ph/0209266]. It features a strong bulge [arXiv:1304.3529], three concentric star-forming rings [arXiv:astro-ph/0004203, arXiv:1401.3366], a counter-rotating stellar disk [arXiv:1803.01501, arXiv:astro-ph/9606099], and a circumnuclear polar gas disk [arXiv:1401.3366, arXiv:1103.1692].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 7217 as a nearby almost face-on Sb spiral with a three-ring star-forming structure, no detected bar, and 149 identified young star groupings.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 7217 is an almost face--on spiral of Sb type, with no major neighbours (the nearest galaxy is the small irregular NGC 7292, at about 1.3 Mpc distance). NGC 7217 is 15.5 Mpc distant (assuming H _0 =75 km/sec/Mpc) with an integrated B magnitude of 11.17 mag and isophotal diameter D _ 25 =3.9 . The physical scale of the galaxy image is 21 pc/pixel.

Images of NGC 7217 show, as already noted by Verdes--Montenegro et al. (1995) and Buta et al. (1995), a 3-ring structure characterized by a high H _ luminosity, suggesting an active current star formation rate. In particular, the outermost ring (about 6 kpc from the galactic centre) contains about 2/3 of the neutral hydrogen mass in the galaxy. Usually, the presence of rings in galaxies is associated with evidence of bar--like structures (see Buta Crocker 1991 and references therein). However, NGC 7217 is remarkably axisymmetric and no bar has been detected so far even in the infrared. The application of our classification algorithm for this galaxy led to an identification of 149 YSGs, which are shown in Fig. 2b.

sources.summary: The paper maps molecular gas in NGC 7217, emphasizing its broad CO nuclear ring, regular rotation, LINER/Seyfert classification, three rings, and possible gaseous bar/spiral structure.

quotes.quote: We present CO(1-0) and CO(2-1) maps of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 7217, obtained with the IRAM interferometer, at 3" and 1.5" resolution respectively. The nuclear ring (at =12"=0.8kpc) is predominant in the CO maps, with a remarkable surface density gradient between the depleted region inside the ring and the inner border of the ring. The CO nuclear ring is significantly broader (500-600pc) than the dust lane ring. The CO(2-1)/CO(1-0) ratio is around 1, typical of optically thick gas with high density. The overall morphology of the ring is quite circular, with no evidence of non-circular velocities.

NGC 7217 is an (R)SA()ab galaxy classified as a LINER/Seyfert. It is particularly axisymmetric, and possesses nuclear, inner and outer rings, at 8, 31 and 77" (Buta et al. 1995). Dominated by a massive and extended bulge, the spiral structure is flocculent, and a possible oval distortion might be the vestige of an ancient bar, which was responsible for the formation of the three rings.

sources.summary: The review highlights NGC 7217 as an isolated Seyfert/LINER with three rings, a dominant bulge, CO concentrated in a nuclear ring, and a possible infalling mini-spiral.

quotes.quote: NGC 7217 is an early-type Seyfert/Liner galaxy, isolated, and with a remarkably regular morphology. It possess 3 rings at 10.7, 32 and 77 `` (0.75, 2.2 and 5.4 kpc), interpreted as nuclear ring, inner and outer rings of an oval distortion (Buta et al. 1995). The blue color of the rings indicates star formation. NGC 7217 has a conspicuous bulge dominating the luminosity of the galaxy. The center of the galaxy has been mapped with the IRAM interferometer (cf Fig. 1).

The molecular gas is concentrated in the nuclear ring, which has an abrupt profile towards the center, delineating a real hole in the gas. The molecular gas then follows the flocculent spiral arms that begin just outside the ring. The dust mini-spiral inside the ring (Fig. 1) has a counterpart in CO emission, much better seen in the CO(2-1) line.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7217 as a key isolated Sab case with a 350 pc inner polar gas disk, old nucleus, ring star formation, and a two-minor-merger origin.

quotes.quote: NGC 7217 An interesting case of a spiral galaxy with the inner polar ionized-gas disk having the radius of only 350 pc n7217pr is represented by an isolated Sab galaxy NGC 7217; here we show the recent HST image of the central part of the galaxy (Fig. n7217 ) where the inner ionized-gas polar disk can be seen `by eye' in the narrow photometric band centered onto the emission lines H + [ NII ] . Its neutral hydrogen disk, 0.7 10^9 M , extending to R 8 kpc, is coplanar to the stellar disk and rotates just as the stars; at the outer edge of this disk intense star formation in a ring is observed though the visible gas density is below the gravitational stability threshold n7217_h1 . Recently we have studied the origin of the complex structure of NGC 7217 in detail last7217 , and here we discuss this galaxy as a pure key point revealing possible formation mechanisms of the inner polar disks.

The galaxy being an early-type spiral without a bar, possesses meantime three rings of current star formation verdes95 . Interestingly, the age of the nuclear stellar population, inside the circumnuclear starforming ring, is very old -- older than 10 Gyr. Obviously, despite violent processes of gas radial re-distribution and external gas accretion betrayed by the inner polar disk presence, the gas has never reached the very center of NGC 7217 for the last 10 Gyr.

We have found that only at least two independent gas-rich minor-merger events can provide a full list of properties: the inner polar disk is formed by an accretion of a gas-rich dwarf from an inclined retrograde orbit, and the outer flaring ringed starforming disk is shaped by merging a prograde-orbiting satellite.

sources.summary: NGC 7217 lacks a bar, and its torque pattern may reflect an oval stellar distribution or inclination uncertainty; the paper finds little convincing gravitationally driven gas transport.

quotes.quote: The two-dimensional torque map shows a pattern typical for an oval potential. Although NGC 7217 has no bar, an oval stellar distribution is possible, however it might be just an deprojection effect due to uncertainties in the inclination angle. The latter case seems to be plausible as the torque pattern is aligned with the major axis (PA=265.5 ) in such a way as expected from an error in inclination angle (see subsec:torque_error ). For the outer disk we found no strong evidence for gas transport caused by the gravitational torque. At the center we found only small evidence for gas inflow from (0.5-0.2) kpc with a maximum of d L/L=0.1 , but this is presumably due to the uncertainty in the inclination angle.

sources.summary: A three-ring nonbarred galaxy whose outer ring supplies most H-alpha flux, with additional HII regions around the sharp inner ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 7217 (Figure img6 , bottom row). This galaxy is classified as type (R)SA()ab. It has a large, flocculent outer ring with a sharp, well-defined inner ring inside the bright bulge. The outer ring appears to be the main source of H flux from this galaxy. There is a wide zone of HII regions surrounding the inner ring, and this zone has a sharp, ring-like boundary on its outer edge.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7217 as an unbarred early-type spiral with rings and a circumnuclear polar gas disk; spectroscopy finds a thin intermediate-age inner disk and thick, metal-poor younger outer disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 7217 is an unbarred early-type spiral galaxy having a multi-segment exponential light profile and a system of starforming rings of the unknown origin; it also possesses a circumnuclear gaseous polar disc. We analysed new long slit spectroscopic data for NGC 7217 and derived the radial distributions of its stellar population parameters and stellar and gaseous kinematics up to the radius of 100 ( 8 kpc). We performed the dynamical analysis of the galaxy by recovering its velocity ellipsoid at different radii, and estimated the scaleheights of its two exponential discs. The inner exponential stellar disc of NGC 7217 appears to be thin and harbours intermediate age stars ( t_ SSP 5 Gyr). The outer stellar disc seen between the radii of 4 and 7 kpc is very thick ( z_0 = 1 3 kpc), metal-poor, [ Fe/H ] <-0.4 dex, and has predominantly young stars, t_ SSP =2 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 7217 with NGC 4826, describing an axisymmetric molecular ring, regular rotation, outward gas flow, and no significant ongoing nuclear fueling.

quotes.quote: NUGA observations of the LINER NGC 7217 have been compared with numerical simulations in . The emerging scenario in this LINER is apparently in direct contrast with what is found for NGC 4826: in NGC 7217, molecular gas is piled up in an axisymmetric ring and kinematics is seen to be dominated by regular rotation. N-body simulations developed in served to monitor the formation and evolution of the gas ring. Results from these simulations show that gas inside the ring experiences positive gravity torques from the stellar oval perturbation which builds up the resonant ring near the ILR; hence, gas in the inner 700 pc is experiencing an outward flow. As for NGC 4826 (but for totally different reasons) we find no evidence of significant ongoing fueling of the NGC 7217 nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7217 as a non-barred ringed galaxy with a spectacular blue outer ring, round outer isophotes, strong bulge, and subtle oval response.

quotes.quote: NGC 7217 is a well-studied nearby example showing a spectacular blue outer ring in colour index maps (Buta et al. 1995b; Fig. non-barred-rings , top row). At low light levels, the isophotes in NGC 7217 become almost exactly round, suggesting an extreme bulge-dominated system like the Sombrero Galaxy, NGC 4594. The strong bulge could therefore be the reason NGC 7217 is non-barred. Buta et al. (1995b) suggested that the strong outer ring formed in response to a subtle broad oval in the mass distribution.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 7217 as an unbarred nuclear-ring galaxy with counter-rotating components, likely tied to a past minor merger and a more circular ring morphology.

quotes.quote: Three sample galaxies---NGC 7217, NGC 7742, and UGC 3789---are known to be unbarred, which thus casts doubt on the notion that a bar may be an essential driver to form the nuclear ring.

For both NGC 7217 and NGC 7742, both nuclear rings and counter-rotating components (with respect to the host stellar background disk) have been reported (see de Zeeuw et al. 2002 and Merrifield Kuijken 1994 for NGC 7742 and NGC 7217, respectively). This can be interpreted as having resulted from a past minor merger with a gas-rich dwarf galaxy on a retrograde orbit silchenko2006 , and the resulting merger-induced ovals could have had similar dynamical effects as those associated with bars, thus potentially leading to ring formation. In the presence of these counter-rotating components, the corresponding ring morphologies tend to be relatively more circular than those in barred galaxies, as shown in Figure 2.

sources.summary: NGC 7217 is described as a ringed LINER whose axisymmetric molecular ring and outward gas flow inhibit, rather than feed, the central AGN.

quotes.quote: We have compared the NUGA observations of the ringed LINER NGC 7217 with numerical simulations in Paper II (Combes et al. ). This galaxy is in direct contrast with what we observe in NGC 4826: in NGC 7217 the molecular gas is distributed in an astonishingly axisymmetric ring (Fig. Fig:1 ). N-body simulations, including gas and star-formation, monitor the formation and evolution of the ring; results from these simulations led us to conclude that gas inside the ring, instead of helping AGN feeding, is experiencing an outward flow. Only a GMC-like non self-gravitating unit is linked with the AGN at the center of the map.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 7217 as the other documented large-scale counter-rotating stellar disk case, with a secondary retrograde component revealed through absorption-line LOSVD analysis.

quotes.quote: The only other documented example of a large-scale counter-rotating stellar disk is in the Sab galaxy NGC 7217, in which Merrifield Kuijken (1994) discovered that the disk is made up from two distinct components with approximately two-thirds on prograde orbits and one-third on retrograde orbits. This smaller retrograde population only showed up after a careful analysis of the line-of-sight velocity distribution (LOSVD) derived from absorption-line spectra at a number of radii in the system: disk stars in the main prograde component follow approximately circular orbits, and so produce large peaks in the observed LOSVDs at the projected circular speed of the galaxy (relative to its systemic velocity); the counter-rotating population shows up in the complete velocity distributions as secondary peaks at close to minus the projected circular velocity. One possibly-related phenomenon is the relatively high frequency with which gas disks in early-type disk galaxies are observed to counter-rotate. Bertola et al. \ (1992) find that approximately 20 of the gas disks found in S0 galaxies rotate in the opposite direction to the stars in these systems. There is no a priori reason to suppose that stars cannot form in such gas just as they do in gas on more normal prograde orbits, and so we might expect counter-rotating stellar populations to form in these systems. However, the gas disks in these early-type galaxies are usually restricted to their cores, and so any counter-rotating stars that they produce are likely to be in a similar small region. Support for this scenario has come from recent observations of the Sa galaxy NGC 3593, which has been found to contain both a counter-rotating gas disk in its core and a counter-rotating stellar disk on a similar small spatial scale (Bertola et al. \ 1996). Similarly, Prada et al. \ (1996) have found that there is a counter-rotating stellar component in the bulge region of the Sb galaxy NGC 7331. Although star formation in central counter-rotating gas disks could result in these relatively small-scale phenomena, it could not produce the large-scale counter-rotating stellar populations that we see in NGC 4550 and NGC 7217, and it is not clear how closely these two phenomena are related.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 7217 as a low-equivalent-width LINER whose aperture-corrected FIR-radio position remains in the starburst-dominated passage of the diagnostic diagram.

quotes.quote: The remaining sources, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, and NGC 7217, are three LINERs with EW(H ) < 10 . For example, the EW(H ) of NGC 7217 obtained with a slit and a large aperture are about 3 and 6 (Ho et al 1997; Kennicutt Kent 1983), respectively. After correcting the aperture effect over this source, the position of NGC 7217 in Fig 2a may move a bit down- and leftward in the FIR-radio diagram, remaining in the SB-dominated passage.

gmw_00000330.name: J1628+3906
gmw_00000330.overview: J1628+3906 is a core-dominated triple radio galaxy with an estimated photometric redshift of 0.9 [arXiv:0810.4779]. Its large-scale radio lobes span a projected linear size of 600 kpc, while its inner components are misaligned with the outer structure, suggesting restarted activity [arXiv:0810.4779].

sources.summary: The paper presents J1628+3906 as a core-dominated triple radio galaxy with large-scale lobes, compact EVN components, and misaligned inner and outer radio structures suggesting restarted activity.

quotes.quote: We present an archival 1.4-GHz VLA B-array image of a CDT source (the galaxy J1628+3906), along with a high-resolution 5-GHz VLBI image of its core, which is unresolved with the VLA. The VLA image (Fig. fig1 , left) shows an extended lobe structure on both sides of the core at arcminute scale. Based on a photometric redshift estimate ( z_ photo = 0.9 ; SDSS DR6 ), the projected linear size of the source is 600 kpc.

There are two weak ( 2-4 mJy) components detected at 5 GHz with the European VLBI Network (EVN) (Fig. fig1 , right). The dominant direction of the inner structure -- two compact symmetric components or maybe a core--jet -- is markedly misaligned with respect to the large-scale structure. In a somewhat similar case of J1252+3310 , the source exhibits a complex, possibly curved inner jet structure. The comparison of the VLA and EVN images also reveals a structural misalignment. The observed structures in J1628+3906 and J1252+3310 can conveniently be reconciled with the ``restarting and repositioning'' scenario.

gmw_00000331.name: NGC 4676
gmw_00000331.overview: NGC 4676 is an early-stage merger of two spiral galaxies [arXiv:1701.03819, arXiv:astro-ph/0402250] featuring a bright optical bridge [arXiv:1110.3046] and two strong tidal tails [arXiv:1701.03819]. The system exhibits widespread, shock-induced star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0402248] and contains a massive tidal dwarf galaxy candidate at the tip of its northern tail [arXiv:astro-ph/0406169, arXiv:astro-ph/0402250].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4676 as the Mice merger, with two tailed galaxies, an optical bridge, component-to-component differences in PAH, CO, and H2 emission, and ionized PAHs in the northern tail.

quotes.quote: NGC 4676 Nicknamed the ''Mice'', each of the two galaxies in NGC 4676 possesses a long tail as observed in the optical light. The two galaxies of this merger system have a projected separation distance of 36.8 ( 17 kpc). In particular, the northern galaxy exhibits a long tidal tail with a projected extent of 48 kpc (measured in the NIR), visible in the FUV, near-infrared, mid-IR and HI emission (see Fig. multi_NGC4676 ). Material is being exchanged between the two disks as indicated by the presence of a bright optical bridge between them.

multi_NGC4676 , most of the PAH and CO emission (each > 90 ) originate from the northern galaxy, while the main H _2 line emission ( > 80 ) is found in the southern galaxy. This finding may suggest a significantly different stellar population or distribution of molecular gas and young stars in the merger components. The northern component has a significantly smaller [ Ne III ] / [ Ne II ] ratio (0.13) and larger PAH 7.7/11.3 m ratio (4.5) than the southern galaxy ( 0.4 and 3.5, respectively), suggesting that the average stellar population is younger and the grains may be more inonized in the northern component.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4676, the Mice merger, as an early-stage equal-mass system with two tidal tails; ionized-gas and HI kinematics give consistent time-since-pericenter and encounter parameters.

quotes.quote: In this work, we focus on modeling one of the most famous galaxy merger systems, NGC 4676 (a.k.a. Arp 242, the Mice). NGC 4676 is an early stage galaxy merger at redshift z=0.02205. It has a very distinctive morphology consisting of two strong tidal tails in the north and south of the system resembling two playing Mice. The straight tail in the northern galaxy indicates that this galaxy is almost edge-on. The southern disc seems to be slightly tilted, though still close to an edge-on view.

We used the kinematics of both photo-ionized gas ( ) and cold gas (HI) to compare the effect of using different kinematic tracers. We found consistent results for the two kinematic tracers. They are also consistent with previous models for the Mice, particularly that of .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4676 as a strongly interacting disk-galaxy pair with long tidal tails, finding shock-induced star formation better matches its morphology, kinematics, and widespread post-passage star formation.

quotes.quote: In this paper I focus on NGC 4676, a strongly interacting pair of disk galaxies with long tidal tails ( TT72 , hereafter TT72; T77 ), as a test-case for models of shock-induced and density-dependent star formation.

On account of their rounded bodies and long tails, the two galaxies making up NGC 4676 were dubbed the ``Playing Mice'' by V58 . The far left panel of Fig. fig01 shows a deep optical image, overlain by contours of H I HvG96 , while the adjacent panel shows a shallower image. The individual galaxies are NGC 4676a, to the north, and NGC 4676b, to the southeast. The length and straightness of the bright northern tail imply that we are viewing the aftermath of a nearly-direct passage roughly edge-on to the orbit and disk planes, while the curve of the fainter southern tail is consistent with a more face-on view of an inclined disk.

These results invite comparison with observations of past star formation in NGC 4676. S74 found that the spectrum of NGC 4676 is dominated by A stars, and attributed this to ``rapid, widespread star formation that effectively ceased at least 5 10^7 years ago''; this diagnosis is supported by more recent observations ( MBR93 ; HvG96 ; SR98 ; dG+03 ). The shock-induced simulations produced just such bursts at first passage ( t 0 ), and the products of these bursts are widely distributed throughout the tidal tails at later times.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4676 with IDENTIKIT, using H I tail morphology and kinematics to constrain encounter geometry, systemic velocities, parameter degeneracies, and time since pericenter.

quotes.quote: Here, four regions are allocated to each tail of ``The Mice'', NGC 4676; these regions track the H I morphology and line-of-sight kinematics of the tails HvG96 . In addition, the solution was constrained by requiring the simulated nuclei (crosses) to match the observed positions, and fall within the observed range of systemic velocities. Six parameters were specified a priori: the orbital eccentricity ( e = 1 ), mass ratio ( = 1 ), pericentric separation ( 4.5 disk scale lengths), time since pericenter ( 1.25 disk rotation periods), velocity scale and velocity zero-point are close to the values adopted in earlier models e.g. B04 .

For a system like NGC 4676, it's reasonable to fix the mass ratio = 1 , adopt an e = 1 (parabolic) initial orbit, and perform a blind search in pericentric separation p , time since pericenter t , velocity scale V , and velocity zero-point v_ 0 , as well as the angles _X and _Y defining direction of view. As the number of parameters to be determined increases, the algorithm yields solutions with comparable overall scores for many parameter combinations. In Fig. fig3 solutions have been visually graded as poor, fair, or good representations of NGC 4676. It's clear that numerical score is an imperfect indicator of quality, since the highest-scoring solutions (in the t = 0.75 , p = 0.25 panel) are classified as fair, but many good solutions do get high scores, while poor solutions consistently get low scores.

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 242 as a merging spiral pair whose northern tail hosts a massive HI/CO TDG candidate unlikely to be a projection effect.

quotes.quote: Arp 242: The northern tidal tail of the interacting system Arp 242 (NGC 4676), also known as ``The Mice'', is seen almost perfectly edge--on. A massive HI condensation is found at its tip (Hibbard van Gorkom 1996). We show in Fig. PV242 the H PV diagram of this tail. The large-scale velocity gradient along the tail has an amplitude of 200 km s ^ 1 .

As shown in this figure, an interpretation with a change in the sign of the velocity gradient before the candidate TDG would not fit the kinematics of the 5 last kpc of the tail, while an interpretation without any change in the sign of the velocity gradient is in good agreement with the observations; only one faint HII region, located 1 kpc before the center of the TDG candidate, is not exactly fitted in this interpretation. It is thus unlikely that the TDG candidate observed in the northern tail of Arp 242 is the result of a projection effect.

sources.summary: The paper uses young-cluster spectroscopy in NGC 4676 to date interaction-triggered star formation, finding two 170 Myr clusters in tidal tails consistent with shock-induced first-passage models.

quotes.quote: chien07 obtained spectra of 12 young clusters in NGC 4676 using LRIS on Keck. These spectra yielded reliable approximations for cluster age and metallicity. Among the ages obtained, two are 170  Myr, which suggests that they likely formed during the first passage of NGC 4676 barnes04 . These two objects are located in the tidal tails of the pair, which is consistent with the spatial distribution of star formation predicted by shock-induced models barnes04 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4676 as a starburst-galaxy example for optical and infrared spectra, PAHFIT decomposition, and PAHdb species contributing to its infrared emission.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:ngc4676_otico_bruto shows an optical spectrum of the nuclear region of the Starburst galaxy NGC 4676, where some emission lines are highlighted, and an infrared spectrum for the same galaxy with the main bands of PAHs indicated.

Fig. fig:spectra_decomposition_PAHFIT_ngc4676 shows the detailed decomposition of the starburst galaxy NGC 4676 from 5 to 15 m obtained with the PAHFIT code in the components: total continuum, ionic lines, stellar lines, best-fit model, and PAH features.

Fig. fig:species_ngc4676 illustrates the fitting obtained for the spectrum of NGC 4676, and it shows the main species that are contributing to the IR emission in NGC 4676. This fitting resulted in sixty species contributing to the galaxy's flux.

sources.summary: The paper presents Arp 242 as a two-spiral interaction with prominent tidal tails, arguing that its northern-tail mass condensation is genuine rather than a projection effect.

quotes.quote: We present here our results for three systems (more in Bournaud et al., 2004) where the presence of massive TDGs had previously been claimed: IC 1182, a merger in the Hercules cluster (see Fig. 2 and van Driel et al., in these proceedings), Arp 105 (NGC 3561, ``The guitar"), an interacting system between a spiral and an elliptical (see Fig. 3) and Arp 242 (NGC 4676, ``The Mice"), an interacting system between two spirals with prominent tidal tails (see Fig. 4, and Hibbard et al., in this volume).

The shapes of the position-velocity diagrams of the two other systems, Arp 105 and Arp 242 rule out projection effects: the massive condensations observed at the extremity of their tidal tails seem real. This is consistent with the detection in these TDG candidates of molecular gas probably formed in the dense HI tidal clouds (Braine et al., 2001).

Analyzing position-velocity diagrams derived from FP data obtained at the CFHT and at the ESO 3.6m, we were able to rule our projection effects as the main contributor to the condensations seen in the northern tail of Arp 242 (NGC 4676) and in the southern tail of Arp 105.

sources.summary: The NICMOS observations detect both nuclei in NGC 4676, finding an optically obscured concentrated infrared source in the northern galaxy and a smooth concentrated second nucleus.

quotes.quote: We have observed NGC 4676 in two pointings, and clearly detect the nuclei of both galaxies. The northern galaxy, NGC 4676 A (Figure f:fortysixseventysixavk ), shows a highly concentrated infrared source, completely obscured in the optical, which we identify as the nucleus. The second nucleus (NGC 4676 B) shows a concentrated nuclear region with a smooth appearance (Figure f:fortysixseventysixbvk ).

gmw_00000332.name: 1H0323+342
gmw_00000332.overview: 1H0323+342 is a nearby, radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1306.4017, arXiv:1503.03203] that exhibits gamma-ray emission [arXiv:1306.4017] and hosts a collimated, relativistic radio jet [arXiv:1711.06284, arXiv:1911.03500]. Its host galaxy displays a complex morphology, interpreted either as a one-armed spiral [arXiv:1711.06284] or a circumnuclear ring produced by a recent merger [arXiv:1711.06284, arXiv:1909.01444].

sources.summary: The paper monitors J0324 + 3410 as a radio-loud NLSy1, finding high-frequency flaring, a variable steep-spectrum low-frequency component, jet evidence, and unusually large radio polarization.

quotes.quote: J0324 + 3410 shows at least two major events present at the high-end of the bandpass which disappear at lower frequencies.

In the case of J0324 + 3410 a steep spectrum component emerges at frequencies below 10.45 GHz. It appears intensely variable with the variability events transversing the bandpass, leaving however a ``naked'' steep spectrum behind. The low-band spectral index spends less than 3 of the time in negative values and can get as steep as 0.4 , very close to the commonly assumed values of 0.5 or 0.7 ; yet an indication of a large scale jet. The significant linear polarisation seen in the source also supports the idea of a significant contribution from a jet as it is discussed in Sect. sec:pol .

J0324 + 3410 however shows values larger than those typically observed in AGN at these frequency bands ( 4 at 5 GHz, e.g. Klein2003AnA ). Figure fig:0324_pol shows its fractional linear polarisation light curve at 8.35 GHz. Its mean is of the order of 6 while at 4.85 GHz it reaches values of 6.9 .

sources.summary: The paper presents 1H 0323+342 as a nearby gamma-ray NLSy1 with an optically thick disk, possible TeV emission, high variability, and preliminary multiwavelength monitoring.

quotes.quote: Particularly, 1H 0323+342 is a source with an optically thick disk , but at the same time showing gamma-ray emission, as observed with Fermi . It is important to underline here that the Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 galaxies have typical masses < 10^8 M_ sun (while quasars and blazars generally have masses > 10^8 M_ sun ) and hence we are really dealing with a new class of gamma-ray emitting active galactic nuclei (AGN). There are even some clues that 1H 0323+342 might show TeV radiation and, moreover, it is the closest (z=0.061) the among the GeV gamma-ray emitting NLSy1s. Given that the location of the gamma-ray emitting zone in other extragalactic radio sources has been found to be as close as 100 R_G to the central black hole , this would be at odds with the high radiation density (and thus pair production probability) of the central parsec in Eddington accreting Seyferts. Since the source does not fit into the extremal hard or soft states, it might, in fact, represent a transitional case in which the inner disk heats up and blows up to a torus configuration, as the accretion rate goes down (or vice versa).

Among the four gamma-ray bright NLSy1 galaxies 1H 0323+342 is the brightest at Integral energies and in fact it has already been detected with Integral and Swift /BAT . This is crucial for variability studies, since its brightness allows us to observe 1H0323+342 with short observations. Moreover, 1H0323+342 is the best target among the four gamma-ray bright NLSy1 galaxies also thanks to its high variability.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1H 0323+342 as a GeV radio-loud NLS1 with blazar-like behavior, a one-armed or ring-like host, modeled disk emission, and detected corona emission.

quotes.quote: 1H 0323+342 ( z=0.0629 , Zhou et al. 2007). Its optical spectrum is characterized with FWHM (H ) =1600 km s ^ 1 , [ OIII ] /H 0.12, and a strong Fe II emission (Zhou et al. 2007). The source also presents remarkable hybrid behaviors of NLS1 galaxies and blazars (Zhou et al. 2007). It is well known that the flat radio spectrum above 1 GHz and the compact core plus the one-sided jet structure (Beasley et al. 2002) with high variabilities at the radio, optical, and X-ray bands, are typical features of blazars. 1H 0323+342 has a high radio loudness of R_ RL =245 (Doi et al. 2012).

The optical observation with the Hubble Space Telescope suggests that this source is hosted in a one-armed spiral galaxy (Zhou et al. 2007), but Ant o n et al. (2008) reported that the host galaxy of this source has a ring structure triggered by an interacting/merging process, which is consistent with the result of a 2D Fourier analysis of the imagery in Le o n Tavares et al. (2014). With the very long baseline interferometer observations, Wajima et al. (2014) reported that the brightness temperature of this source is greater than (5.2 0.3) 10^ 10 K and the derived equipartition Doppler factor is greater than 1.7 and the variability Doppler factor is 2.2. Its five broadband SEDs with Fermi /LAT observations in different campaigns are compiled from literature, as shown in Figure SEDs (a).

sources.summary: The paper finds 1H 0323+342 varied strongly in Fermi data, reached a rapid GeV flare with hour-scale doubling, and showed significant gamma-ray spectral curvature.

quotes.quote: Bi-weekly binned light curve of 1H 0323+342 shows that it is nearly steady in rays during the initial four years of the LAT observations, however, displays flaring behavior at two epochs in 2013, one around MJD 56,300 and the other around MJD 56,500. They are marked as H2 and H3 in Figure lc . The F_ var for this source is found to be 0.498 0.046, which is on the higher side of that obtained for FSRQs ( 0.24 0.01 ) by 2010ApJ...722..520A , thus indicating substantial flux variations.

The recent GeV flare detected from 1H 0323+342 2013ATel.5344....1C had its daily ray flux as high as (1.75 0.33) 10 ^ 6 which is 23 times larger compared to the five years averaged flux. The photon statistics during this epoch (2013 August 28 to 2013 September 1) is good enough to generate six hour binned light curve (see Figure 3hr_6hr ). In contrast to the earlier observations where ray variability of the NLSy1 galaxies were characterized over longer time scales 1 day; e.g., 2011MNRAS.413.2365C,2012A&A...548A.106F , this epoch of high activity provides evidence of extremely fast variability observed from a NLSy1 galaxy. A flux doubling time as small as 3.09 0.85 hours (assuming exponential rise of the flare) is the fastest ray variability ever observed from this class of AGN.
A statistically significant ( 4 ) curvature is noticed in the low activity state ray spectrum of 1H 0323+342.

sources.summary: The paper compares 1H 0323+342 with PKS 1502+036, finding lower gamma-ray luminosity, lower bulk Lorentz factor, an emission region inside the BLR, and optical-UV disk dominance.

quotes.quote: With this in mind, we generate SEDs of two other NLSy1 galaxies covering their GeV outbursts, namely, 1H 0323+342 2014ApJ...789..143P and PMN J0948+0022 2015MNRAS.446.2456D .

In this plot, PKS 1502+036 is slightly more luminous at optical-UV energies than 1H 0323+342, however, both the shape and the luminosity of the X-ray spectrum of PKS 1502+036 are similar to that of 1H 0323+342. At ray energies, PKS 1502+036 is more luminous than 1H 0323+342. The apparent differences in the ray band can be understand in terms of the higher Doppler boosting in the case of PKS 1502+036. For 1H 0323+342, 2014ApJ...789..143P noticed the bulk Lorentz factor of the flaring emission region as =8 , whereas for PKS 1502+036, it is 25.

Furthermore, there are few other factors which one should take into account, e.g., the location of the emission region, which was found to be inside the BLR for 1H 0323+342 and outside the BLR for PKS 1502+036. This suggests a higher magnetic field for the case of former, implying a high level of synchrotron and SSC emission. A high synchrotron emission is not reflected in the optical-UV spectrum of 1H 0323+342 due to the fact that its synchrotron radiation peaks at around sub-milimeter frequencies and thus the observed optical-UV radiation is dominated by the disk emission, even during the GeV flare see, 2014ApJ...789..143P .

sources.summary: The paper models 1H 0323+342 as a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert with jet and disk/corona emission, finding soft X-ray excess, weak broad Fe emission, rapid black-hole spin, and variable continuum components.

quotes.quote: The RLNLS1 or NLS1-Blazar 1H 0323+342 is an AGN with relativistic jets as revealed by the gamma-ray emission discovered with , and strong accretion disk emission as revealed by broad optical/UV emission lines requiring the ionising continuum from the disk. It is a nearby (z=0.061) NLS1 with strong radio ( R_ 1.4GHz = 318 ; 2011nlsg.confE....F ) and hard X-ray emission. It is known to harbour a black hole with a mass of 10^ 7 and has a hybrid nature showing properties of both the NLS1s and the blazars 2007ApJ...658L..13Z .

The optical-to-hard X-ray spectrum of 1H 0323+342 is complex, and consists of multiple spectral components -- a broadband X-ray power-law ( 1.8 ), an iron line and hard X-ray excess emission, described by a blurred reflection component ( R 0.5 ), a soft X-ray excess component, strong optical/UV emission. The strong UV emission cannot be accounted by an accretion disk alone, the excess UV emission is in the form of a steep power law ( 3-3.5 ), which most likely arises from the jet in the radio-loud NLS1. The iron line around 6.4 is weak but broad in nature, and along with the hard X-ray excess emission, consistent with the blurred reflection component with R 0.5 . The soft X-ray excess is well described by both blurred reflection and thermal Comptonization and it may well, have a contribution from both the processes. The rapid spin of the black hole and the inner extent of the accretion disk indicate that truncated disk and retrograde spin may not be necessary for launching the jets.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes 1H 0323+342 spectra, rejecting pileup as the low-energy feature cause and reporting weak Fe K emission with possible broad-line structure.

quotes.quote: 1H 0323+342: 2018MNRAS.475..404K discuss the feature in 1H 0323+342, and show that the fit can be greatly improved by allowing a higher Galactic absorption column. We therefore re-fit the data, allowing for a higher column. This improves the fit to ^2 /dof of 205/165, but leaves a narrow residual at 0.66 keV. Fitting this with a narrow Gaussian absorption line improves the fit by ^2 of 18, for 2 additional degrees of freedom. As the EPIC-pn count rate for this observation ( 4 s ^ 1 ) is above the threshold for pileup in large window mode, we extract a spectrum from an annular source region, excluding the central 9 pixels. When we fit this annular spectrum with the same model, the feature remains, so we conclude that it is not due to pileup (and we note that 2018MNRAS.475..404K found no evidence for strong pileup effects in the pn data).

In addition to the low energy residuals, 1H 0323+342 shows weak Fe K emission, and is the only source in our sample which does so. An emission feature was found in the 2009 \ spectrum Walton13 , time-averaged \ XRT spectrum 2014ApJ...789..143P , and in both \ spectra and the \ spectrum 2018MNRAS.479.2464G and interpreted as a relativistic line from the accretion disk. 2018MNRAS.475..404K found residuals in the 2015 \ spectrum consistent with two narrow emission lines at 6.4 and 6.95 keV (the neutral K line and the Fe xxvi K line).

sources.summary: The paper uses 1H 0323 + 342 as a lower-redshift NLS1 comparison, noting its lower black-hole mass, discrepant scaled SED, reduced jet-model parameters, and soft-to-hard X-ray transition.

quotes.quote: We presented a very similar analysis of the lowest-redshift NLS1, 1H 0323 + 342, in . Here we make a comparison with our findings from PKS J1222 + 0413. Obviously, PKS J1222 + 0413 black has a much greater BH mass than 1H 0323 + 342. We therefore expect PKS J1222 + 0413 to be more similar to a standard (high-mass) blazar. Indeed, when we scale a standard FSRQ jet by mass and mass accretion rate we see that discrepancy between the predicted SED and the data is much less than found for 1H 0323 + 342.

As with 1H 0323 + 342, the resultant predicted SED has a low Compton dominance and a shape much more akin to a BL Lac, albeit at a much higher luminosity than BL Lacs. We can better fit the data by turning down the magnetic field strength. (With 1H 0323 + 342 it was necessary to turn down both the magnetic field strength and the power injected into the relativistic electrons.) As with 1H 0323 + 342, a typical NLS1 SED model fits the observed NIR--X-ray SED well, if a (jet) power-law is added to account for hard X-rays. In 1H 0323 + 342 found that the X-ray photon index below 2 keV was very soft, typical of (non-jetted) NLS1s, whereas above 2 keV, the photon index was much harder, typical of nonthermal jet emission. This was interpreted as a transition from accretion-flow-dominated to jet-dominated emission.

sources.summary: Swift data show correlated X-ray, UV, and optical variability in 1H 0323 + 342, with a broken X-ray spectrum indicating jet emission plus a Seyfert-like soft excess.

quotes.quote: A strong correlation between X-ray, UV, and optical emission and simultaneous flux variations have been observed in 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022 as expected in case the jet radiation is the dominant mechanism. Correlated multiband variability favours the jet-dominated scenario also in FBQS J1644 + 2619 and PKS 2004--447. The summed X-ray Telescope spectra of 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, and FBQS J1644 + 2619 are well fitted by a broken power law with a break around 2 keV.

Moreover, in order to compare the variability at different frequencies, we quantify the observed variability of 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, FBQS J1644 + 2619, and PKS 2004--447 in optical, UV, and X-rays bands through the variability amplitude, V_ amp , calculated as the ratio of maximum to minimum flux corrected for Galactic extinction. The variability amplitude may depend on the sampling of the light curves at the different frequencies. However, at least for a comparison between different bands, the simultaneity of observations in optical, UV, and X-rays guaranteed by the Swift observations helps mitigate this problem.

The variability amplitude in X-rays of the six sources varies between 2.7 and 8.5. These jetted NLSy1 are less variable than the other non-jetted NLSy1, suggesting that a different emission mechanism is driving the flux variations.

sources.summary: The paper studies 1H0323+342 as a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy, finding energy-dependent X-ray variability and spectra requiring multiple independently varying components.

quotes.quote: 1H0323+342 is one of narrow-line radio-loud Seyfert 1 galaxies (RL-NLS1), which is a new class of gamma-ray emitting AGNs.

Therefore, we studied the energy-dependence of time variability of the X-ray emission of 1H0323+342, which have been observed by Suzaku in 2009 and 2013, in order to constrain the emission mechanism. We found that the lower energy below 1 keV and the higher energy above 7 keV show a different variability from the middle energy band, indicating at least two emission components in the X-ray band. X-ray spectrum is not a simple power-law, but requires an additional features; a broken power-law plus flat hard component, or a power-law plus a relativistic reflection component. Each spectral component seems to vary independently.

Therefore, we studied the energy-dependence of time variability of the X-ray emission of a RL-NLS1s 1H 0323+342, which have been observed by Suzaku in 2009 and 2013. Energy-dependence of time variability Fig. lc13 is a light curve of 1H 0323+342 observed by Suzaku in 2013. 1H 0323+342 varies with a time scale of several ks, but the soft X-ray band below 1 keV shows a independent behavior from the other bands .

sources.summary: The paper models 1H 0323+342 as the nearest gamma-ray NLS1, placing its gamma-ray emission within the BLR and finding a relatively underpowered jet.

quotes.quote: Here we study the origin of the ray emission in the lowest-redshift candidate among the currently-known ray emitting NLS1s, 1H 0323+342, and take a new approach. We observationally constrain the external photon field using quasi-simultaneous near-IR, optical and X-ray spectroscopy. Applying a one-zone leptonic jet model, we simulate the range of jet parameters for which this photon field, when Compton scattered to higher energies, can explain the ray emission. We find that the site of the ray emission lies well within the BLR and that the seed photons mainly originate from the accretion disc.

The jet power that we determine, 1.0 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 , is approximately half the accretion disc luminosity. We show that this object is not simply a low-mass FSRQ, its jet is intrinsically less powerful than predicted by scaling a typical FSRQ jet by black hole mass and accretion rate. That ray emitting NLS1s appear to host underpowered jets may go some way to explaining why so few have been detected to date. galaxies: active -- galaxies: jets -- galaxies: Seyfert -- gamma-rays: galaxies -- galaxies: individual: 1H 0323+342 The detection of several narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxies by the Fermi Gamma-Ray Space Telescope suggests the existence of a rare, new class of ray emitting active galactic nuclei (AGN).

sources.summary: The review presents 1H 0323+342 as a nearby radio-loud NLSy1 with blazar-like jets, rapid gamma-ray variability, multi-band variability, and merger-related host structure.

quotes.quote: The most robust arguments to support the presence of closely aligned relativistic jets in a radio-loud NLSy1 galaxy, 1H 0323+342 ( z=0.06 ), were presented by Zhou et al. (2007). The optical spectrum of this object fulfills the usual definition of a NLSy1, whereas, it also exhibits multi-frequency behavior that is characteristics of blazars. This includes a flat radio spectrum at GHz frequencies and a compact core plus one-sided jet structure on milliarcsecond scale at 8.4 GHz resolved with Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA). The source is highly variable at the radio, optical, and X-ray energies.

Interestingly, the 2013 September outburst of the nearest known NLSy1 source, 1H 0323+342, revealed a rapid ray flare with the shortest flux doubling time down to 3 hours (see Figure fig:1H323_g_lc , Paliya et al. 2014) indicating the ray emission to be originated from an extremely compact region in the jet. This kind of enigmatic ray behavior is typically observed from FSRQs class of blazars (e.g., Tavecchio et al. 2010; Tanaka et al. 2011; Abdo et al. 2011; Paliya 2015; Shukla et al. 2018). X-rays: Unlike the surveying operation of the LAT, X-ray telescopes operate mostly in pointed mode, thus denying a continuous sky coverage to explore the X-ray variability behavior of NLSy1 objects. However, existing observations provide evidences about their intriguing nature. For example: a comparison of the and X-ray light curves of 1H 0323+342 led to the observation of a number of `orphan' X-ray flares with no ray counterparts (Figure fig:1H323_g_lc , see also Paliya et al. 2014).

The Hubble Space Telescope imaging of the nearest NLSy1, 1H 0323+342, reveals a clear extended structure, possibly a spiral arm or a star-forming ring due to a recent merger (see Figure fig:host ; Zhou et al. 2007; Ant o n et al. 2008; Le o n Tavares et al. 2014).

sources.summary: The paper treats 1H 0323+342 as a nearby jet-dominated NLS1/blazar-like source with mostly stable NuSTAR flux and hard non-thermal emission.

quotes.quote: 1H 0323+342 1H 0323+342 is a nearby source z=0.061 , Marcha1996 , characterized by a flat radio spectrum and an IBL-like SED. It is listed among the blazars of uncertain type in the BZCAT catalog, however it has been classified by other authors as a ray emitting narrow-line Seyfert 1 ( NLS1), a rare class of AGNs with low black hole masses . In these sources, the jet emission contributes to the hard X-ray radiation, resulting in power-law dominated spectra. found that the broadband SED of 1H 0323+342 shows a high Compton dominance, as for FSRQs, but the luminosity is similar to a BL Lac.

\ observed this source 7 times (once in 2014 and the remaining 6 times in 2018). In these observations, the flux was found to be mostly at the level of (4-6) 10^ 12 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 and only on MJD 58350.62 it increased up to (9.13 0.03) 10^ 12 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 . The photon index remained approximately constant ( 1.8 ) in all observations.

sources.summary: The paper models 1H 0323+342 as the nearest gamma-ray NLS1, with a beamed radio jet, superluminal knots, compact variability, and SEDs best explained by a disc-BLR external-Compton scenario.

quotes.quote: 1H 0323+342 is the closest source among currently known NLS1 galaxies (z=0.0625, Landt_2017 ). 1H 0323+342 is thought to be moderately radio-loud, with radio-loudness (RL) ranging from 4 to 25 ( Zhou_2007 ). characterized the pc-scale jet structure and kinematics of 1H 0323+342 using multi-epoch radio VLBA observations (MOJAVE Monitoring of Jets in Active galactic nuclei with VLBA Experiments programme, ), complemented with single-dish Effelsberg and IRAM data. In addition to the compact stationary core, six moving knots were identified, among which five were found to have superluminal apparent velocities (1-7) , suggesting relatively low viewing angle values, 4 13 .

In the disc BLR scenario, reasonable account of the SEDs is achieved with an emitting region located at R_ = 2.5 10^3 \ [ R_G ] . The relativistic jet peaks at sub-millimeter frequencies. Kynoch_2017 attributed the IR part of the SED, as defined by the Spitzer and the WISE data, to thermal emission from the extended dusty torus, thus requiring that the synchrotron emission does not contribute substantially to it. In the highest activity state this may not be fulfilled, but those IR data are not simultaneous nor contemporaneous and for an even more powerful jet, one might have a dominating synchrotron component. The thermal disc emission is a major contribution at optical/UV wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper reports that 1H 0323 + 342 has a quiescent radio spectrum plus a rapidly evolving high-frequency component, type-4 variability, and stronger high-frequency events.

quotes.quote: 1H 0323 + 342: As it can be seen in Figure fig:LC-nlsy , its radio spectrum displays a quiescent part reaching up to roughly 10 GHz beyond which a high frequency component (hereafter HFC) appears. Its mean spectral index is of the order of 0.23 (calculated between 2.6 and 10 GHz), not very different from a typical value of 0.5 ( S ^ ). The HFC shows intense spectral evolution which gradually shifts its peak progressively towards the steep spectrum component.

The pace of the evolution is remarkably fast causing significant displacements within the month that typically separates two observations. The variability pattern according to the classification discussed in Sect. classes , is of type 4. Its light curves although only shortly sampled, shows a collection of events more prominent at higher frequencies and with cross-band lags indicative of the spectral evolution superimposed on a long term decreasing trend.

sources.summary: The paper studies 1H 0323+342 as a Fermi-detected radio-loud NLSy1 with orphan optical/X-ray flares, disk-dominated optical/UV emission, relativistic-jet signatures, and mixed FSRQ/RQ-NLSy1 characteristics.

quotes.quote: We present a multi-wavelength study of the radio-loud narrow line Seyfert 1 galaxy (NLSy1), 1H 0323+342, detected by Fermi Gamma Ray Space Telescope . Multi-band light curves show many orphan X-ray and optical flares having no corresponding ray counterparts. Such anomalous variability behavior can be due to different locations of the emission region from the central source. During a large flare, ray flux doubling time scale as small as 3 hours is noticed.

Few physical properties such as prominent accretion disk emission, requirement of EC mechanism to explain ray emission along with the presence of significant curvature in the ray spectrum of 1H 0323+342 indicates its similarity with FSRQs, albeit at low black hole mass end, whereas the X-ray properties of the source are found to be similar to that of conventional RQ-NLSy1 galaxies. These dual characteristics along with the fact that it is the nearest NLSy1 galaxy with a detected relativistic jet, makes the study of this source of utmost importance.

sources.summary: The paper lists 1H 0323 + 342 among Fermi-detected gamma-ray NLSy1 galaxies and says its flare and hard X-ray spectrum point to a powerful relativistic jet.

quotes.quote: Five radio-loud NLSy1 galaxies have been detected at high significance by Fermi LAT so far: 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, and PKS 2004 447 , with a redshift between 0.061 and 0.585. The average apparent isotropic luminosity of these sources in the 0.1--100 GeV energy band is between 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 and 10 ^ 47 erg s ^ 1 , a range of values typical of blazars . This may be an indication of a small viewing angle with respect to the jet axis and thus a high beaming factor for the ray emission, similarly to blazars.

An intense ray flaring activity was observed by LAT also from 1H 0323 + 342 . This is another indication that radio-loud NLSy1 are able to host relativistic jets as powerful as those in blazars. The X-ray spectra of NLSy1 are usually characterized by a steep photon index ( _ X > 2, ). On the contrary, a relatively hard X-ray spectrum was detected in the Swift /XRT observations of SBS 0846+513 , PMN J0948 + 0022 , 1H 0323 + 342 , and PKS 1502 + 036 .

sources.summary: The paper highlights 1H 0323+342 as a nearby gamma-ray NLS1 with a spiral or ring-like host, radio variability, and a one-sided core-jet VLBI morphology.

quotes.quote: We also present high-resolution radio imaging of 1H 0323+342, which is remarkable for its spiral or ring-like host.

1H 0323+342 shows a core--jet morphology characterized by the prominent core, a straight jet, and a total absence of emission for the receding side of the jet; i.e. no counter-jet is detected. A stationary feature is seen close to the core while six components move along the relativistic jet. Five jet components show superluminal motion with velocities from 1 up to 7 2015PhDT.......232K,2016arXiv160803232F . The source shows fast variability seen with single-dish and VLBI monitoring alike. The flaring events are of low to mild amplitude, but more rapid compared to the typical long-term behavior of blazars. 1H 0323+342 shows high brightness temperatures. While report a Doppler factor of 3.6 at 14.6 GHz, and a higher one ( = 4.3) at 2.64 GHz, we deduce a more extreme value of T _ b = 5.7 10^ 12 K and an associated Doppler factor of 5.2, from the most rapid and largest flux density variation.

sources.summary: The paper finds 1H 0323+342 has a steep spectrum with possible curvature, several adaptive-bin flares, and a compact emission region implied by rapid flux doubling.

quotes.quote: 1H 0323+342: Its spectrum is steep, =2.86 0.04 , and the are detected up to 10 GeV. There is a marginal evidence of a spectral curvature around 1 GeV (Fig. sed_all (f) black curve) and both LP or PLEC ( =1 ) models can better explain the observed data (blue and red curves in Fig. sed_all (f), respectively). However, because of low statistics ( 3.1 ) PL modeling cannot be excluded.

The adaptively binned light curve calculated for 15 uncertainty shows several periods when the flux significantly increased (Fig. var_all [ ] red data). The most significant increase was observed on MJD 56534.3 when within 16.2 hrs the flux was as high as (1.98 0.31) 10^ 6 photon cm^ 2 s^ 1 and the photon index hardened to =2.22 0.15 . During this flare, the flux-doubling time was as short as 0.49 days, implying the emission is produced in a very compact region, 1.2 10^ 15 cm bagh .

sources.summary: The review highlights 1H 0323+342 as an unusually extended, gamma-ray loud NLSy1 with blazar-like variability, polarized pc-scale jet emission, and atypically high apparent speed for its radio luminosity.

quotes.quote: There are currently only 16 known examples with radio-emitting regions larger than 20 kpc, with the largest (1H 0323+342) having a projected extent of 140 kpc . This rare group of NLSy1s are all very radio loud ( R > 100 ), and 2/3 of them have classical lobe-core-lobe morphology. Fermi has so far detected ray emission from 6 of these NLSy1s, and the relatively high core dominance in their radio images would suggest that their core emission is likely Doppler boosted by relativistic bulk motion of pc-scale jets viewed at small angles to the line of sight.

It is worth noting the location of 1H 0323+342 at the extreme upper left edge of the distribution. This NLSy1 displays blazar-like variability at all wavelengths , has appreciably high linear polarization in its pc-scale jet , and appears to have an atypically large apparent speed (9.1 ) for its radio luminosity ( 2 10^ 24 ). This could be indicative of a very high intrinsic jet speed and/or high Doppler boosting factor (see, e.g., ).

sources.summary: J0324 + 3410 is the best-sampled NLSy1 for EVPA randomness; the paper finds a preferred optical EVPA and a projected magnetic-field orientation about 40 deg from its radio jet.

quotes.quote: J0324 + 3410: the randomness of the EVPA Intrigued by the dependence on the synchrotron peak frequency of the randomness of the EVPA , we examined the distribution of the EVPA in the best-sampled case in our dataset, J0324 + 3410 (Fig. fig:J0324_evpa_hist ). As shown in the left panel of Fig. fig:J0324_evpa_hist , the EVPA exhibits a preferred angle which can be approximated by the median of 6.7^ . In we show that the 15 GHz radio jet has a position angle of around 124^ . Assuming then that the transmitting plasma is transparent at optical wavelengths the projected magnetic field (perpendicular to the observed EVPA ) will be at 40.7^ with the radio jet; this orientation is ambiguous as to whether a poloidal or a toroidal configuration is dominant in that part of the flow (Fig. fig:J0324_evpa_hist right hand panel).

In the case of J0324 + 3410 we show that EVPA shows a preferred orientation which corresponds to a projected magnetic field of around 40^ to the radio jet. This orientation is ambiguous with regards to whether a toroidal or a poloidal field dominates at those scales.

sources.summary: The paper maps 1H 0323+342 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy, revising evidence on its black hole, surroundings, and self-similar radio jet structure.

quotes.quote: Taking advantage of the most recent measurements by means of high-resolution radio observations and other multiwavelength campaigns, it is possible to elaborate a detailed map of the narrow-line Seyfert 1 Galaxy 1H 0323+342 . This map will open the possibility of intriguing hypotheses about the generation of high-energy rays in the narrow-line region.

Indeed, J 0324+3410 has been the target of many studies at high-resolution radio frequencies , and is currently under monitoring in the framework of the MOJAVE program M onitoring O f J ets in A ctive galactic nuclei with V LBA E xperiments: https://www.physics.purdue.edu/MOJAVE/ . . It was found that the jet from J 0324+3410 is one sided with a change in shape at about 7 mas from the core, being parabolic close to the central black hole, and becoming conical as the distance increases .

Both Hada and Kovalev proposed that one solution could be to have a greater mass for the central black hole in J 0324+3410 , of the order of 10^ 8 M_ as suggested by . The aim of the present work is to revise all the available information about the central mass black hole of J 0324+3410 , its surroundings and its jet, in order to obtain a consistent picture of this source.

sources.summary: The review describes 1H 0323 + 342 as a Fermi-detected NLSy1 with superluminal jet features, a complex soft X-ray spectrum, and a collimated parsec-scale jet.

quotes.quote: In addition, apparent superluminal jet components were observed in SBS 0846 + 513 dammando13a , PMN J0948 + 0022 and 1H 0323 + 342 lister16 , suggesting high ( > 10) Lorentz factors and small ( < 10 ^ ) viewing angles.

Differently from FSRQ, a narrow Fe line at 6.4 keV has been reported for one source, 1H 0323 + 342 (Figure Xray , right panel), which has the softest X-ray spectrum of the ray-emitting NLSy1 kynoch18, dammando17b . The majority of ray-emitting NLSy1 for which good-quality X-ray spectra are available show an excess of emission at low energies with respect to the extrapolation of the hard X-ray spectral continuum model , making them different from typical blazars. As an example, the left panel of Figure Xray shows this excess below 2 keV in the X-ray spectrum of PMN J0948 + 0022. The 0.3--10 keV energy range spectra of ray-emitting NLSy1 are usually well fitted by a broken power-law with a break around 2 keV larsson18 In case of 1H 0323 + 342 a more complex X-ray spectrum was observed (e.g., kynoch18 ).

For 1H 0323 + 342, the nearest ray-emitting NLSy1, the morphology of the inner jet obtained by using high-resolution multifrequency VLBA observations is well characterized by a parabolic shape up to 7 mas, an indication that the jet is continously collimated near the base of the jet (Figure MOJAVE_VLBA , right panel). Beyond 7 milliarcsecond (mas) the jet expands more rapidly at larger scales, transitioning into a hyperbolic/conical shape hada18 . The acceleration and collimation zone seem to co-exist in this source, ending up with a bright quasi-stationary feature at 7 mas (a deprojected distance of 100 pc) corresponding to a recollimation shock doi18 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 1H 0323+342 as the nearest gamma-ray narrow-line Seyfert 1, finding soft excess, reflection features, and hard jet-related excess.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The closest of these exotic AGN is 1H 0323+342 ( : 03 24 41.16, : +34 10 45.8), with a redshift of z = 0.06 2007ApJ...658L..13Z .

quotes.quote: We present the results of X-ray spectral and timing analyses of the closest gamma-ray emitting narrow-line Seyfert 1 ( NLS1) galaxy, 1H 0323+342. We use observations from a recent, simultaneous XMM-Newton / NuSTAR campaign. As in radio-quiet NLS1s, the spectrum reveals a soft excess at low energies ( 2 keV) and reflection features such as a broad iron K emission line. We also find evidence of a hard excess at energies above 35 keV that is likely a consequence of jet emission.
The closest of these exotic AGN is 1H 0323+342 ( : 03 24 41.16, : +34 10 45.8), with a redshift of z = 0.06 2007ApJ...658L..13Z .

1H 0323+342 also has a double-hump SED characteristic of NLS1s that peaks in the radio band and gamma-rays, implying synchrotron emission and synchrotron self-Compton (or EC) mechanisms that would result from a jet.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1H 0323+342 as the nearest Fermi-detected NLSy1, hosted by a spiral or interacting late-type galaxy, with Fe K emission and optical spectra used for Fe II analysis.

quotes.quote: The ray detection supports the presence of a relativistic jet as the origin of the radio power for these sources komossaetal06 . They were selected because high-quality optical spectra were available for the three of them. The three ray sources are 1H 0323+342 ( B2 0321+33 1H 0323+342 J03246+341), 3C 286 ( J13311+305), and PKS 2004-447 ( J20079--445).

J03246 is the closest ray NLSy1 ( z = 0.063 ), hosted either by a spiral or an interacting late-type galaxy zhouetal06,antonetal08 , and it is the only known NLSy1 showing a Fe K emission line in X-rays kynochetal18 . This object was detected by the Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope soon after its launch abdoetal09 .

The spectra of these three sources were obtained using the Asiago 1.22m Telescope for J03246 (full description of the data reduction in foschinietal15 ), the SDSS archive for J13311, and the FORS2 instrument on the Very Large Telescope for J20079--445 bertonetal21 .

sources.summary: The paper studies J0324+3410 as a blazar host with ring-like morphology, blue nuclear and ring zones, and possible interaction- or merger-triggered star formation.

quotes.quote: We investigate the properties of the host galaxy of the blazar J0324+3410 (B2 0321+33) by the analysis of B and R images obtained with the NOT under good photometric conditions. The galaxy was studied using different methods: Sersic model fitting, unsharp-masked images, B-R image and B-R profile analysis. The images show that the host galaxy has a ring-like morphology. The B-R colour image reveals two bluish zones: one that coincides with the nuclear region, interpreted as the signature of emission related to the active nucleus, the other zone is extended and is located in the host ring-structure. We discuss the hypothesis that the later is thermal emission from a burst of star formation triggered by an interacting/merging process.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1H 0323 + 342 as the closest faint radio NLS1, with non-negligible radio flux, polarized optical emission, and host imaging suggesting a spiral arm or merger-related starburst ring.

quotes.quote: Among the faintest radio sources, 1H 0323 + 342 is the closest NLS1, so in this case the low radio luminosity is compensated by the proximity of the source. Indeed, its flux density is not negligible ( 600 mJy; see Table table:sample ).
Also the optical emission of 1H 0323 + 342 is polarized, but with a lower degree (0.7 0.8 ), although still in the range of other blazars ( In , the two NLS1s are erroneously classified as FSRQs. ).

Among the NLS1s, only one can be imaged with sufficient resolution to understand its host galaxy: it is 1H 0323 + 342 ( z=0.061 ), which was observed by the Hubble Space Telescope . These observations have shown a spiral arm , although other observations from the Nordic Optical Telescope (NOT) seem to suggest a circum-nuclear starburst ring, probably the trail of a recent merger .

sources.summary: 1H 0323 + 342 is a Fermi-detected radio-loud NLSy1 whose HST and Nordic Optical Telescope imaging show one-armed or circumnuclear-ring host morphology.

quotes.quote: Up to now 5 radio-loud NLSy1 galaxies have been detected at high significance by Fermi LAT: PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, 1H 0323 + 342, PKS 2004 447, and SBS 0846 + 513. Here we analyze the first four years of ray observations of these sources.

Among the ray emitting NLSy1s detected by Fermi LAT only for 1H 0323 + 342 Hubble Space Telescope (HST) and Nordic Optical Telescope observations are available. These observations revealed a one-armed galaxy morphology or a circumnuclear ring, suggesting two possibilities: the spiral arms of the host galaxy or the residual of a galaxy merging . On the other hand, no significant resolved structures have been observed instead by HST for SBS 0846 + 513 , and no high-resolution observations are available for the remaining ray NLSy1s.

sources.summary: The paper reports J0324+3410 as a gamma-ray detected RLNLS1 with hard-X-ray detection, host-galaxy subtraction, outburst variability, and intermittent hard-tail X-ray spectra.

quotes.quote: All the sources are point-like, and therefore we consider the emission from the host galaxy to be negligible; only J0324+3410 in the V filter displayed some hint of host galaxy, which was properly subtracted. We did not analysed the Burst Alert Telescope (BAT, Barthelmy et al. 2005) data because the average fluxes of RLNLS1s in hard X-rays are well below the instrument sensitivity. Indeed, by looking at the two available catalogs built on BAT data, we found only one detection of J0324+3410 in both the 70-month survey of the Swift /BAT team (Baumgartner et al. 2013) and the Palermo 54-month catalog (Cusumano et al. 2010). J0324+3410 was first detected by Foschini et al. (2009) by integrating all the available direct observations performed during the period 2006--2008 (total exposure 53 ks).

Some interesting episodes were described in the case of J0324+3410 by Foschini et al. (2009), Foschini (2013), and Tibolla et al. (2013): the source has generally a soft spectral index, typical of NLS1s, but sometimes --- as the jet became active --- the X-ray spectrum displays a break at a 2--3 keV and a hard tail appears (see also Paliya et al. 2014).
In the case of J0324+3410, with more data (Online Material Table 5), there is no evident trend to link the change in flux with a change of the spectral slope.

sources.summary: The paper measures jet kinematics and viewing angle for the radio-loud NLS1 galaxy 1H 0323+342, describing its variable multiwavelength emission, peculiar host morphology, and superluminal VLBI components.

quotes.quote: The focus of this paper is a viewing-angle measurement of the radio-loud NLS1 galaxy 1H 0323+342. This galaxy, at redshift z =0.0629, was recognized as a radio-loud NLS1 galaxy, sharing the properties of NLS1 galaxies and blazars by . Its host galaxy displays a peculiar morphology in form of a one-armed spiral 2007ApJ...658L..13Z or ring structure 2008A&A...490..583A, 2014ApJ...795...58L .

1H 0323+342 is highly variable at all wavebands including the radio 2014ApJ...781...75W, 2015A&A...575A..55A , optical 2014PASJ...66..108I , X-rays 2009AdSpR..43..889F, 2014ApJ...789..143P, 2015AJ....150...23Y and rays 2009ApJ...707L.142A, 2011MNRAS.413.2365C, 2013ATel.5344....1C, 2015AJ....149...41P .
Using different methods we ultimately constrain the viewing angle towards 1H 0323+342 to be in the range _ var 4^ - 13^ .

sources.summary: The paper discusses 1H 0323+342 as a gamma-ray NLSy1 with modeled broken-power-law X-ray indices, complex spectra, reported variability behavior, and gamma-ray luminosity comparable to FBQS J1644+2619.

quotes.quote: For 1H 0323+342, we show in Fig. bknpow_all the photon indices obtained by fitting a broken power law to a spectrum simulated based on the double power-law model fitted to the Suzaku data in .
