sources.summary: The paper presents multiwavelength monitoring of PMN J0948 + 0022, a gamma-ray narrow-line Seyfert 1 AGN whose powerful relativistic jet shows correlated variability and high gamma-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: We present more than three years of observations at different frequencies, from radio to high-energy rays, of the Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) Galaxy PMN J0948 + 0022 ( z=0.585 ). This source is the first NLS1 detected at energies above 100 MeV and therefore can be considered the prototype of this emerging new class of ray emitting active galactic nuclei (AGN). The observations performed from 2008 August 1 to 2011 December 31 confirmed that PMN J0948 + 0022 generates a powerful relativistic jet, able to develop an isotropic luminosity at rays of the order of 10^ 48  erg s ^ 1 , at the level of powerful quasars.

PMN J0948 + 0022 (also known as SDSS J094857.31 + 002225.4, z=0.585 0.001 Sloan Digital Sky Survey Team, SDSS Data Release 2, Nov. 18, 2004 from: http://www.sdss.org/dr2/products/spectra/getspectra.html ) is a very peculiar AGN, with an optical spectrum of a Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxy (Williams et al. 2002). Zhou et al. (2003) first reported the unexpected connection of its NLS1 character with strong and flat-spectrum radio emission. The optical spectrum of PMN J0948 + 0022 exhibits the usual characteristics of NLS1s (cf. Osterbrock Pogge 1985, Goodrich 1989): FWHM(H ) =1500 55  km/s, [ OIII ] /H <3 , strong optical FeII emission (Zhou et al. 2003).

sources.summary: The review describes SDSS J094857.3 + 002225 as a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 with blazar-like radio properties, relativistic-jet evidence, and the first explicit Fermi gamma-ray detection of the class.

quotes.quote: Specifically, they reported about the radio properties of three sources -- SDSS J094857.3 + 002225 ( z=0.585 ) , SDSS J084957.97 + 510829.0 ( z=0.584 ) , and 1H 0323 + 342 ( z=0.061 ) - that were very similar to blazars (flat or inverted radio spectrum, high brightness temperature), suggesting the presence of a relativistic jet viewed at small angles. Interestingly, all these three sources were later detected at high-energy rays . These works triggered deeper studies at radio frequencies of some RLNLS1s , where the typical radio properties of relativistic jets were confirmed, and, in addition, the radio morphology resulted to be very compact on parsec scale.

Indeed, the first RLNLS1 to be detected at MeV-GeV energies -- SDSS J094857.3 + 002225 (alias PMN J0948 + 0022) - was also present among the list of bright sources detected after the first three months of Fermi activity . However, it was still identified as a FSRQ, although the text refers to another specific paper in preparation: ``The source PMN J0948 + 0022, associated with 0FGL J0948.3+0019, has a flat radio spectrum but shows an optical spectrum with only narrow emission lines, leading to an `uncertain' type classification in Roma-BZCat. A detailed analysis of this source is presented in Abdo et al. (2009a)'' [ the latter being the reference in the present work ] . It was indeed in that it was reported the first high-energy ray detection of a RLNLS1 in an explicit and ``conscious'' way.

sources.summary: The paper models PMN J0948+0022 as a gamma-ray narrow-line Seyfert 1 with blazar-like jet emission, EC-dominated SEDs, and GeV spectral variability tied to jet parameters.

quotes.quote: PMN J0948+0022 (z = 0.5846) is the first NLS1 detected in GeV band by Fermi/LAT. The inverted spectrum in radio band indicates the presence of a relativistic jet viewed at small angles (Zhou et al. 2003), which is similar to the properties of blazars. The significant variabilities of PMN J0948+0022 are detected, especially in GeV band (Abdo et al. 2009; Foschini et al. 2012).

The SEDs of PMN J0948+0022 are well explained with the syn+SSC+EC model, as shown in Figure 1(a). The EC component of the SEDs for PMN J0948+0022 presents a further constraint on , and thus makes a tighter constraint on and B than that for BL Lacs in Zhang et al. (2012). The broadband SEDs of PMN J0948+0022 are dominated by the EC process, and there are significant variabilities in GeV band.

sources.summary: The paper uses PMN J0948+0022 as a comparison radio-loud NLS1, noting similar kpc-scale radio emission, detected polarization, different spectral-index behavior, and bluer-when-brighter variability after a Fermi flare.

quotes.quote: The famous Fermi LAT-detected NLS1, PMN J0948+0022 abdo2009 , a low _ peak radio/ loud NLS1 at z=0.585 , shares observational properties similar to J1100+4421 including kpc-scale radio emission doi2012 , but lacks the high [ O _  III ] /H flux ratio of J1100+4421. If J1100+4421 follows the general distributions observed for low _ peak blazars in ikejiri2011 , a high degree of polarization might be expected for J1100+4421 as was indeed detected from PMN J0948+0022 itoh2013 . PMN J0948+0022 shows a different behavior in its power-law indices than J1100+4421. The extinction-corrected ( A_V=0.263  mag; ; assuming the extinction law given by ) SDSS spectrum of PMN J0948+0022 gives a power-law index _ =-1.77 0.07 .

On the other hand, itoh2013 measured an optical power-law index of 0< _ <1 during a brighter phase of PMN J0948+0022 after the detection of a ray flare with Fermi LAT. They indicated that the index varies at different brightness phases, which is a well known behavior known as the ``bluer-when-brighter'' trend for blazars (e.g., ) and quasars (e.g., ); we note that J1100+4421 does not show such a change in its power-law indices.

sources.summary: The review describes PMN J0948 + 0022 as a gamma-ray NLSy1 with large flares, moderate spectral evolution, and X-ray spectra modeled with reflection or Comptonisation plus jet emission.

quotes.quote: Some of the ray-emitting NLSy1 do exhibit large-amplitude ray flares: SBS 0846 + 513 dammando12,dammando13a (see Figure LATflare , left panel); PMN J0948 + 0022 dammando15b,foschini11b (see Figure LATflare2 , left panel), 1H 0323 + 342 paliya14 , PKS 1502 + 036 dammando16b , PKS 2004 447 gokus19 , with an apparent isotropic ray luminosity of 10 ^ 48 erg s ^ 1 , comparable to that of bright FSRQ. This is another indication that NLSy1 are able to host relativistic jets as powerful as those in FSRQ. Variability was observed during all ray flares on a daily time-scale, and a subdaily time-scale in case of PKS 1502 + 036 dammando16b,paliya16a (see Figure LATflare , right panel). The ray flaring activity of SBS 0846 + 513 and PMN J0948 + 0022 has been associated with a moderate spectral evolution, a behaviour observed also in bright FSRQ and low-synchrotron-peaked BL Lacs (e.g., abdo10b ).
As an example, the left panel of Figure Xray shows this excess below 2 keV in the X-ray spectrum of PMN J0948 + 0022.

In agreement with this, the analysis of the spectra of PMN J0948 + 0022 dammando14 , FBQS J1644 + 2619 larsson18 , and 1H 0323 + 342 kynoch18 can be well described by models that include reflection and/or Comptonisation in addition to the jet emission. PMN J0948 + 0022 and 1H 0323 + 342 have shown breaks in the root-mean square (RMS)-variability around the spectral breaks, lending further support to a scenario where different components dominate at low and high energies dammando14, kynoch18 .

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBA observations of SDSS J094857.3+002225, finding compact, variable emission requiring Doppler boosting and arguing that its accretion disk resembles a very high state.

quotes.quote: We observed the narrow-line quasar SDSS J094857.3+002225, which has the highest known radio loudness for a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy (NLS1), at 1.7--15.4 GHz with the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA). This is the first very-long-baseline interferometry (VLBI) investigation for a radio-loud NLS1. We independently found very high brightness temperatures from (1) its compactness in a VLBA image and (2) flux variation among the VLBA observation, our other observations with the VLBA, and the Very Large Array (VLA). A Doppler factor larger than 2.7--5.5 was required to meet an intrinsic limit of brightness temperature in the rest frame. This is evidence for highly relativistic nonthermal jets in an NLS1.

The accretion disk of SDSS J094857.3+002225 is probably in the very high state, rather than the high/soft state, by analogy with X-ray binaries with strong radio outbursts and superluminal jets such as GRS 1915+105.

sources.summary: The paper reports a multiwavelength campaign on the gamma-ray NLS1 PMN J0948 + 0022 during its 2010 outburst, emphasizing its powerful relativistic jet and blazar-like luminosity.

quotes.quote: We report about the multiwavelength campaign on the Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) Galaxy PMN J0948 + 0022 ( z = 0.5846 ) performed in 2010 July-September and triggered by high activity as measured by Fermi /LAT. The peak luminosity in the 0.1-100 GeV energy band exceeded, for the first time in this type of source, the value of 10^ 48 erg/s, a level comparable to the most powerful blazars. The comparison of the spectral energy distribution of the NLS1 PMN J0948 + 0022 with that of a typical blazar -- like 3C 273 -- shows that the power emitted at gamma rays is extreme.

The observed coordinated broad-band variability confirmed that: (a) the ray source discovered by Fermi /LAT is associated with the NLS1 PMN J0948 + 0022; (b) the continuum of the NLS1 is dominated by the emission of a powerful relativistic jet viewed at small angles. Again in 2010 July, PMN J0948 + 0022 exploded in an intense outburst at rays, reaching a peak luminosity of 10^ 48 erg/s .

sources.summary: PMN J0948+0022 is used as a GeV-flaring NLSy1 comparison object whose SED is more powerful than PKS 1502+036, with a more luminous accretion disk and stronger high-energy output.

quotes.quote: With this in mind, we generate SEDs of two other NLSy1 galaxies covering their GeV outbursts, namely, 1H 0323+342 2014ApJ...789..143P and PMN J0948+0022 2015MNRAS.446.2456D .

A comparison of the SED of PKS 1502+036 with PMN J0948+0022 reveals that the latter one is more powerful at all the energies. The accretion disk luminosity of PMN J0948+0022 is derived as 9 10 ^ 45   2012A&A...548A.106F and 1.18 10 ^ 46   2015A&A...575A..13F based on the accretion disk model fitting and optical spectroscopic approach, respectively. This suggests the accretion disk of PMN J0948+0022 to be more luminous than PKS 1502+036 and can be clearly seen in the optical-UV band. The higher luminosity of the former at X-ray and ray energies could be due to stronger boosting and larger power of injected electrons 2015MNRAS.446.2456D .

sources.summary: The review describes PMN J0948+0022 as a radio-loud NLSy1 with relativistically beamed radio emission, LAT gamma-ray detection, and measured superluminal motion indicating beamed jet emission.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, studies focused on individual NLSy1 sources reported a few peculiar ones, e.g., PMN J0948+0022 ( z=0.59 ) shows radio flux variations, very high radio brightness temperatures ( >10^ 13 K, Zhou et al. 2003), indicating relativistically beamed emission (see also Wang et al. 2006).
Within the first six months of LAT operation, a ray source positionally consistent with the radio-loud NLSy1 galaxy PMN J0948+0022 was found (Abdo et al. 2009b).

Superluminal motion has been observed from three NLSy1 galaxies (Figure fig:kinematics ): 1H 0323+342 ( _ app, =9.1 0.3 , Lister et al. 2016), SBS 0846+513 ( _ app, =6.6 0.8 , Lister et al. 2019), PMN J0948+0022 ( _ app, =9.1 0.3 , Lister et al. 2019). The bulk Lorentz factor ( >v_ app / ) and the jet viewing angle ( _ v <2 (/v_ app ) ) can be constrained from the observed superluminal motion. The derived parameters suggest the presence of beamed jet emission in NLSy1 galaxies (see also Lister 2018, for a recent review).

sources.summary: The paper highlights PMN J0948+0022 as showing spectacular rapid variability, symmetric rise and decay, and lower UV variability from accretion-disc dilution of synchrotron emission.

quotes.quote: PMN J0948 + 0022 : a spectacular variability has been observed on 2009 June 23 (see Fig. 0948_event1 ) with an increase from 1.1 to 0.4 mag going from v to w2 filter in 1.6 h (corresponding to 1 h in rest frame, see Table rapid2 ) and a decrease at the initial level in a comparable time (see also Fig. 0948_SED , centre panel). The approximate symmetry of the event, with a similar rising and decaying time, suggests that the relevant time-scale in this case can be the light crossing time of the emitting region e.g. chiaberge99 . A second peak with a smaller amplitude and at lower significance has been observed a few hours later. A similar rapid variability in optical bands has been reported for PMN J0948 + 0022 in liu10 and eggen13 . The lower variability amplitude observed at higher frequencies implies that the synchrotron emission is more contaminated by thermal emission from accretion disc at higher frequency, as expected by the SED modelling of the source see e.g. dammando15b .

sources.summary: The paper reports a 2010 multiwavelength campaign on PMN J0948 + 0022 during an extreme gamma-ray outburst, emphasizing its NLS1 classification, relativistic jet, and blazar-like power.

quotes.quote: We report on a multiwavelength campaign on the radio-loud Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) Galaxy PMN J0948 + 0022 ( z = 0.5846 ) performed in 2010 July-September and triggered by a high-energy ray outburst observed by the Large Area Telescope (LAT) onboard the Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope . The peak flux in the 0.1 100 GeV energy band exceeded, for the first time in this type of source, the value of 10^ 6 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , corresponding to an observed luminosity of 10^ 48 erg s ^ 1 . Although the source was too close to the Sun position to organize a densely sampled follow-up, it was possible to gather some multiwavelength data that confirmed the state of high activity across the sampled electromagnetic spectrum. The comparison of the spectral energy distribution of the NLS1 PMN J0948 + 0022 with that of a typical blazar -- like 3C 273 -- shows that the power emitted at rays is extreme.

sources.summary: The paper presents PMN J0948 + 0022 as a gamma-ray RL-NLS1 with blazar-like SED parameters and July 2010 flares showing relativistic jets as powerful as those in blazars.

quotes.quote: For example, the physical parameters resulting from the modeling of the average SED of PMN J0948 + 0022 during the multifrequency campaign in August 2008-January 2009 (see ) are typical of a source midway between FSRQs and BL Lacs, with low power with respect to FSRQs (see Fig. 0948_SED ).

A first answer arrived in July 2010 when PMN J0948+0022 underwent two strong ray flares with peak flux of 100 10 ^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , corresponding to an apparent isotropic ray luminosity of 10 ^ 48 erg s ^ 1 , comparable to that of the bright FSRQs . The extreme power of these flaring episodes confirms that this RL-NLS1 hosts relativistic jets as powerful as those in blazars, despite the relatively low mass and the rich environment due to the high accretion rate. The continuous monitoring of the entire ray sky provided by Fermi LAT allows us to catch, if it happens, a new intense ray flare from one of these sources. It is important to know if also other RL-NLS1s can produce similar ray flares to determine if PMN J0948 + 0022 is an archetypical source of this new class of ray AGNs or if it shows peculiar characteristics also with respect to the other RL-NLS1s.

sources.summary: The paper describes PMN J0948 + 0022 as the first gamma-ray detected NLS1, with blazar-like multiwavelength behavior, a 2009 flux peak, and a stronger 2010 high-energy outburst.

quotes.quote: Already after a few months of operation, there was the first detection at rays ( E>100 MeV) of a NLS1: it was PMN J0948 + 0022 ( z=0.5846 ) . This discovery immediately triggered a multiwavelength (MW) campaign (2009 March-July), which confirmed that the ray emission was indeed associated with the NLS1 and that the MW behavior was typical of a relativistic jet, like those in blazars . The NLS1 had a peak in the ray flux on April 1, 2009, with a value of 4 10^ 7 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and then declined, followed by a similar trend at all the wavelengths, while radio emission increased reaching the peak about a couple of months later. In 2010 July, PMN J0948 + 0022 underwent a much stronger outburst, reaching the power of 10^ 48 erg/s in the 0.1-100 GeV band and this event was preceded by a significant change in the radio polarization angle, which occurred about one year before .

sources.summary: The paper discusses PMN J0948 + 0022 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 with thermal disk emission, complex radio/gamma behavior, and a VERITAS upper limit after a Fermi-LAT flare.

quotes.quote: Contrary to what is observed in PMN J0948 + 0022 , no significant evidence of thermal emission from the accretion disc has been observed in SBS 0846 + 513. A complex connection between the radio and ray emission was observed for SBS 0846 + 513 and PMN J0948 + 0022 (Fig. fig3 , right panel), as discussed in detail e.g. in . At Very High Energy (VHE; E > 100 GeV), VERITAS observations of PMN J0948 + 0022 were carried out during 2013 January 6--13, after the ray flare observed by Fermi LAT on 2013 January 1. These observations resulted in an upper limit of F _ > 0.2 TeV < 4 10 ^ 12 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses PMN J0948+0022 as the prototype gamma-ray NLSy1 comparison, noting its higher luminosity and redshift than FBQS J1644+2619, similar radio-to-X-ray flux, and much stronger gamma-ray Compton dominance.

quotes.quote: For comparison, we also show in Fig. sed the SED of the prototype ray NLSy1 PMN J0948+0022, as observed during an intermediate flux state in 2011 D'Ammando2015a . While PMN J0948+0022 has a larger luminosity than FBQS J1644+2619, it also has a higher redshift, and the flux level of the two sources turn out to be similar in the radio -- X-ray range. The main difference between the SEDs is the much larger contribution at ray energies for PMN J0948+0022, revealing a higher Compton dominance in this source. While the average ray and X-ray luminosities are similar in FBQS J1644+2619, the luminosities differ by almost two orders of magnitude in PMN J0948+0022.

sources.summary: The paper discusses J0948 + 0022 as the Fermi-detected NLSy1 PMN J0948+0022, monitored at radio to millimeter bands and showing rapid, hard-spectrum variability.

quotes.quote: In 2009 the LAT instrument onboard the Fermi GST satellite detected high-energy rays ( E>100 MeV) from the Narrow Line Seyfert 1 galaxy (NLSy1) PMN J0948+0022 with a blazar-like SED 2009ApJ...699..976A .
J0948 + 0022 show frequent events of activity which happen considerably fast.

In the case of J0948 + 0022 the source shows an impressive spectral evolution and remains at the hard-spectrum state (i.e. 0.0 with S ^ ) 4 of the time and its spectral index can reach values of +1.0 ; an indication of high frequency components dominating the observed emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes PMN J0948+0022 as a gamma-ray-bright radio-loud NLSy1 whose optical variability suggests competing jet synchrotron and disk-emission contributions, with optical flux uncorrelated with polarization degree.

quotes.quote: The most gamma-ray bright NLSy1 PMN J0948+0022 showed minute-scale optical variability, correlated with polarization degree . This indicates that synchrotron emision from the jet is dominant in the optical band, but other study shows disk emission is also dominant in the optical band .
In PMN J0948+0022, optical flux and PD are not correlated and maximum PD reaches more than 10 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PMN J0948 + 0022 as a gamma-ray NLSy1 with VHE follow-up, detailed XMM-Newton X-ray spectra, and modeled 2013 flaring-state SEDs.

quotes.quote: At Very High Energy (VHE; E > 100 GeV), VERITAS observations of PMN J0948 + 0022 were carried out a few days after the ray flare observed by Fermi LAT on 1 January 2013 (Figure LAT , right panel).
The high quality of the XMM-Newton observation of PMN J0948 + 0022 performed in 28--29 May 2011 allowed us to study in detail its X-ray spectrum, as reported in .
For PMN J0948 + 0022 we compared the broad-band SED of the 2013 flaring activity state with that from an intermediate activity state observed in 2011.

gmw_00000340.name: Mrk 178
gmw_00000340.overview: Mrk 178 is a metal-poor starburst dwarf galaxy located in the Canes Venatici group [arXiv:0805.1073, arXiv:1410.2635]. It features a highly asymmetric gas distribution confined to its northern half [arXiv:1409.1239, arXiv:1404.6252] and hosts a major star-forming region to the south [arXiv:0805.1073].

sources.summary: The review describes Mrk178 as the closest metal-poor Wolf-Rayet galaxy, resolving WR knots, finding spatial ISM temperature variations, homogeneous nebular oxygen abundance, and a metallicity of 12+log(O/H)=7.72.

quotes.quote: In K13 we present the first optical IFS study of Mrk 178 based on IFU data obtained with the INTEGRAL IFU at the 4.2m WHT, Roque de los Muchachos observatory (see Fig. fov ). The proximity of Mrk 178 (distance 3.9 Mpc) combined with the IFS technique allow us to locate and resolve SF knots hosting a few WRs, and also to characterize the WR content. In addition, we are able to probe the spatial correlation between massive stars and the properties of the surrounding ISM.

The WR knot spectra reveal the presence of nitrogen-type and carbon-type WR stars in Mrk 178 (see Fig. mrk178.fig1 ). By comparing the observed spectra of the WR knots with SMC/LMC template WRs, we empirically estimate a lower limit for the number of WRs ( 20) in our Mrk178 FOV that is already higher than that currently found in the literature ( 2-3 WRs from Guseva et al. 2000). Regarding the ISM properties, our statistical analysis suggests that spatial variations in the gaseous T _ e [ OIII ] exist and that the scatter in T _ e [ OIII ] can be larger than that in O/H within the observed FOV. Thus, caution should be exercised when analysing integrated spectra of SF systems which do not necessarily represent the ``local'' ISM properties around massive star clusters. The nebular chemical abundance in Mrk 178 is homogeneous over spatial scales of hundreds of parsecs. The representative metallicity of Mrk 178 derived is 12+log(O/H) = 7.72 0.01 (error-weighted mean value of O/H and its corresponding statistical error).

sources.summary: The paper presents IFS results for Mrk178, detecting WR bumps across the field, estimating at least 20 WR stars, and finding extended HeII emission with an unknown excitation source.

quotes.quote: In K13 we present the first optical IFS study of Mrk178, the closest metal-poor WR HII galaxy. IFS data of Mrk 178 were obtained with the INTEGRAL IFU at the 4.2m WHT. In this work we examine the spatial correlation between its WRs and the neighbouring ionized ISM. The strength of the broad WR features and its low metallicity ( 1/10 Z _ ) make Mrk178 an intriguing object.

We have detected the blue and red WR bumps in different locations across the FOV ( 300 pc 230 pc) in Mrk178 (fig. kehrig.mrk178.fig1 ). The study of the WR content has been extended, for the first time, beyond its brightest SF knot (knot B in fig. kehrig.mrk178.fig1 ) uncovering new WR star clusters (knots A and C in fig. kehrig.mrk178.fig1 ). Using SMC/LMC template WRs crowther06 , we empirically estimate a minimum of 20 WRs in our Mrk178 FOV, which is already higher than that currently found in the literature.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 178 as a low-metallicity starburst dwarf with unusually strong Wolf-Rayet features, broad bumps, and a southern star-forming region sampled by SDSS.

quotes.quote: Mrk 178 is a starbursting dwarf galaxy in the Canes Venatici group and we adopt a distance of 3.89 Mpc for this object based on the tip of the Red Giant Branch distance from 2003A&A...398..467K . The oxygen abundance of 12 + log O/H = 7.73 0.08 and the equivalent width of 15.4 0.4 \ are in excellent agreement with Guseva et al (2000) who found 7.82 0.06 and 15.92 0.5 \ respectively from an independent observation of the galaxy. Mrk 178 has one major star forming region to its south where the SDSS spectrum, shown in the top row of Figure fig:mrk178 , is also obtained. The prominence of the WR features is obvious and as the bottom row of the figure shows, these are very broad, featureless bumps.

The strength of the red bump shows clearly that the main source is WC stars as pointed out by Guseva et al as well. The immediate impression from Figure fig:mrk178 as compared to Figure fig:spectrum_samples is the prominence of the WR features relative to the nebular lines.

sources.summary: The paper highlights IFU results for the metal-poor WR HII galaxy Mrk 178, emphasizing extended nebular HeII emission, uncertain ionization, and aperture effects on WR-bump measurements.

quotes.quote: Here, we highlight some results from the first 3D spectroscopic study of Mrk 178, the closest metal-poor WR HII galaxy , focusing on the origin of the nebular HeII emission and the aperture effects on the detection of WR features.

We summarize here some recent results on Mrk178, one of the most metal-poor nearby WR galaxies see for more details K13 : 1) The origin of high-ionization nebular lines (e.g. HeII 4686), apparently more frequent in high-z galaxies, is still an open question. One widely favored mechanism for He ^ + ionization involves hot WRs, but it has been shown that nebular HeII 4686 is not always accompanied by WR signatures, thus WRs do not explain He ^ + ionization at all times K08,K11,SB12 . In Mrk178, we find nebular HeII 4686 emission spatially extended reaching well beyond the location of the WR stars (Fig. fig , left-panel). The excitation source of He ^ + in Mrk178 is still unknown. 2) From the SDSS spectra, we have found a too high EW(WR bump) value for Mrk178, which is the most deviant point among the metal-poor WR galaxies in Fig. fig , right-panel.

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 6541 as a heavily disturbed dwarf with strongly asymmetric H I confined to the northern half and a position on the CVn I cloud edge.

quotes.quote: Several galaxies have heavily disturbed distributions, characterized by large-scale asymmetries, long filaments, and/or a large optical- offset (NGC 4449, I Zw 18, NGC 1705, UGC 6541, NGC 1569, and I Zw 36).

UGC 6541 has a strongly asymmetric distribution. Gas emission is detected only in the Northern half of the galaxy. UGC 6541 is located to the North-Western edge of the CVn I cloud Karachentsev2003 . Another BCD (NGC 3741, Karachentsev2013 ) lies at a projected distance of 300 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 6541 as a small galaxy with central light excess, uncertain star-forming or foreground nature, and a steep blue-centered color gradient.

quotes.quote: On individual galaxies : UGC 6541 : small galaxy, with a light excess close to the center as compared to an exponential profile. As can be seen on the image, this can be a star-forming region or a superimposed star. The color profile shows no deviation in the region concerned by the bright object, so its nature is difficult to assess. The color gradient of this galaxy is steep, and in the sense of the center being bluer.

sources.summary: The study classifies UGC 6541 as an unsettled starburst dwarf with asymmetric gas detected only north of the galaxy, possibly from a disrupted disk.

quotes.quote: UGC 6541 has a very asymmetric distribution. Gas emission is detected only in the northern half of the galaxy. This may be the remnant of a disrupted disk.

sources.summary: The paper discusses UGC 6541 as a CVn I galaxy observed with WFPC2, describing its stellar population or morphology and deriving a TRGB-based distance.

quotes.quote: A blue compact galaxy from Markarian's lists is located on the NW edge of the CVn I cloud. It was resolved into stars for the first time by Georgiev et al.(1997), who derived a distance of 3.5 0.7 Mpc via the brightest blue stars. The distance to UGC 6541 from the luminosity of TRGB is D = 3.89 0.47 Mpc, which is in reasonable agreement with the previous estimate.

gmw_00000341.name: SDSS J1047+0739
gmw_00000341.overview: SDSS J1047+0739 is a metal-poor dwarf emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2011.10053]. It exhibits low-level variability and persistent broad Balmer emission, which supports the presence of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2011.10053].

sources.summary: The paper includes SDSS J1047+0739 among metal-poor dwarf emission-line galaxies with persistent broad Balmer emission, Gemini follow-up spectra, low-level variability, and evidence favoring an AGN interpretation.

quotes.quote: We obtained new Gemini long-slit spectroscopy in March -- May 2020 using the GMOS instrument on Gemini-North (GN-2020A-FT-204; PI Burke) for three of the four IT08 galaxies: SDSS J1047+0739, SDSS J1222+3602, and SDSS J1025+1402. We targeted the H line with the R831 grating and a 1 ^ slit width.

Our flux-calibrated Gemini spectra of SDSS J1047 and SDSS J1222 showed variations in the narrow-line fluxes relative to the SDSS spectra due to seeing and aperture variations compared to the 3 ^ SDSS fibre. To mitigate aperture effects and allow for a fair comparison of the broad Balmer luminosities, we normalised the Gemini spectrum to the SDSS flux-levels using the peak narrow H flux levels.

Low-levels of variability are detected in all IT08 galaxies with the possible exception of SDSS J0045. The variability in these three galaxies show red noise, as expected in the commonly-used stochastic DRW model of AGN variability Kelly2009,MacLeod2010 . This detection of variability in these three galaxies supports the AGN scenario and likely indicates the AGNs are not strongly obscured.

gmw_00000342.name: SDSS J1222+3602
gmw_00000342.overview: SDSS J1222+3602 is a metal-poor dwarf emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2011.10053]. Gemini long-slit spectroscopy of the galaxy shows persistent broad Balmer emission with no evidence of strong variability [arXiv:2011.10053].

sources.summary: The paper includes SDSS J1222+3602 among metal-poor dwarf emission-line galaxies with Gemini spectra, persistent broad Balmer emission, low-level variability, and broad-line decomposition uncertainties.

quotes.quote: We obtained new Gemini long-slit spectroscopy in March -- May 2020 using the GMOS instrument on Gemini-North (GN-2020A-FT-204; PI Burke) for three of the four IT08 galaxies: SDSS J1047+0739, SDSS J1222+3602, and SDSS J1025+1402. We targeted the H line with the R831 grating and a 1 ^ slit width.

Our flux-calibrated Gemini spectra of SDSS J1047 and SDSS J1222 showed variations in the narrow-line fluxes relative to the SDSS spectra due to seeing and aperture variations compared to the 3 ^ SDSS fibre. To mitigate aperture effects and allow for a fair comparison of the broad Balmer luminosities, we normalised the Gemini spectrum to the SDSS flux-levels using the peak narrow H flux levels.

The large uncertainties in the broad-line width in the SDSS epoch of J1222 reflect the ambiguity of such decomposition. Given the additional systematic uncertainties due to aperture effects, instrumental resolution, and photometric conditions, we find no evidence for strong variability of the broad Balmer emission in the IT08 sample.

gmw_00000343.name: SDSS J1025+1402
gmw_00000343.overview: SDSS J1025+1402 is a red, symmetric galaxy classified as a broad-line quasar [arXiv:1307.4899]. It is identified as a Balmer-absorption active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2011.10053] that hosts a blueshifted outflow [arXiv:2011.10053] and exhibits star-forming line ratios [arXiv:1307.4899].

sources.summary: The paper includes SDSS J1025+1402 in Gemini follow-up and identifies it as a Balmer-absorption AGN with a real blueshifted H absorption line indicating an outflow.

quotes.quote: We obtained new Gemini long-slit spectroscopy in March -- May 2020 using the GMOS instrument on Gemini-North (GN-2020A-FT-204; PI Burke) for three of the four IT08 galaxies: SDSS J1047+0739, SDSS J1222+3602, and SDSS J1025+1402. We targeted the H line with the R831 grating and a 1 ^ slit width.

SDSS J1025: Balmer Absorption Line We identify a 119 46 km s ^ 1 blueshifted (relative to the broad H ) narrow absorption line in the spectrum of SDSS J1025. This line is real, and not due to telluric absorption. It is present at lower spectral resolution in the SDSS spectrum at the same blueshift.

We identify SDSS J1025 as a new Balmer-absorption AGN. According to Wang2015 , only eight are reported in the literature (including their AGN). We interpret this as evidence for an AGN-driven outflow.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSSJ1025+1402 as a red symmetric XMP with large line offsets, star-forming BPT ratios, and possible AGN activity inferred from its red color and broad H line.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ1025+1402 - This red symmetric (Table 3) source shows Balmer and forbidden line offsets in excess of 60 km s ^ 1 (Table 4). The neighbouring sources in the SDSS field show typically lower redshifts, which discards possible H i contamination, and the nearest radio continuum source (NVSS) lies 5 away. The SDSS classifies this source as a broad-line quasar at a redshift of 0.1004 (Table 4). The [ N ii ] 6584 /H and [ O iii ] 5007 /H ratios put this source in the realm of star-forming galaxies in the BPT diagram. However, the red color, broad H line, large redshift and large instrinsic luminosity indicate that the source may host an Active Galactic Nuclei (AGN; Izotov, Thuan Guseva 2007).

gmw_00000344.name: LRG J0239 0134
gmw_00000344.overview: LRG J0239 0134 is a highly distorted, ring-like galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.062 [arXiv:2202.04663, arXiv:astro-ph/0011096]. While initially interpreted as a ring galaxy, its morphology may instead be driven by a pair of superbubbles from an intense central starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0011096].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GGL-A as a previously known distorted ring-like galaxy in the A370 core and reports its source redshift from the MUSE follow-up.

quotes.quote: Nevertheless, there are two previously-identified GGLs contained within the cluster multiple-image region. The first (GGL-A) is a singly-imaged but highly distorted ring-like galaxy at z = 1.062 , first identified by sou99 .

Mirroring an ongoing galaxy-galaxy lens (GGL) search in the BUFFALO fields, we perform a follow-up search in the spectroscopic data. Of the five identified GGL candidates in the A370 HST imaging, four are at least partially contained within the MUSE footprint. We easily identify lens redshifts for all four candidates (all confirmed cluster members) and source redshifts for two: GGL-A ( z_s = 1.062) and GGL-B ( z_s = 1.032); see Fig.

sources.summary: The paper argues LRG J0239 0134 may be a high-redshift starburst with superbubbles rather than a standard collision-made ring galaxy.

quotes.quote: The unusual morphology of LRG J0239 0134 at z =1.062 has been interpreted as a ring galaxy. Here we propose an alternative idea that the ring-like morphology is attributed to a pair of superbubbles driven by the intense starburst in the central region of this galaxy. Supporting evidence for a superbubble model is; 1) the poststarburst nature in the central body suggests that a burst of supernova explosions could occur at least 10^7 yr ago, 2) the dark lane seen in the central body suggests that we observe this object from a nearly edge-on view, and, 3) the ring-like morphology is not inconsistent with an idea that it is a pair of superbubbles.
However, if this is the case, this galaxy provides an important example of the superwind activity at high redshift.

gmw_00000345.name: Antennae galaxies
gmw_00000345.overview: The Antennae galaxies, also designated NGC 4038/39, are a nearby gas-rich spiral-spiral merger [arXiv:0809.5020, arXiv:1312.2522] featuring extended tidal tails [arXiv:0809.5020, arXiv:1305.5865] and strong, ordered magnetic fields [arXiv:1201.4051, arXiv:2211.00012]. This interacting system hosts a major off-nuclear starburst [arXiv:1305.0828, arXiv:1709.00010] concentrated in its dusty disk-overlap region [arXiv:2202.07368, arXiv:2401.04835].

sources.summary: The paper models the Antennae as a nearby spiral-spiral merger with tidal tails, comparing high-resolution SPH simulations against observed morphology and H I kinematics.

quotes.quote: The nearby interacting 'Antennae' galaxy pair (NGC 4038/39) is one of the best-studied local systems of merging spirals, thus presenting us with an ideal laboratory for the study of galaxy evolution models. The Antennae are believed to be in a state prior to their final encounter with rapid subsequent merging, which puts them in the first position of the Toomre (1977) merger sequence. Here we present first numerical high-resolution, self-consistent, smoothed particle hydrodynamics (SPH) simulations of the Antennae system, including star formation and stellar feedback, and compare our results to VLA HI observations by Hibbard et al.

The 'Antennae' galaxies (NGC 4038/39, Arp 244, VV245) are a well-known, archetypal example of a spiral-spiral galaxy merger (see Fig. FigHibbHIoptical ). They are nicknamed after their spectacular appearance with a prominent pair of tidal tails, which was already noted in early optical images (e.g. Duncan 1923, Schweizer 1978). At present there is a huge amount of data collected for the Antennae both from ground- and space-based telescopes, covering a wide range of wavelength regimes.

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 244 as the nearest prototypal gas-rich major merger, emphasizing interaction-triggered star formation, overlap-region starbursts, and sequential star formation across the colliding disks.

quotes.quote: , the Antennae (NGC 4038/39, Arp 244) is the nearest prototypal major merger between two gas-rich spiral galaxies ( Toomre1972 ; Hibbard2001 ). Thus it provides us with a unique opportunity to study the induced star formation process as a consquence of interaction in detail. It has been extensively studied at essentially all wavelengths from X-ray to radio ( Hummel1986 ; Read1995 ; Vigroux1996 ; Mirabel1998 ; Nikola1998 ; Whitmore1999 ; Neff2000 ; Wilson2000 ; fabbiano2001 ; Gao2001 ; Hibbard2001 ; fabbiano2003 ; fabbiano2004 ; Wang2004 ; Hibbard2005 ; Mengel2005 ; bastian2006 ; Gilbert2007 ; Schulz2007 ; brandl2009 ).

ISO mid-infrared (MIR) observations Mirabel1998 show that the most intense starburst in this system takes place in the so-called overlap region between the two nuclei, which indicates its intermediate merging stage, almost totally obscured in the optical. Considering the abundant molecular gas, wide spread star formation and overall modest star formation efficiency, Gao et al. (2001) argued that Arp 244 has the potential of producing an ultraluminous extreme starburst in a later stage of merging. In the Antennae, observations with have identified thousands of super star clusters (SSCs) Whitmore1999 possibly being formed as part of the merging process.

Based upon this model, across the overlap regions of Arp 244, the sequential star formation paths we have found can be explained naturally. As the two colliding disks begin to interpenetrate each other, the regions that overlap first, e.g. region 6, may have more of the gravitationally unstable GMCs layers formed first, leading to in situ starburst first there.

sources.summary: The paper studies the Antennae merger overlap region, arguing that galaxy-interaction-driven convergent flows compress gas, power shock-excited H2 emission, and help form SGMC 2 and a pre-cluster cloud.

quotes.quote: Detailed observations of the nearby Antennae galaxy merger NGC4038/4039 at a distance of only 22 Mpc schweizer08 provide important benchmarks for our understanding of how the large-scale dynamics of galaxy interactions trigger star formation on much smaller scales. Contrary to the 'canonical' scenario of merger-induced star formation, which predicts intense starbursts in the nuclear regions e.g., barnes96 , most stars in the Antennae are formed off-nucleus, in a heavily obscured, gas-rich, infrared-luminous region where both galaxies permeate each other, the ``overlap region'' e.g., vigroux96,klaas10 . Most of the star formation in the overlap region of 20 yr ^ 1 zhang01 occurs in massive super-star clusters (SSCs) with masses larger than 10^4 and up to a few 10^6 \ whitmore95,zhang99,whitmore10 .

Thus, we consider that the velocity gradient observed with the H _2 line emission across the SINFONI field is tracing a convergent flow driven by the galaxy interaction. The associated gas compression has created the conditions for the gas to cool and to become molecular, like in numerical simulations of convergent flows hennebelle08 . As observed in the galaxy collision in Stefan's Quintet, the mechanical energy of the interaction is not fully thermalized in large scale shocks guillard09 . Due to the inhomogeneous, multi-phase, nature of the interstellar medium much of the gas kinetic energy decays from large to small scales before it is dissipated. Thus, we consider that it is the galaxy interaction that drives the molecular gas turbulence.

sources.summary: The paper studies the Antennae merger's molecular clouds, finding two cloud populations, strong star formation in the overlap region, and environmental conditions that may form super star clusters.

quotes.quote: To study this process, we used the Submillimeter Array and the Plateau de Bure Interferometer to obtain high angular resolution ( 1 5 or 160 pc) images of the Antennae overlap region in CO(2--1) to search for the molecular progenitors of the super star clusters.

A broken power-law fit to the mass function yields slopes of = -1.39 0.10 and = -1.44 0.14 for the low- and high-mass cloud population, well-matched to the mass function found for super star clusters in the Antennae galaxies. We find large velocity gradients and velocity dispersions at the locations of intense star formation, suggestive of compressive shocks. It is likely that these environmental factors contribute to the formation of the observed massive molecular clouds and super star clusters in the Antennae galaxies.

In this paper we revisit the Antennae galaxies, NGC 4038 and NGC 4039, with higher resolution imaging of the molecular gas to study the formation of SSCs. As one of the most spectacular starbursts in the local universe, the Antennae has been studied in great detail over the last several decades in various wavelengths, ranging from the X-rays to the mm/cm regime, and everything in between (e.g., mirabel98,neff00,wilson00,wilson03,fabbiano01,fabbiano03,hibbard01,hibbard05,zhang01,kilgard02,mengel02,zazas02a,zazas02b,fall05 ). It is also one of the best-studied SSC populations whitmore95,whitmore99,whitmore10 . The overlap region of the Antennae, where NGC 4038 and NGC 4039 collide, is one of the most intensely and violently star-forming regions in the nearby universe evans97,mirabel98,gao01,gao08,brandl09,klaas10,zhang10 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Antennae as a two-galaxy prograde merger with long tidal tails, molecular gas in the overlap region, and a new model near second passage before coalescence.

quotes.quote: The Antennae consists of two distinct galaxies (NGC 4038 and NGC 4039), each with long tidal tails which cross close to their respective disks. The ``overlap region'' of the disks contains most of the molecular gas Wilson2000 and is the primary source of the far-infrared luminosity Mirabel1998,Xu2000 , though it is not clear if the activity in this region is due to physical contact between the two disks or if they are seen as overlapping in projection. Neither nucleus has evidence for a strong AGN. As with the previous systems, the tidal tails suggest prograde interactions for both disks.

Consistent with expectations from visual inspection of the tail morphology and with previous dynamical models for the system, we find an encounter geometry of two prograde disks (Figure fig:idkAntennae ). The disk angles are (i, )=(65^ , 345^ ) and (70^ , 95^ ) for NGC 4038 and NGC 4039 respectively. The separation at first pericentric passage is approximately 5 kpc. The system is viewed around its second passage (t _ now =260 Myr after first passage) and the main bodies will merge in approximately 70 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper studies the Antennae, a nearby gas-rich spiral merger with active star formation and long tidal tails, using VLA polarimetry to trace magnetic fields and radio emission.

quotes.quote: The Antennae pair (NGC 4038/9) is the nearest merger 22 Mpc; schwe08 between two gas-rich spirals. The bodies of the colliding galaxies host sites of active star formation in the form of super starclusters, with a global star formation rate of 20 M _ yr ^ 1 zhang01 . The Antennae galaxies hosts tidal tails that measure over 100 kpc in H i and likely originate from outskirts of the progenitors.

We have studied the magnetic field properties of the spiral galaxies NGC 4038/9, also known as the Antennae galaxies, undergoing late stage merger. The galaxies were observed between 2 and 4 GHz using the VLA in DnC and CnB arrays and studied the polarization properties using the combined DnC+CnB array data.

We detect the total intensity radio continuum emission from the gas-rich southern tidal tail extending up to 18 kpc at 3 level. This region shows steep non-thermal spectrum with lying in the range 1.2 to 1.6 , indicating that the tail is composed of an older population of CREs. Employing the technique of RM synthesis, we detect polarized emission from the southern tidal tail extending up to 20 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper uses MUSE spectroscopy of the Antennae Galaxy to map faint diffuse ionized gas in the central merger and southern tidal-tail region, finding Lyman-continuum leakage can explain it.

quotes.quote: The ``Antennae Galaxy'' (NGC 4038/39) is the closest major interacting galaxy system and therefore often taken as merger prototype. We present the first comprehensive integral field spectroscopic dataset of this system, observed with the MUSE instrument at the ESO VLT. We cover the two regions in this system which exhibit recent star-formation: the central galaxy interaction and a region near the tip of the southern tidal tail. In these fields, we detect and diffuse ionized gas to unprecedented depth.

By comparing luminosities with the HST catalog of young star clusters in the central field, we estimate that there is enough Lyman-continuum leakage in the merger to explain the amount of diffuse ionized gas that we detect. We compare the Lyman-continuum escape fraction of each against ionization-parameter sensitive emission line ratios. While we find no systematic trend between these properties, the most extreme line ratios seem to be strong indicators of density bounded ionization. Extrapolating the Lyman-continuum escape fractions to the southern region, we conclude that just from the comparison of the young stellar populations to the ionized gas there is no need to invoke other ionization mechanisms than Lyman-continuum leaking for the diffuse ionized gas in the Antennae.

sources.summary: The paper uses Hubble observations of the Antennae to test a simulation-trained merger-stage regressor, finding stable redshift variants and agreement with dynamical models.

quotes.quote: Second, we test our model on a well-known system, the Antennae galaxies. Our models trained on simulation snapshots successfully predict the merger stage to the the correct order of magnitude on real observations, matching estimates produced by detailed dynamical modeling. Antennae Observations In addition to simulation data, we use archival Hubble Space Telescope observations of the Antennae galaxies (NGC 4038/NGC 4039) to test our model https://www.spacetelescope.org/projects/fits_liberator/antennaedata/ . This well-known system is an archetypal major merger at redshift z=0.05 which has been extensively modeled 2008AN....329.1042K,2018MNRAS.475.3934L .

Testing on Antennae Observations We tested our two-filter, simulation-trained model on four redshift variants of the Hubble space Telescope observations of the Antennae galaxy ( z=0.5, 1.0, 1.5 and 2.0 ). Similar regression estimates suggest stability of the model across redshifts. Existing dynamical models of the system 2008AN....329.1042K,2018MNRAS.475.3934L estimate that the time of observation is between 500-600 Myrs after first passage. This corresponds to normalized times of 0.24-0.29 at z=0.05 (Figure fig:antennae ).

sources.summary: The paper applies a segmentation method to Chandra observations of the Antennae, describing complex diffuse emission, embedded point sources, dust-lane absorption, and soft southern halo emission.

quotes.quote: The \ observations of the Antennae galaxies provide a good test case for application of . The X-ray data (see Figure  fig:antennae , top left panel) shows complex structures. Specifically, the data reveal several point sources and extended regions, with several clumps of diffuse emission of different extent and surface brightness, along with a population of unresolved point-like sources superposed. Some of the point sources lie within the extended sources (e.g., in the extended region at the bottom of the image) and some of the extended sources are entangled with each other. As a conservative scenario we used the first \ observation of the Antennae galaxies obtained on December 1st 1999 OBSID 315; Fabbiano2001 .

Notice that the maps clearly demonstrate that the diffuse emission in the Antennae generally have softer spectra than the point sources. Maps such as these can be used to identify the extent of dust lanes in the Antennae system; e.g., the segments at (0.4,0.22), (0.3,0.25), and (0.6,0.75), which are characterized by harder spectra than the surrounding segments, a characteristic of increased absorption cf., Zezas2006 . Furthermore, notice that the southern region (around Region 3 in the bottom left panel of Figure  fig:antennae ) is surrounded by a halo of relatively soft X-ray emission, in agreement with the spectral analysis of who find emission from soft 0.6 keV thermal emitting gas.

sources.summary: The paper studies the Antennae Galaxies as the closest major merger, cataloging overlap-region GMCs and finding high luminosities, surface densities, turbulent pressures, linewidths, and SSC-forming conditions.

quotes.quote: As the closest major galaxy merger and home to thousands of super star clusters (SSCs), the Antennae Galaxies (NGC 4038 and NGC 4039) are an important location to study the molecular clouds at sites of vigorous star formation. We cataloged giant molecular clouds (GMCs) in the region where the two galaxies overlap using high-resolution ( 0.1" 10 pc) Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) observations of the ^ 12 CO(2-1) and ^ 13 CO(2-1) emission lines. Of the 72 individual GMCs identified in the overlap region, 17 are within uncertainties of having the necessary mass, pressure, and size needed to form super star clusters (SSCs). Of those 17 GMCs, only one has significant ionizing radiation, indicating that the birth environments are likely still intact in the 16 other GMCs.

At a distance of 22 Mpc 2008AJ....136.1482S , the merging Antennae galaxies (NGC 4038/39) are of particular interest as the closest major galaxy merger. Their interaction has resulted in a dense region under high pressure where the galaxies overlap in their merger referred to as the overlap region.

Compared to other sources in this sample, the GMCs from the Antennae, as well as in other starbursts and in the centers of galaxies, have the highest luminosities, surface densities, and turbulent pressures. The GMCs in starbursts and at the centers of galaxies also have large linewidths, although the linewidths in the Antennae are black among the widest .

sources.summary: The paper compares the Antennae cluster luminosity function to M51, describing its double-power-law form, brighter bend, higher mass truncation, and more massive GMC truncation as environmental differences.

quotes.quote: The LF of clusters in the ``Antennae'' galaxies 1999AJ....118.1551W , however, is much better approximated by a double power-law distribution. The bright side ( M_V 10 ) has a steeper slope ( 2.7 ) than the faint side ( 2 ). The latter is close to the value found for other galaxies. This double power-law nature with a bend , was interpreted by the authors as a turn-over in the mass function.

M51 and the ``Antennae'' are forming enough clusters such that the CIMF is sampled until , and a bend in the LF is observable. The difference between \ in M51 and the ``Antennae'' galaxies suggest that \ is environment dependent. These environmental differences might be caused by variations in the giant molecular cloud (GMC) mass distribution. 2003ApJ...599.1049W shows that the cloud mass function of the ``Antennae'' galaxies is truncated at higher masses than that of M51, which in turn is at much higher masses than in the Milky Way 1997ApJ...476..166W .

sources.summary: The paper studies the Antennae galaxies as a nearby interacting pair, mapping molecular gas and dust, modeling PDR conditions, and describing them as an early or intermediate-stage merger.

quotes.quote: To study the properties of the interstellar medium in the prototypical merging system of the Antennae galaxies (NGC 4038 and NGC 4039), we have obtained (1--0), (2--1) and (3--2) line maps, as well as a map of the 870 m continuum emission. Our results are analysed in conjunction with data from X-ray to radio wavelengths. In order to distinguish between exact coincidence and merely close correspondence of emission features, we compare the morphological structure of the different emission components at the highest available angular resolution. To constrain the physical state of the molecular gas, we apply models of photon dominated regions (PDRs) that allow us to fit CO and [ C ii ] data, as well as other indicators of widespread PDRs in the Antennae system, particularly within the super giant molecular cloud (SGMC) complexes of the interaction region (IAR) between the two galaxies.

The Antennae galaxies (NGC 4038/39, Arp 244) are the nearest example of such a strongly interacting pair of galaxies. For the distance of the system (see Stanford et al. 1990; Wilson et al. 2000; Gao et al. 2001) we adopt 20 Mpc (1 '' covers 100 pc). Believed to be a symmetric encounter between two normal Sc type spirals, the Antennae have been well-studied at most wavelengths and are the subject of extended numerical simulations. The Antennae system is thought to be a merger in its early or possibly intermediate stages.

sources.summary: The paper studies the Antennae merger and its young star-cluster system, finding a log-normal intrinsic mass function for clusters formed in the interaction-driven starburst.

quotes.quote: The Antennae (NGC 4038/39 = Arp 244) is a pair of late type spirals in the course of merging. It is the youngest in Toomre's (1977) dynamical age sequence of 11 interacting and merging systems and comfortably nearby. This makes it a well-studied system all over the wavelength range from X-ray through radio wavelengths. The interaction has triggered a strong burst of star formation that has also produced a large number of 700 bright star clusters as first observed with HST by Whitmore Schweizer (1995) ( WS95 ). Many of those bright clusters seem to be young Globular Clusters ( GCs ) due to their small effective radii and high luminosities.

The intrinsic MF we obtain for the YSCs brighter than the completeness limit in The Antennae clearly looks log-normal in shape with the maximum at a mean YSC mass of 4 10^5 M_ . Stellar mass loss within the clusters from the present mean age of 2 10^8 yr through an age of 12 Gyr will lead to a decrease in mass of 15 for a Scalo -- IMF ( < 10 for Salpeter). Thus, without any destruction or evaporation effects the mean mass of the secondary GC system in The Antennae at the age of a Hubble time would be 3.4 10^5 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper adopts a revised Antennae distance and uses NICMOS imaging to identify the NGC 4038 and NGC 4039 nuclei, emphasizing dust-obscured nuclear complexity.

quotes.quote: We use a different distance to the Antennae as compared to Paper I. In that paper we adopted the distance of 21.6 implied by the observed redshift of the Antennae, neglecting any potential peculiar velocity of the system. Instead, we adopt here the value of 13.8 Mpc, which was recently derived from measurements of the tip of the red giant branch TRGB sav04 . While this distance puts the Antennae closer than has been assumed in most previous work, the TRGB-method has been well-demonstrated and the inferred distance should be more accurate than the canonical redshift-distance.

The nucleus of NGC 4039 is an easily identified concentrated source (see Figure f:fortyfortyninevk ). By contrast, NGC 4038 does not seem to possess a highly concentrated nuclear region; however, a probable nucleus can be identified in all three NIR filterbands (marked with a box in Figure f:fortyfortyeightvk ). Given the patchy dust obscuring the view into the nucleus in the optical regime, optical identifications of the nuclei can be misleading.

sources.summary: The paper uses The Antennae as a local merger analogue, comparing its luminous HMXB population and young star clusters with expectations for primordial globular clusters.

quotes.quote: For example, the HMXB populations in recent galaxy mergers such as ``The Antennae'' cf. 2001ApJ...554.1035F and ``The Cartwheel'' cf. 2004A&A...426..787W have X-ray luminosities in excess of L_X 10^ 38 erg/s . This is interesting because, as we discuss below, young star clusters in such galaxy mergers are likely to be local analogues of primordial globular clusters, and suggests that our assumed X-ray luminosities are reasonable. It is less straightforward to assess whether or not HMXBs form in the numbers we might expect based on our model in young star clusters in ``The Antennae'' or ``The Cartwheel''. The luminosity functions presented in 2004NuPhS.132..369G and 2004A&A...426..787W can provide us with some insight, however.

Of order 50 HMXB candidates are known in ``The Antennae''. Without knowing the detailed spatial distribution of these candidates, it is difficult to do a straightforward comparison -- the sources may be associated with a single cluster, in which case f_ sur =1 would seem to be favoured, or they could be associated with 10 clusters in which case f_ sur =0.1 would be favoured. Clearly, this is a very uncertain game to play and would require more detailed modelling (e.g. taking observed distribution of SSCs in the Antennae, populating them with HMXBs and making mock observations), but the numbers do not seem unreasonable.

sources.summary: The paper models the interacting Antennae galaxies, matching their morphology, kinematics, overlap-region starburst, star-formation history, and merger stage shortly after the second encounter.

quotes.quote: We present a new high-resolution N-body/SPH simulation of an encounter of two gas-rich disk galaxies which closely matches the morphology and kinematics of the interacting Antennae galaxies (NGC 4038/39). The simulation includes radiative cooling, star formation and feedback from SNII. The large-scale morphology and kinematics are determined by the internal structure and the orbit of the progenitor disks.

This indicates that the Antennae galaxies are in a special phase only about 40 after the second encounter and 50 before their final collision. This is the only phase in the simulation when a gas-rich overlap region between the nuclei is forming accompanied by enhanced star formation. The star formation rate as well as the recent star formation history in the central region agree well with observational estimates. For the first time this new model explains the distributed extra-nuclear star formation in the Antennae galaxies as a consequence of the recent second encounter.

sources.summary: The paper presents Herschel PACS far-infrared maps of the interacting Antennae system, resolving dusty star-forming knots, especially the overlap-region complexes K1 and K2.

quotes.quote: The Antennae ( Arp 244 , NGC 4038 / NGC 4039 ) is a key object in this sample, since it is an interactive system, where strong extranuclear star formation was found previously in the so-called overlap area of the two disks between the two nuclei. Optical images show dark dust lanes interspersed with a few HII regions in this area e.g. whitmore95 . It houses several supergiant molecular cloud complexes stanford90,wilson00,gao01,schulz07 , which provide a large reservoir for bursts of star formation. Prominent 6 and 20 cm radio emission hummel86 , which spatially matches the CO emisison peaks, suggests there are ongoing starbursts.

The Antennae is an interacting luminous (10 ^ 11 L _ ) IR system close to the 2nd encounter when the two galaxy disks will start to merge into a single system e.g. karl08 . The new FIR observations, along with the available radio and CO data, suggest that the gravitational forces have shuffled a huge amount of interstellar material along an arc connecting the two galaxy nuclei. Several kpc-sized, large ISM complexes house the most active star formation sites and evolve independently.

sources.summary: The thesis uses the Antennae galaxies as the closest major-merger reference, describing their tidal tails, repeated passages, starburst regions, and kinematic progenitor clues.

quotes.quote: For mergers, the natural candidate is the Antennae galaxies (NGC 4038/39, antennae ), named after the peculiar shape of their long tails, already observed in photographic plates by Duncan1923 Back then, the two galaxies were still considered as a single nebula .

Velocity maps and early simulations of the Antennae suggest that the two nuclei have already undergone a passage next to each other and that we are observing the second one Note however that this contradicts the classification of the Antennae as the first step of the Toomre sequence (see toomre_sequence ) while the next step (represented by the Mice) clearly shows an undergoing first passage. See also Karl2010 . . These two passages would be responsible for the fireworks we see today. One might notice the major features of this region: the arc-shape structure in the northern part surrounding the nucleus of NGC 4038, and the overlap region between the two cores.

In order to better understand the Antennae, one has to gather morphological and kinematical data, from the numerous different observational sources. The work of Hibbard2001 brings many clues, through maps. E.g., in hibbard , one can easily distinguish the two progenitors thanks to steep velocity gradients.

sources.summary: The review uses the Antennae as a nearby interacting, IR-bright galaxy pair with thousands of young super star clusters and well-studied cluster masses and ages.

quotes.quote: The ICMF has been extensively studied in the Antennae galaxies, where large numbers of SSCs facilitate the statistics. The Antennae are a nearby (13.3 Mpc, Saviane et al. \ 2008) 2008ApJ...678..179S IR-bright pair of gas-rich interacting galaxies. HST images revealed a system of thousands of young SSCs spread across the galaxy pair 1995AJ....109..960W, 1999AJ....118.1551W . ISO imaging determined that the youngest clusters and brightest infrared emission occur in a dusty and embedded region between the two galaxies 1998A&A...333L...1M .

The cluster mass function (CMF) in the Antennae is power law with 2 over the range of masses 10^4 - 10^6 M _ 2005ApJ...631L.133F . The median cluster mass in the Antennae appears to be about an order of magnitude less than the median globular cluster mass of giant ellipticals such as M87 2002IAUS..207..545H . Ages determined from the SSC colors in the Antennae range from 10^6 - 10^8 yr 1999ApJ...527L..81Z , with a median of 10-20 Myr 2005ApJ...631L.133F, 2005A&A...443...41M , but with a small population extending to more than 10^ 9 yrs.

sources.summary: The paper treats the Antennae as a nearby colliding disk-galaxy pair, uses simulations matching its morphology and star-formation history, and concludes its cluster disruption history resembles that in quieter galaxies.

quotes.quote: The most intensively studied interacting galaxies are the Antennae ( NGC 4038 /39), at a distance D 20 SchweizerEtAl2008AJ . They consist of two normal disk galaxies that began to collide a few 10^8 ago. The stellar population and interstellar medium (ISM) of the Antennae have been observed over an enormous range of wavelengths, from X-ray to radio see, e.g. and references therein ZhangFallWhitmore2001ApJ, HibbardEtAl2001AJ, KassinEtAl2003AJ, ZezasEtAl2006ApJS..166..211Z, 2009ApJ...699.1982B, 2010A&A...518L..44K .

Interacting and merging galaxies like the Antennae generally show signs of enhanced star formation in the past few 10^8 , along with more complex morphology and kinematics, compared with isolated disk galaxies. In this Section, we briefly present and analyze all published and two new simulations of the Antennae galaxies that make specific predictions for their star formation history.

The main conclusion of this paper is that the star clusters in the interacting Antennae galaxies are disrupted in much the same way as those in other galaxies. In most if not all star-forming galaxies, including the Antennae, the observed age distribution of clusters can be approximated by a power law, dN/d ^ , with an exponent in the range 1.0 0.7 (see 2010ApJ...713.1343C and Section CFH here).

sources.summary: The paper describes the Antennae as a benchmark interacting pair with unusually strong regular magnetic fields between the galaxies, along a tidal tail, and about twice typical spiral strengths.

quotes.quote: Up to now, properties of magnetic fields were studied in detail only for one merging system -- the Antennae galaxies (Chy zy Beck ). This is one of the best known pair of interacting objects. Even though it is still debatable how advanced is this system in the process of merging. According to the most recent studies the galaxies are already after the second encounter and not after the first one, as was considered earlier (Karl et al. ).

The most unusual magnetic fields in the Antennae system are those strong and regular ones in between galaxies -- in the overlapping region. Strong regular fields are also at the base of a tidal tail, from where they are stretched and transported along the tidal tail into the intergalactic medium. In this system magnetic fields are two times stronger than in typical spirals.

sources.summary: The paper treats The Antennae as a nearby gas-rich spiral merger whose infrared-bright, merger-triggered star formation and cold/warm molecular gas are heated by turbulent motions plus supernovae and stellar winds.

quotes.quote: The Antennae (NGC 4038/39, Arp 244) is a young, nearby, ongoing merger between two gas rich spiral galaxies, NGC 4038 and NGC 4039. At a distance of only 22 3 Mpc schweizer2008 , the Antennae may represent the nearest such merger. Its infrared brightness of L_ IR = 7.5 10^ 10 L_ gao2001 approaches that of a LIRG and is likely the result of merger triggered star formation. The majority of the star formation is occurring within the two nuclei (NGC 4038, NGC 4039), along with a third region where the two disks are believed to overlap stanford1990 , also sometimes referred to as the interaction region (IAR, e.g. schulz2007 ).

We find that the molecular gas in the Antennae is in both a cold ( T_ kin 10-30 K ) and a warm ( T_ kin 100 K ) state, with the warm molecular gas comprising only 0.2 of the total molecular gas fraction in the Antennae. Furthermore, the physical state of the molecular gas does not vary substantially, with the pressure of both the warm and cold components being nearly constant within uncertainties and our angular resolution across the Antennae.

Mechanical heating is sufficient to match the total cooling and heat the molecular gas throughout the Antennae, with both turbulent heating due to the ongoing merger, and supernovae and stellar winds contributing to the mechanical heating.

sources.summary: The paper uses the Antennae galaxies as a nearby starburst-merger benchmark, presenting a parsec-scale simulation of merger-driven stellar populations and star-cluster formation.

quotes.quote: The Antennae galaxies (NGC 4038/39, Arp 244) have received a particular attention, both from the observational and the numerical sides, being the closest major merger in the starburst phase ( 20 , Whitmore1999a , see also Saviane2008, Schweizer2008 ). Several regions, and not only the galactic nuclei, host the formation of very massive stellar objects ( 10^6 , 10 , Whitmore1995, Mengel2005, Whitmore2007, Bastian2009, Whitmore2010, Herrera2011 ). In total, more than 1000 star clusters have been detected in the central 10 of the Antennae. The richness of this system and the profusion of observational constraints make the Antennae an excellent benchmark for theories and simulations on the role of merger in (re-)shaping the structural properties of galaxies and of their stellar populations.

In this paper, we present a hydrodynamical simulation of the Antennae galaxies, at 1.5 resolution, including star formation and stellar feedback (photo-ionisation, radiative pressure and supernovae). The gain from previous works in term of resolution and physics implemented allows us to better describe the formation and early evolution of stellar systems by probing smaller objects and resolving the inner structure of the most massive cases. Renaud2014a used this simulation to establish a relation between the compressive nature of the tides and the interstellar turbulence in mergers and the enhancement of star formation. Here, we focus our analysis on the properties of the stellar populations formed during the several stages of the interaction.

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 244 as an interacting, starbursting Antennae merger and maps H-alpha superbubbles across its HII regions to estimate shell sizes, expansion velocities, luminosities, masses, energies, and ages.

quotes.quote: The strongly interacting pair of galaxies NGC 4038/4039 (also Arp 244, and commonly known as the Antennae) is in the intermediate stages of a merger. The interaction has triggered a powerful starburst, notably in the overlap region where the interaction is strongest. As this object is one of the nearest examples of this type of starbursts it attracts considerable attention, and is one of the most studied galaxy pairs in the sky. This environment is an ideal laboratory for the probing of feedback by massive stars.

We applied bubbly , a program sensitive to the presence of expanding shells to a Fabry-P e rot observation of the Antennae pair of galaxies mapping the H emission line. The program detects and fits multiple components to the H line profile, for which the data obtained with GH FaS is ideal given its high kinematic (8 km s ^ 1 ) and spatial (seeing-limited) resolution. Using a map of the detected expansion we report the presence of 17 superbubbles associated with most of the brightest HII regions in the object. We estimate the sizes, expansion velocities and luminosities of the superbubbles and use these quantities to derive their shell density, mass, kinetic energy and age.

sources.summary: The paper models the Antennae galaxies as a gas-rich spiral merger, matching their morphology, off-nuclear starburst, tidal tails, and cluster metallicities while following evolution into a remnant.

quotes.quote: We present a high-resolution smoothed particle hydrodynamics simulation of the Antennae galaxies (NGC 4038/4039) and follow the evolution 3 Gyrs beyond the final coalescence. The simulation includes metallicity dependent cooling, star formation, and both stellar feedback and chemical enrichment. The simulated best-match Antennae reproduces well both the observed morphology and the off-nuclear starburst. We also produce for the first time a simulated two-dimensional metallicity map of the Antennae and find good agreement with the observed metallicity of off-nuclear stellar clusters, however the nuclear metallicities are overproduced by 0.5 dex.

The Antennae galaxies (NGC 4038/4039) are the nearest and arguably best studied example of an ongoing major merger of two gas-rich spiral galaxies. This system contains two clearly visible, still distinct discs, together with the signature feature of a beautiful pair of narrow elongated tidal tails extending to a projected size of 20' , which corresponds to 100 kpc at an estimated distance of 20 Mpc. The exact distance to the Antennae system is still surprisingly uncertain.

sources.summary: The paper uses an Antennae-like interacting-galaxy simulation that reproduces the observed Antennae star-formation activity and compares modeled regions with real CO-bright, massive-cluster-forming areas.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we analyze a parsec-resolution simulation of an Antennae-like model of interacting galaxies. We find that the interplay of physical processes has complex and important variations in time and space, through different combinations of mechanisms like tides, shear and turbulence. These can have similar imprints on observables like depletion time and CO emission.

The simulation also retrieves the observed star formation activity of the Antennae during the interaction (see below). Uncertainties remain on the validity of non-integrated quantities like the number, spatial distribution and masses of star-forming clouds that would change, e.g., by varying the density threshold and SFE, even if keeping the SFR unchanged. However, the morphology of the star-forming areas and the properties of the young massive cluster formed in the simulation are in line with the observations Renaud2015 , such that our models can be used to help the interpretation of observation data down to the scale of 10 .

In the real Antennae, the corresponding regions yield strong CO(1-0) emission Wilson2003 , and host or have recently hosted intense star formation activities, in particular in the form of massive star clusters 5 10 old, see e.g. Whitmore1999, Bastian2009, Herrera2012 .

sources.summary: The paper studies dense gas and star formation across the merging Antennae, finding regional variation, low dense-gas efficiency in both nuclei, and vigorous overlap-region star formation.

quotes.quote: We study the relationship between dense gas and star formation in the Antennae galaxies by comparing ALMA observations of dense gas tracers (HCN, HCO ^+ , and HNC J =1-0 ) to the total infrared luminosity ( L_ TIR ) calculated using data from the Herschel Space Observatory and the Spitzer Space Telescope. The Antennae extends the linear L_ TIR L_ HCN relationship found in previous studies. The L_ TIR L_ HCN ratio varies by up to a factor of 10 across different regions of the Antennae implying variations in the star formation efficiency of dense gas, with the nuclei, NGC 4038 and NGC 4039, showing the lowest SFE _ dense (0.44 and 0.70 10^ 8 yr ^ 1 ).

The rarity of wet, major mergers (gas-rich galaxies with a mass ratio 3) makes the Antennae a particularly unique environment for studying star formation in interactions. Recent simulations suggest the Antennae is 40 Myr after its second pass Karl:2010 , placing it at an intermediate stage in the Toomre sequence. Thus, the Antennae contains multiple generations of stars from merger-induced starburst behavior. Furthermore, different regions within the Antennae exhibit varying degrees of current ( 100 Myr, Brandl:2009 ) star formation, with the overlap region of the Antennae (see Fig. fig:data ) experiencing a particularly violent episode ( Star Formation Rate, SFR >4 M _ yr ^ 1 , Brandl:2009,Klaas:2010 ; this work).

sources.summary: The paper uses the Antennae galaxy as a local merger validation case, degrading ALMA CO data so the interacting pair becomes indistinguishable and falls in the method's merger region.

quotes.quote: The Antennae galaxy, a prototypical example of a merger system in the local Universe, as observed at low-angular resolution. ALMA Science Verification observations of the CO(3-2) emission line are employed for creating these data https://almascience.nrao.edu/alma-data/science-verification/antennae-galaxies https://almascience.nrao.edu/alma-data/science-verification/antennae-galaxies . The low- z spiral and the Antennae galaxy are in the disk and merger regions defined by the SERRA disks and merger distributions, respectively (empty markers in Fig. fig:pvcube ).

The Antennae or NGC4038/9 are two spiral galaxies and the nearest example of a major galaxy merger. In Sect. sec:new_par , we used the ALMA Science Verification data targeting their CO(3-2) emission line e.g., Herrera_2012 . Since we wanted to apply and PVsplit on medium/low-angular resolution data, we convolved the ALMA high-angular resolution datacube (beam size of 1.2" 0.6") with a Gaussian beam of 122". As a result, similarly to the mock data of the simulated merging galaxies discussed in this work, the two interacting galaxies are indistinguishable and we fit the kinematics of the system with 2 independent resolution elements along the semi-major axis.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes ordered magnetic fields in the Antennae galaxies, comparing FIR and radio polarization and linking field differences to merger-disturbed warm gas and relic-arm dynamics.

quotes.quote: We characterize the morphology of the ordered B-fields in the multi-phase ISM of the closest merger of two spiral galaxies, the Antennae galaxies. We compare the inferred B-fields using 154 thermal dust and 11 cm radio synchrotron emission polarimetric observations. We find that the 154 B-fields are more ordered across the Antennae galaxies than the 11 cm B-fields. The turbulent-to-ordered 154 B-field increases at the galaxy cores and star-forming regions.

The relic spiral arm has an ordered spiral 154 B-field, while the 11 cm B-field is radial. The 154 B-field may be dominated by turbulent dynamos with high ^ 12 CO(1-0) velocity dispersion driven by star-forming regions, while the 11 cm B-field is cospatial with high HI velocity dispersion driven by galaxy interaction. This result shows the dissociation between the warm gas mainly disturbed by the merger, and the dense gas still following the dynamics of the relic spiral arm.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4038/39 as a merger with enhanced magnetic fields, extended star formation across both disks, warm dust in the overlap region, and X-ray superbubbles and loops.

quotes.quote: Chy zy Beck (2004) have used the VLA to produce detailed maps of radio total power and polarization in NGC 4038/39 (see Chy z y's poster paper). The derived mean total magnetic field of 20 G is twice as strong as in normal spirals and appears tangled in regions of enhanced star formation. The field peaks at 30 G in the southern part of the Overlap Region, suggesting strong compression where the star formation rate is highest.

For example, any good HST, Spitzer, or Chandra image of NGC 4038/39 shows that enhanced star formation extends over a projected area of 8 11 kpc (Fig. 2).
