At 6.75 \ and 15 , the emission is clearly dominated by two bright spots located near the nuclear bar ends (Fig. 1). These peaks are almost twice brighter than the remainder of the circumnuclear region. A third local maximum, much less intense, is visible at the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper builds an H-alpha region catalog for M100, describing it as barred grand-design spiral NGC 4321 with 1,948 measured regions, strong circumnuclear star formation, and miniature spiral armlets.

quotes.quote: From a new mosaic image in the \ line of the complete disc of the spiral galaxy M100, a catalogue is composed listing 1948 individual \ regions. I give details of the data collection and reduction procedure, and of the production of the \ region catalogue. For each \ region, the catalogue gives its position relative to the centre of the galaxy, its deprojected distance to the centre, its radius, and its calibrated luminosity. An indication is included as to whether the \ region is located in the arms, between them, or in the circumnuclear star-forming region.

M100 (=NGC 4321) is a grand-design galaxy of type .SXS4.. (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991) with a moderately strong bar. In previous papers, we have discussed the \ distribution and kinematics (Knapen et al. 1993b) and described CO measurements (Cepa et al. 1992; Knapen et al. 1996), which was combined with the \ image described in detail in the present paper to determine massive SF efficiencies in arm and interarm regions (Knapen et al. 1996). The galaxy has a circumnuclear region (CNR) of strongly enhanced SF, where a pair of miniature spiral armlets occurs in a resonance region between the inner and outer parts of the bar (Knapen et al. 1995a,b). I adopt a distance to M100 of D=16.1 1.3 Mpc (Ferrarese et al. 1996).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC4321 as a Virgo-infalling spiral with a more star-forming bulge, a large main-sequence slope difference, and a clean outer low-SFR sequence.

quotes.quote: The final sample includes 8 objects: NGC0628, NGC3184, NGC3938, NGC4254, NGC4321, NGC4535, NGC5194, NGC5457. Distances, sizes, inclinations and morphologies are those adopted by the DustPedia collaboration, based on the HyperLEDA database The HyperLEDA database is available at http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ 2014A&A...570A..13M , and are available on their website (see Tab. tab:sample ). The sample stellar masses are in the range between 10^ 10 10^ 11 M_ , and star formation rates between 1 and 4 M_ yr ^ 1 These sources represent the evolved descendants of high-redshift normal star-forming galaxies, that are rotationally supported systems, at least by z=2 e.g. 2015ApJ...799..209W, 2009ApJ...706.1364F, 2018ApJS..238...21F . Moreover, their regular dynamical properties suggest that they have quiescently assembled their stellar mass through cosmic times.

The two galaxies that are infalling in the Virgo cluster, NGC4254 and NGC4321, show two peculiar characteristics. NGC4254 is almost entirely made of cells that are located above the spatially resolved MS relation; indeed, this galaxy is the one that, according to its integrated properties, is located at the largest distance from the integrated MS relation (see Fig fig:GlobalProp , X symbol), but still within the MS scatter. NGC4321 and NGC5194, instead, show the largest difference in slope with respect to the average MS relation. Interestingly, it also shows a second 'quenched' sequence below the MS, also observed in galaxies such as NGC4535 and NGC5194, and visible as a ring of red cells in Figures A4, A5 and A6.

sources.summary: The paper uses M100's nuclear ring, central bar, spiral-arm dynamics, and star-forming sites to interpret the progenitor environment of SN 2020oi.

quotes.quote: M100 has been extensively studied due to its close proximity and its active star formation sites Sakamoto1995,1998Garcia_GasM100,2007Castillo_M100,2016Azeez_KSLaw_M100,2018Elmegreen_IRPeaks_M100 . To date, seven SNe have been discovered within M100, but only SN 2020oi occurred within black its central 5 . This makes it possible to leverage previous analyses to further characterize the progenitor system and its formation as a consequence of the dynamical evolution of its host galaxy. SN 2020oi exploded within a ``nuclear ring'' of radius 5 where the majority of star formation within M100 occurs 2001Ryder . 2005Allard used SAURON IFU spectroscopy to probe the ring's H emission and gas dispersion.

In their model of nuclear ring formation, cold gas is channeled inward along the dust lanes of the spiral arms under the gravitational influence of the central bar. This gas settles near the inner Lindblad resonances for the galaxy at the contact points between the nuclear ring and the innermost spiral arms.

sources.summary: WiNDS shows NGC 4321 as a Virgo grand-design spiral with coherent residual velocity flows, dwarf companions, HI extension, and stellar extensions indicating satellite interaction.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:candidate_ngc4321 we show NGC 4321 (M100), a grand-design nearby spiral galaxy belonging to the Virgo Cluster, classified as SABbc (RC3). The galaxy has a small bulge and two well-defined, prominent and, symmetric spiral arms, as shown on its B band image (panel a). The nucleus of the galaxy is compact and bright. NGC 4321 is a very low-inclination galaxy, with a kinematical inclination of i 25^ and an optical radius of R_ opt = 16.3 kpc from RC3.

These perturbations, observed in the ionized gas of NGC 4321, could be the result of a recent interaction with one of its satellite galaxies. Indeed, NGC 4321 has two dwarf companion galaxies, NGC 4328 and NGC 4323, located at projected distances of 24 kpc and 28 kpc from their host, respectively 2005ApJ...632..253H . NGC 4321 also shows diffuse stellar extension in two directions, one of them towards NGC 4323, likely the result of ongoing interaction with this satellite 2005ApJ...632..253H .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC4321 as one of two face-on spiral galaxies observed with PHANGS-ALMA CO data, tracing filamentary gas kinematics, velocity gradients, and hierarchical gravitational collapse.

quotes.quote: We investigated the kinematics and dynamics of gas structures on galaxy-cloud scales in two spiral galaxies NGC5236 (M83) and NGC4321 (M100) using CO (2 1) line. We utilized the FILFINDER algorithm on integrated intensity maps for the identification of filaments in two galaxies. Clear fluctuations in velocity and density were observed along these filaments, enabling the fitting of velocity gradients around intensity peaks. The variations in velocity gradient across different scales suggest a gradual and consistent increase in velocity gradient from large to small scales, indicative of gravitational collapse, something also revealed by the correlation between velocity dispersion and column density of gas structures.

We use the combined 12m+7m+TP PHANGS-ALMA CO (2 1) data cubes to investigate gas kinematics and dynamics in the two galaxies, which have a spectral resolution of 2.5 km s ^ 1 and angular resolutions 2.1 '' and 1.7 '' , corresponding to linear resolutions 51 pc and 123 pc for NGC5236 and NGC4321 at the distances 4.89 Mpc and 15.21 Mpc Leroy2021-257,Anand2021-501 , respectively. The field-of-view (FOV) of NGC5236 and NGC4321 in the ALMA observations are 10.5 Kpc 10.8 Kpc and 17.1 Kpc 15.5 Kpc, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4321 as an intermediate Virgo spiral whose infall history, gas content, abundance dilution, and star formation lie between NGC 4501 and NGC 4303.

quotes.quote: We now present the application of this multiphase chemical evolution to three galaxies in Virgo, NGC 4501, close to the center, an intermediate galaxy as NGC 4321 and a HI normal galaxy NGC 4303, which is in the periphery of the cluster, as typical examples for these different zones. The data used in our models for these three galaxies are presented in Table galaxies where we display their morphological types (T) and Arm Class in columns (2) and (3) as derived by Tully (1988) and Biviano (1991), respectively.

This difference between data and models does not appear in NGC 4303 models. For Model B of NGC 4321 it is 1-1.5 M_ /pc^ 2 for R > 10 kpc , while for Model M it does not exist. This implies that this last model is better able reproduce the outer disk, while the first one better fits the data in the inner regions.

We see that the infall rate is clearly higher in the inner region of NGC 4501 compared to the two other galaxies, NGC 4321 rests in middle of the two others, while NGC 4303 has a slow infall even in this central region. For the outer regions, the infall rate continues to be larger for NGC 4501, intermediate for NGC 4321 and low for NGC 4303, following the same trend as in the 4 kpc radial zone, but differences among galaxies are now smaller.

sources.summary: The paper uses SN2006X to probe M100's disk ISM, describing the Virgo spiral as near-solar with a galaxy-wide dust-to-gas ratio about twice the Milky Way's.

quotes.quote: \ is a normal type Ia supernova that exploded within or behind the disk of the host galaxy M100 (also known as NGC 4321 ) in the Virgo cluster.

The host galaxy recession velocity is 1571 \ (at a distance of 15.2 Mpc) whereas the component of the rotation velocity along the line of sight at the apparent SN location is about 75 \ ( 1995AJ....109.2444R , 2007PASJ...59..117K ). The latter coincides approximately with the strongly saturated Na i D and Ca ii H K components and a weakly saturated CN vibrational band. These strong components, unaffected by the supernova event, originate from an interstellar molecular cloud (or system of clouds) within the disk of M100 at some distance in front of \ 2007Sci...317..924P .

Infrared/sub-mm data shows that M100 is a typical spiral galaxy with a metallicity close to solar, a Galactic like PAH-to-dust mass fraction and a dust-to-gas mass ratio about twice that of the Galaxy 2007ApJ...663..866D . From this point of view, keeping in mind it is an overall average of the entire M100 galaxy, there is twice the amount of dust compared to gas in M100 than in the Milky Way (thus also the lower reddening inferred from CH and CN).

sources.summary: NGC 4321 is presented as a barred spiral where outer gas flow follows spiral arms, bar torques dominate inward, and the spiral corotation radius marks an inflow-outflow switch.

quotes.quote: The inner region of NGC 4321 has been extensively studied in the NIR Kna95a, Kna95b and shows a prominent nuclear bar aligned with the large-scale stellar bar. The large-scale bar is coupled to 4 spiral arms. The gas flow follows the spirals in the outer disk and is increasingly dominated by the torques caused by the bar towards the center.

For the outer disk (from 12 - 18 kpc) we found a significant outflow (d L/L=0.2 ) and on intermediate scales (from 12 - 6 kpc) inflow. The switch from inflow to outflow is roughly occurring at the CR radius of the spirals at 11.2 kpc. At the CR radius of the bar no switch from in- to outflow is found, suggesting that the standard CR determinations are not applicable for this case because of a superposition of bar and spiral wave pattern.

sources.summary: The paper finds that M 100's bar produces a central profile bump, favors a double-Gaussian fit, and corresponds to the largest core fraction in the sample.

quotes.quote: M 100 - The barred nature of the galaxy clearly shows up as a bump in the high-resolution profiles e.g. 24 \ and 250 , see also pohlen , and as a result, the fitting function favors a double Gaussian profile, with very good fits of the central component achieved from 24 \ to 500 . Table tab:results , however, shows the central component is unresolved at 160, 350, and 500 , while for the other wavelengths a scale-length of 11'' 2'' is indicated by the fits. When the central component is unresolved, our capacity to accurately measure its contribution will depend on how bright it is compared to the disk component. Thus it is possible that the core fraction we compute at 160 \ (the band with the worst resolution) is in fact a lower limit to the actual core fraction, pointing again to the limited ability of the MIPS 160 \ band to accurately measure core contributions.

The necessity to account for a core component to represent the surface brightness profile already appears weakly related to the morphological type: it is on the intermediate type galaxy M 100 that we observe the largest core fraction. It is quite tempting, and probably logical, to relate that to the presence of a weak bar in M 100, but this can only be asserted on a reasonable sample, as the presence of a bar does not guarantee there is a strong MIR core component rousselbar .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4321 as a relatively unperturbed barred Virgo spiral with a faint H i tail, roughly symmetric radio emission, and disk radio sources.

quotes.quote: NGC 4321 NGC 4321 is another grand-design barred spiral galaxy of SABb type. It is located at the distance of 3 9 from the cluster core to the north (1.17 Mpc in the sky plane), which places it in the Virgo cluster outskirts. This relatively unperturbed galaxy shows a faint H i tail extending to the southwest (Knapen ).

The total power emission (Fig. 4321tp ) is roughly symmetric with however slightly more extensions to the north. It is consistent with the observations of Urbanik et al. ( ) at 10.7 GHz. The disk contains three radio sources.

sources.summary: The paper compares earlier resolved Herschel analysis of M 100 with its own integrated measurements, noting the prior kappa value and limitations from high-density detected pixels.

quotes.quote: During the \ Science Demonstration Phase, Eales2010C applied a resolved variant of the \ method (where they pegged the dust mass surface density to the gas mass surface density, assuming a fixed Schmidt-Kennicutt law and a Milky Way dust-to-gas ratio) to observations of two of the first HRS targets, M 99 and M 100. Whilst their analysis was limited by the fact they had \ observations of only these two galaxies, and assumed an uncorrected solar metallicity, they noted that once the HRS was complete it would represent an ideal dataset for a full determination of . Converted to our 500 \ reference wavelength as per Equation Equation:Kappa_Wavelength , they found _ 500 = 0.027 m^ 2 kg^ 1 for M 99, and _ 500 = 0.031 m^ 2 kg^ 1 for M 100. These values are smaller than (although still within the uncertainties of) the values we find for these galaxies; indeed, as seen in Figure Fig:Year_vs_Kappa , these are the smallest of all values of \ reported in the literature.

The nature of the resolved analysis employed by Eales2010C makes it difficult to directly compare our values; however, the fact that they could only consider pixels with significant detections of dust, , and CO, will have limited their analysis to regions with higher ISM surface-density -- regions which might therefore have systematically different dust properties. In contrast to this, the reader should note that we are using integrated, galaxy-wide values for all of our measurements.

sources.summary: The paper describes M 100 as a Virgo barred spiral with symmetric arms, a circumnuclear ring, H II/LINER nucleus, and measured SFR, stellar, dust, and H I masses.

quotes.quote: M 100 (NGC 4321, Fig. subfig:m100_maps ) is located at a distance of 15.9 Mpc 2010PASP..122.1397S and it is a member of the Virgo Cluster. M 100 has been classified as SAB(s) by 1995yCat.7155....0D , with two well-defined, symmetrical spiral arms emerging from the bar in the galactic centre. M 100 also hosts a circumnuclear ring with a diameter of 2 kpc. 1997ApJS..112..315H classified the nucleus as H ii /LINER.

The present-day SFR is estimated to be around 6 M _ yr ^ 1 2019A&A...624A..80N . M 100 has approximately 5 10^ 10 M _ of stars, 4 10^7 M _ of dust 2019A&A...624A..80N , and 3 10^9 M _ of H i gas 2019A&A...623A...5D .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4321 as a grand-design example where azimuthal PHANGS fluctuations correlate, with high star formation and molecular gas concentrated in spiral arms.

quotes.quote: Figure n4321-deltas shows correlated fluctuations between several quantities for the grand-design galaxy NGC 4321, shown in the insert. These are all fluctuations in azimuth, meaning that the average value of the quantity at that radius has been subtracted. In the various panels, the excess molecular surface density _ mol is shown versus the excess SFR density, _ SFR (lower left), the excess CO velocity dispersion is shown versus the excess _ SFR (upper left) and _ mol (upper right), and the excess virial parameter _ vir is shown versus the excess _ SFR (lower left).

The figure shows a positive correlation for ( _ SFR ) in the sense that regions with higher than the azimuthally-averaged _ SFR at their radius also have higher . We note there are not many regions with low _ SFR or low pixel-wise measurements of _ mol (blue dots in the top-right) for NGC 4321, although there are sub-zero values. This is because star formation is highly concentrated in a small fraction of each annulus where there are spiral arms, so the increases above average are strong but atypical and the decreases below average are close to the average.

sources.summary: The paper studies M100 as a barred spiral whose nuclear ring is fed by bar-driven gas inflow and has formed stars in repeated bursts over about 500 Myr.

quotes.quote: We present integral field spectroscopic observations obtained with SAURON of the bar and circumnuclear region of the barred spiral galaxy M100, complemented by new Spitzer Space Telescope imaging of the region. Aims We use these data to enhance our understanding of the formation, evolution, and current properties of the bar and ring. Methods We derive the kinematics of the gas and the stars and quantify circular and non-circular motions using kinemetry.

Our kinematic and morphological results thus confirm a picture in which the nuclear ring in M100, considered typical, is fed by gas flowing in from the disc under the action of the bar, is slowed down near a pair of resonances, and forms significant amounts of massive stars. Detailed stellar population modelling shows how the underlying bulge and disc were put in place a number of Gyr ago, and that the nuclear ring has been forming stars since about 500 Myr ago in a stable succession of bursts. This confirms that nuclear rings can form under the influence of a resonant structure set up by a bar, and proves that they are stable features of a galaxy rather than one-off starburst events.

sources.summary: The paper describes M 100 as a spiral with non-axisymmetric central gas motions, matching OIII and stellar kinematic features, plus an old center, young ring, and surrounding intermediate-age stars.

quotes.quote: Another galaxy with strong non-axisymmetric gas motions in the central regions is M 100 (NGC 4321). Knapen et al. (2000b) present an H image with strong streaming motions, and model it with a numerical model. Wong et al. (2004) perform a harmonic Fourier decomposition. In Allard et al. (2006) we show that the [ OIII ] map of M100 accurately reproduces the non-axisymmetric features, and that these features are also found in the stellar kinematics.
The maps show that the center of M 100 is relatively old. Going outwards, a very young ring follows, after which there is a region with stars of about 10 ^8 years (see A06 ).

sources.summary: The paper maps ionized and molecular gas in M100, finding circumnuclear rotation, bar and mini-spiral streaming motions, and evidence that a double inner Lindblad resonance shapes the core.

quotes.quote: We present high angular and velocity resolution two-dimensional kinematic observations in the spectral lines of \ and CO J=1 0 of the circumnuclear starburst region in the barred spiral galaxy M100, and compare them with kinematics derived from our previously published numerical modeling. The \ data, fully sampled and at sub-arcsecond resolution, show a rotation curve that is rapidly rising in the central 140 pc, and stays roughly constant, at the main disk value, further out. Non-circular motions are studied from the \ and CO data by detailed consideration of the velocity fields, residual velocity fields after subtraction of the rotation curve, and sets of position-velocity diagrams. These motions are interpreted as the kinematic signatures of gas streaming along the inner part of the bar, and of density wave streaming motions across a two-armed mini-spiral. Comparison with a two-dimensional velocity field and rotation curve derived from our 1995 dynamical model shows good qualitative and quantitative agreement for the circular and non-circular kinematic components.
This work focuses on observations and interpretations of the two-dimensional CNR gas kinematics in M100 (=NGC 4321), a galaxy with a bar of moderate strength.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4321 as the Virgo spiral host of SN 2006X, placing the supernova near a spiral arm and tying deep absorption to the galaxy disk.

quotes.quote: SN 2006X was discovered in the Virgo Cluster spiral galaxy NGC 4321 (13) .

The apparent position of SN 2006X is very close to a spiral arm of NGC 4321, which is inclined by 28 degrees with respect to the line of sight (S13) . Due to the relatively high extinction (S11) , the SN most likely exploded within or behind the disk of the host galaxy. Moreover, the SN is projected onto the receding side of the galaxy, and the component of the rotation velocity along the line of sight at the apparent SN location is about 75 km s ^ 1 (S4, S13) , which coincides with the strongly saturated Na I D component (Fig. S1). This suggests rather unequivocally that the time-invariant, very deep absorptions arise within the disk of NGC 4321.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4321/M100 as the nearby Virgo grand-design spiral host of SN 2006X, uses its Cepheid distance, and notes its LINER/H II classification and supernova history.

quotes.quote: We present extensive optical ( UBVRI ), near-infrared ( JK ) light curves and optical spectroscopy of the Type Ia supernova (SN) 2006X in the nearby galaxy NGC 4321 (M100).
It is 12'' W and 48'' S of the center of the nearby galaxy NGC 4321 (M100), a grand-design Sbc galaxy in the Virgo cluster having a Cepheid distance of 30.91 0.14 mag (Freedman et al. 2001).

The host galaxy of SN 2006X, NGC 4321 (M100), is a well-studied LINER/H II galaxy (e.g., Ho, Filippenko, Sargent 1997). As one of the largest spiral galaxies in the Virgo cluster, it produced SNe 1901B, 1914A, 1959E, 1979C, and 2006X in roughly the last century. Ferarrese et al. (1997) reported a Cepheid distance to NGC 4321 of m-M = 31.04 0.17 mag.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4321 as a normal spiral example whose mass-map methods agree closely and whose arm-interarm contrast drops in stellar mass maps.

quotes.quote: For the two grand-design spirals (NGC 4321 and 4254) the radial decrease of M/L is a clear effect of the younger, lower metallicity stars that populate the outer disk see e.g.

For NGC 4321 we observe that the four methods result in very similar structure, with .m.s. in the residuals between 5 and 7 per cent. Method (d) ( _i(g-i) from CB07) provides the closest match to the default method (a), with an average pixel-by-pixel offset of 3 per cent and residual .m.s. of 5 per cent.

sources.summary: The paper reports polarized emission across most of NGC 4321's disk, bright northwest interarm emission, a southeast decline, and generally low polarized fractions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4321 (M100) In this spiral galaxy, polarized emission is clearly detected throughout most of the disk. In the northwest, bright polarized emission is present throughout the interarm regions. But in the southeast the polarized surface brigntess declines dramatically. The lowest brightness of polarized emission occurs near the receding major axis (PA = 159 ^ , as tabulated in Table table:summary ). The polarized fraction in this disk is generally rather low, ranging from less than or about 2 in the inner regions to about 5--10 in the interarm regions, with the polarized fraction tending to be higher outside of the arm than inside the arm.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4321 as the sample's normal field-like spiral, with symmetric extended H I, normal radio properties, rich molecular gas, and star formation tied to molecular gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4321 (Fig. fig:plot_n4321 ): this is the only galaxy in the sample which resembles closely a ``normal'' field spiral galaxy. The H i distribution is symmetric and extends beyond the optical radius. Its radio continuum properties including polarization are also normal (Vollmer et al. 2007). The Nobeyama CO(1--0) distribution is consistent with the IRAM 30m CO(2--1) distribution presented in Schruba et al. (2011). The galaxy is molecular-gas rich (Wong Blitz 2002).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4321's star-forming regions using UV, optical, and IR imaging, finding radial H-alpha trends, modest reddening, and no significant spiral-arm offsets.

quotes.quote: We study the star forming regions in the spiral galaxy NGC 4321 (M100). We take advantage of the spatial resolution (2.5 arcsec FWHM) of the Swift /UVOT camera and the availability of three UV passbands in the region 1600< <3000 \ , in combination with optical and IR imaging from SDSS, KPNO/H and Spitzer /IRAC, to obtain a catalogue of 787 star forming regions out to three disc scale lengths. We use a large volume of star formation histories, combined with stellar population synthesis, to determine the properties of the young stellar component and its relationship with the spiral arms. The H luminosities of the sources have a strong decreasing radial trend, suggesting more massive star forming regions in the central part of the galaxy. When segregated with respect to NUV-optical colour, blue sources have a significant excess of flux in the IR at 8 m, revealing the contribution from PAHs, although the overall reddening of these sources stays below E(B--V)=0.2 mag.

sources.summary: NGC 4321 has a rising isotope-ratio profile, systematic internal variations, and a bright inner region likely fed by bar-driven gas flows.

quotes.quote: In the left panel of Figure fig:trends , we see ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O increase with increasing radius for NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 5055 and NGC 6946. Again NGC 628 represents the outlier, with a low ratio and perhaps a dropping rather than rising profile.
Three of our targets, NGC 3351, NGC 3627, and NGC 4321 host bright inner regions ( 500 pc, 0.05 r_ 25 ) likely fed by gas flows along the bars in these galaxies.

In NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 5055 and NGC 6946 we do observe systematic internal variations. Here ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O increases by 40 with increasing radius. We also find a decreasing ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O ratio with increasing surface density of star formation.

sources.summary: The paper uses M100 as an ALMA cube test case, recovering its spiral morphology, broad spectra, and velocity gradients with compact learned representations.

quotes.quote: The M100 source is a ``grand-design'' spiral galaxy, for which a CO line was used as a tracer of the distribution of molecular hydrogen in the spiral arms.

For instance, M100 shows a clear spiral form in the zero- th moment and a very large FWHM and complex spectrum, while the TW Hya shows a very defined protoplanetary disk in the images with a narrow and a Gaussian shaped spectral line.

For the M100 data, we use the non-parametric DBSCAN algorithm Ester96 to form clusters of neighboring points while discarding the unconnected ones ( =7 [ pix ] ). In a second stage we selected only the 10 brightest clusters to discard very small clusters. At last, we use K-Means to summarize the data in only 50 points. This process is shown in Figure fig:m100-clust , and the final result is reported in the schematic representation of Figure fig:m100-clust2 . In this representation the size of each node represents the flux, the color of each node represents the radial velocity, and the edges correspond to a ``gravity'' connection (i.e., F_i F_j d^2 ) between nodes. The spiral structure and the velocity gradients of M100 can be easily observed in the figure. For example, this representation might help to seed a n-body simulation starting from real data.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4321 to test CO-map choices and finds its high-CO-intensity behavior and central conversion-factor depression resemble those of NGC 3627.

quotes.quote: We estimate the uncertainty associated with our choice of CO map by considering NGC 4321, for which we have ALMA, BIMA, and IRAM 30-m CO (1-0) maps. We only have CARMA data for NGC4254 so we cannot explore how CARMA compares to our other CO (1-0) sources. We repeat our complete analysis with each NGC 4321 CO (1-0) map.

NGC 3627 and NGC 4321 in the EMPIRE data (Figure fig:density-density-empire ) also exhibit different behavior at high and low I_ CO (2-1) . For these galaxies, the slope relating HCN/CO to I_ CO(2-1) steepens near _ 10 I_ CO(2-1) 1.75 ( 55 K km s ^ 1 350 M _ ) and these galaxies show higher _ 10 I_ CO(2-1) and lower HCN/CO compared to the other two targets.
Specifically, they found 2 3 times lower _ CO in the center of NGC 3627 and NGC 4321 compared to the disks.

sources.summary: The paper measures molecular-gas motions in NGC 4321, selecting a spiral-arm region where velocity and density structure functions reveal coherent oscillatory flows on roughly 400 pc scales.

quotes.quote: Here we measure the motion of molecular gas in the Milky Way and in nearby galaxy NGC 4321, assembling observations that span an unprecedented spatial dynamic range ( 10^ 1 10^3 pc).
On large (from 100 pc to > 1000 pc) scales, we analyse observations of nearby galaxy NGC 4321 from the Physics at High Angular resolution in Nearby GalaxieS (PHANGS-ALMA) survey.

The velocity structure functions measured in 1-D along the crests of the spiral arm of NGC 4321 ( Figure:structfunc a) and in the CMZ gas stream ( Figure:structfunc d) exhibit local minima at specific spatial scales. Structure functions display localised minima in response to periodicity in spatial or temporal data, indicating that the observed velocity fluctuations oscillate with an intrinsic wavelength (for an intuitive demonstration of this behaviour, see the Supplementary Information and fig:edf1 ). We measure the wavelengths of these oscillations to be 405^ +92 _ 76 pc and 22.0^ +5.4 _ 6.3 pc in the spiral arm and CMZ gas stream, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4321 as an important Virgo spiral with many supernovae, derives a PHANGS-HST TRGB distance, but adopts the closer Cepheid distance.

quotes.quote: NGC 4321 (M100) is a large spiral galaxy located within the Virgo Cluster. Its location within Virgo, as well as the fact that it has been host to seven observed supernovae (including SN2006X, a type Ia) makes it an extremely important target for which to have an accurate distance. PHANGS-HST has two parallel fields for this target, one of which lies right on top of the nearby dwarf galaxy NGC 4328, which we discuss separately. The other parallel is relatively well-placed, and we use the outer portion of this field to determine m_ TRGB = 26.97 0.23 mag. From this, we derive D = 15.44 1.62 Mpc. Given the relatively large uncertainty in our distance, we adopt the Cepheid distance of D = 15.21 0.49 Mpc 2001ApJ...553...47F to this galaxy, though we note that our own determination is very close to this value.

sources.summary: The paper reports anomalous atomic-hydrogen clouds in M 100, links one cloud to star formation, and argues multiple mechanisms may form such clouds in this Virgo spiral.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of two kinematically anomalous atomic hydrogen ( ) clouds in M 100 (NGC 4321), which was observed as part of the Deciphering the Interplay between the Interstellar medium, Stars, and the Circumgalactic medium ( DIISC ) survey in \ 21 cm at 3.3 \ spectroscopic and 44 30 spatial resolution using the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array The National Radio Astronomy Observatory is a facility of the National Science Foundation operated under cooperative agreement by Associated Universities, Inc. . These clouds were identified as structures that show significant kinematic offsets from the rotating disk of M100. The velocity offsets of 40 \ observed in these clouds are comparable to the offsets seen in intermediate-velocity clouds (IVCs) in the circumgalactic medium (CGM) of the Milky Way and nearby galaxies. We find that one anomalous cloud in M 100 is associated with star-forming regions detected in H and far-ultraviolet imaging. Our investigation shows that anomalous clouds in M 100 may originate from multiple mechanisms, such as star formation feedback-driven outflows, ram-pressure stripping, and tidal interactions with satellite galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4321 to show how measured molecular arm/interarm contrasts rise from prior kiloparsec-scale values to stronger north and south arm contrasts at 100 pc resolution.

quotes.quote: For example, for NGC 4321, 1996A&A...308...27K obtained an average molecular contrast of 1.7 and 1.8 for the north and south spiral, respectively, at a resolution of 15'' ( 1 kpc). They targeted individual pointings with the Nobeyama 45 m telescope along the arms and immediate interarm regions (similar to the second binning alternative that we introduce in Appendix sec:sanity_checks ). Despite the methodological differences, these molecular contrasts are very similar to the ones we obtain at 1.5 kpc resolution, 1.8 and 1.6 for each arm, respectively. However, when we shift to high resolution, the molecular contrasts become 2.7 and 2.4 for the north and south arm, respectively. This warns against direct comparison of contrasts derived at different resolutions.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4321 has CO kinematic signatures suggestive of radial outflow, but argues stellar-bar elliptical streaming likely explains the non-circular motions.

quotes.quote: The CO velocity field of this well-studied Virgo cluster galaxy shows a strong s_1 term peaking between R =20 \ and 40 \ with s_1/c_1 0.35 and a slope |ds_3/ds_1| 0.05 , making it a clear candidate for radial flows. Interpreted as radial flow, the s_1 term implies an outflow at 60 \ in the plane of the galaxy. However, elliptical streaming must be considered as well, since NGC 4321 is known to possess a stellar bar with a radial extent of 60 \ Knapen:95 .

Moreover, the \ velocity field from R =40 -180 \ shows a negative slope in the s_1 s_3 plane as expected for a bar potential [ Fig. fig:s13:4321 (d) ] . The presence of a dominant s_1 term in the CO kinematics would be consistent with our modeling of gas flow in the ILR region of a barred galaxy [ cf. \ Fig. fig:s13models (a) ] .

sources.summary: The review uses M100 as a key circumnuclear-ring example, citing spiral armlets, resolvable star-forming knots, CO arms, and non-circular motions from high-resolution studies.

quotes.quote: The star formation can result from the spontaneous gravitational collapse of gas clouds if these become Jeans-unstable (e.g. Elmegreen 1994), from cloud-cloud collissions, or it can be triggered by shocks or density-wave spiral armlets (such as in the case of M100, see Knapen et al. 1995a,b, 1999b). The importance of star-forming CNRs lies in the fact that they are excellent inflow laboratories, for a variety of reasons.

For example, Ryder Knapen (1999) used the upgraded UKIRT to obtain images in the J, H and K bands of the core region of M100 with an angular resolution of 0.3 , from which over 40 individual emitting knots, presumably star-forming, could be identified.

In M100, leading spiral arms within the CNR have also been observed in CO (e.g. Sakamoto et al. 1995; Knapen et al. 1999b). The main interpretational problem remains the uncertainty in the CO to 2 \ conversion factor, X , but it is certain that the standard value cannot be used without invoking large uncertainties in the derived gas mass (e.g. Reynaud Downes 1999).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4321 as an HII/LINER weakly barred spiral with CO arms and nucleus, and enhanced central CO line ratios indicating warmer or denser gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4321 (M100) is classified as HII/LINER h97 .

However, the southern half of NGC 4569 has a significantly lower line ratio while the central region of NGC 4321 and the northern emission peak of NGC 4569 have significantly higher line ratios. These line ratios suggest that the gas may be on average somewhat warmer and/or denser in the center of NGC 4321 and north of NGC 4569, while the southern disk of NGC 4569 may be somewhat cooler or less dense than typical disk regions.

NGC 4321 is classified as weakly barred SAB(s)bc, buta07 ; high resolution CO J =1-0 observations show two spiral arms and gas in the nucleus as well as faint emission bridging the arms and the nucleus gb05 .

sources.summary: NGC 4321 is described as the unperturbed reference spiral in the sample, with symmetric radio continuum, normal arm/interarm spectral behavior, and an azimuthally symmetric polarization gradient.

quotes.quote: NGC 4321 is the only galaxy in our sample which shows relatively symmetric emission distributions at all wavelengths as it is observed in field spiral galaxies (Fig.  fig:n4321 ). The 6 cm and 20 cm total power distributions follow the stellar and gas distribution. The radio continuum spectrum is flat ( > -0.8 with S_ ^ ) in the regions of the main spiral arms and steep ( 0.8 ) elsewhere. The polarized emission and the degree of polarization are highest in the interarm regions.

sources.summary: M100 is the second Virgo spiral target; the paper combines SPIRE and gas maps, finds molecular gas dominates inside 100 arcsec, and reports dust-emission mapping works best.

quotes.quote: M99 (NGC 4254) and M100 (NGC4321) are two spiral galaxies in the Virgo Cluster, which we take to have a distance of 16.8 Mpc.

In M99, the molecular material dominates the atomic gas within 80 arcsec of the galaxy centre and in M100 within 100 arcsec. We observed M99 and M100 during the Herschel Science Demonstration Phase with SPIRE.
Based on this criterion, mapping the ISM from the dust emission works best for M100 but mapping the ISM using the gas observations works best for M99.

sources.summary: For NGC 4321, the paper reports no extra thermal dust component, links molecular gas and dust attenuation to FIR emission near star formation, and notes high face-on optical depth.

quotes.quote: A similar analysis on NGC 4254 and NGC 4321 (Bendo et al. 2013, in prep.) does not find such a dust component.

The slope is relatively similar for NGC 4254, NGC 4321, and NGC5194, but it is about twice as steep for NGC 2403. Conversely, NGC 2403 levels at a much lower face--on optical depth than average, whereas NGC 4254 and NGC 4321 level at a higher face--on optical depth.

We see that there is a tight correlation between the total gas surface density derived with a modified black body and from the combination of HI and CO observation as shown by the high value of the Spearman correlation coefficient for NGC 2403 ( =0.79 ), NGC 4254 ( =0.95 ), NGC 4321 ( =0.96 ), and NGC 5194 ( =0.95 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4321's Cepheid distance and extinction map for SN 2006X, finding host-position extinction and a low R_V consistent with prior work.

quotes.quote: We used the Cepheid distance to NGC 4321 of 15.2 Mpc, and an absolute brightness at peak of M_B = -19.1 mag Wang2008ApJ...675..626W to best fit the simulated spectra to observations. At the position of SN 2006X in the extinction map of NGC 4321, A_V = 1.05 mag (for R_V = 3.1), and, Milky Way reddening along the line of sight is E(B-V)_ Gal = 0.026 mag. We determined from spectra at seven different epochs (Table table KINGFISH bestfit ) an average R_V = 1.46 0.29, and E(B-V) = 1.39 0.05 mag. This R_V ratio is consistent with the values determined by others.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4321 in its resolved star-forming galaxy sample and reports a strong peak in its SFR surface-density map.

quotes.quote: We have therefore selected eight galaxies in our sample: NGC 628, NGC 925, NGC 1097, NGC 3351, NGC 4321, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, and NGC 7793. Properties As we can see in Table tab:sample , the sample is made of fairly regular star--forming galaxies in the nearby universe, spanning the Hubble sequence from Sab to Sd. This ensures different levels of star-forming activity (from sSFR=-10.76 yr ^ 1 for NGC 4736 to sSFR=-9.59 yr ^ 1 for NGC 7793) and different levels of diffuse dust emission sauvage1992a .
A strong peak is also present in NGC 4321 and NGC 5055.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4321 best fitted by an absorbed power law plus hot gas, but the host hot-gas component dominates and leaves the spectral index poorly constrained.

quotes.quote: NGC 4321 Only a single spectrum of NGC 4321 could be produced from the Swift data. We find that an absorbed power law plus a hot gas component is best-fitting ( ^2_R = 1.62 ), consistent with previous spectral studies of NGC 4321 Gonzalez-Martin2009, Roberts2001 . In our data, however, the hot gas component from the host is dominant, meaning the underlying spectral index is not well constrained. The results of this spectral fitting are therefore not included in subsequent analysis.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4321 as a Virgo nuclear-burst target with a 10 percent central dense-gas fraction and nuclear bars that may lower central dense-gas star-formation efficiency.

quotes.quote: It also covers strongly barred galaxies (NGC 2903, NGC 3627), flocculent disks (NGC 5055, NGC 6946), strong nuclear bursts (NGC 2903, NGC 4321, NGC 6946), Virgo cluster members (NGC 4254, NGC 4321), and interacting galaxies (NGC 3627, NGC 5194).

The recent higher resolution ( 8 500 pc) study of nearby galaxies presented by GALLAGHER18 , shows that a median value of 10 is characteristic of the inner kpc of four nearby galaxy disks (NGC 3351, NGC 3627, NGC 4254 and NGC 4321). Our EMPIRE central dense gas fractions are 9 , 7 and 10 for NGC 3627, NGC 4254 and NGC 4321, respectively.

In addition to that, nuclear bars such as those present in NGC 4321 SAKAMOTO95,GARCIABURILLO98 may also be responsible for this orbit structure and contribute to the lower SFE observed in barred galaxy centers.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4321 (M100) as a nearby SABb-SABbc galaxy with two filamentary spiral arms and many regular chains of infrared dust-emission sources.

quotes.quote: However, a regular distribution of star-forming regions persists along both arms with the same characteristic distance. investigated the distribution of IR dust emission sources in the 8 m band, which represent regions of modern star formation with an age <1-2 Myr, in the slightly inclined ( i=24 according to the leda database http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ ) and relatively close (distance 16.2 Mpc according to the ned database http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ ) SABb-SABbc-type galaxy NGC 4321 (M100). sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4321 in the high recent-SF-change group, describes it as a LINER starburst, and notes likely past cluster interactions.

quotes.quote: NGC 628, 4254, 4303, 4321, 4535, and 5068 belong to the high end of the distribution, while NGC 1365, 1512, 1566, 3351, and 3627 belong to its low end.This is also summarised in the last column of Table tab:yes_no . Looking at the distribution across the galactic disks in the 3 ^ rd column of Figure fig:all_maps , we observe large areas of increasing recent SF in NGC 628, 4254, 4303, and 4321, while NGC 1512, 1566, and 3627 exhibit recent SF activity that ranges from roughly unchanged to decreasing.

AGN feedback Among AGN hosts in the sample, there are Seyfert galaxies NGC 1365 veron_06 and NGC 1566 Shobbrook_66 , low ionization nuclear emission region (LINER)/Seyfert type 2 galaxies NGC 4303 and NGC 3627 agn_3627 , and the starburst NGC 4321 which is another LINER galaxy Wozniak_99 .
NGC 4321 has probably interacted with other cluster companions in the past as well e.g., Knapen_93 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4321 has central atomic-gas depletion with CO enhancement, bar-associated SFR suppression, some regions following a power law, and gas surface density exceeding the critical value.

quotes.quote: We find that four of the galaxies (NGC 4321, 4501, 4736, 5457) show both an \ deficit and CO enhancement in their inner regions, suggesting a large-scale conversion of atomic to molecular gas. This phenomenon has long been noted e.g., Morris:78 , and its occurrence appears to be unrelated to the Hubble type of the galaxy, since 4736 is classified as early-type (Sab) whereas 5457 is classified as late-type (Scd).

The two exceptions are NGC 4321, where the SFR is suppressed in the vicinity of a bar ( R =15 -55 , where _ mol 1.4 ), and NGC 4736, where the SFR is enhanced near a resonance ring ( R =30 -55 , where _ mol 1.6 ). Even in NGC 4321 and 4736, however, there are regions where a power-law relationship is roughly obeyed.

At first glance, it is impressive how well the relation _ gas _ crit holds for some of the galaxies (NGC 4501, 5033, and 5055). On the other hand, there are two galaxies (NGC 4321 and 4414) where _ gas > _ crit over a significant range of radius, while in NGC 4736 and 5457, _ gas < _ crit across most of the disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4321 as a grand-design spiral with confined gas disk, slight lopsidedness, streaming motions, central S-shaped velocity distortion, and enhanced circumnuclear star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4321: This grand-design spiral galaxy has been frequently mapped in the H emission line using high-resolution FP interferometry ( 1990A&A...234...23A 1990A&A...234...23A , 1990A&AS...83..211C 1990A&AS...83..211C , 1997ApJ...479..723C 1997ApJ...479..723C , 2000ApJ...528..219K 2000ApJ...528..219K ), in the molecular CO emission-line ( 1990PhDT.........6C 1990PhDT.........6C , 1995AJ....110.2075S 1995AJ....110.2075S , 1995AJ....109.2444R 1995AJ....109.2444R , 1998A&A...333..864G 1998A&A...333..864G , Helfer et al. 2003) and in the 21-cm \ emission-line ( 1990AJ....100..604C 1990AJ....100..604C , 1993ApJ...416..563K 1993ApJ...416..563K ). The \ disc is almost totally confined within the optical one but with a slight lopsideness towards the SW ( 1993ApJ...416..563K 1993ApJ...416..563K ). The , CO and velocity fields show kinematical disturbances such as streaming motions along the spiral arms and a central S-shape distortion of the iso-velocity contours along the bar axis. The circum-nuclear region (CNR) and shows the presence of an enhanced star formation region as a four-armed ring-like structure and a CO spiral-like structure.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4321 as a Virgo grand-design spiral whose barred radio structure matches its high-resolution CO distribution and RC-CO profiles.

quotes.quote: NGC 4321 . This is a well-studied, grand-design spiral galaxy, located within the Virgo Cluster. A barred radio structure is present in our radio image (Fig. figura1a ). The same distribution is visible in the high-resolution CO image. This is evident also from the point-by-point correlation and from the radial plot.

sources.summary: NGC 4321 is described as a barred spiral with a weak spiral-affected outer bar, a clear near-infrared secondary bar, and sharp Fourier-mode maxima at the torque peak.

quotes.quote: 'The bar in NGC 4321 manifests itself with a rather weak outer part, affected in morphology by the spiral arms, and an aligned inner part which is well-defined in the near-IR (Knapen et al. 1995). This secondary bar, with a length of some 10 arcsec, has a clear SG-type morphology. The morphological type of this galaxy is SAB(s)bc, but the amplitude profile of the bar is similar as in any other morphological type: the density maximum is sharp and in addition to the A_2 component the higher Fourier modes, A_4 and A_6 (even A_8 is present), are also prominent. All modes appear nearly at the same radial distance, which is also the location of the Q_T maximum.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4321 (M100) as SN 2006X's well-studied spiral host, near whose arm the supernova occurred, with recent and present star formation and several recorded supernovae.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we show that if SN 2006X is from a WD + MS system with the optically thick wind, the age of its progenitor is smaller than 1 Gyr, which implies that there is star formation during the recent 1 Gyr in its host galaxy. The host galaxy of SN 2006X, NGC 4321 (M100), is a well-studied spiral galaxy (e.g. Ho et al. 1997) and SN 2006X locates near one of its arms (Wang et al. 2008). As one of the largest spiral galaxies in the Virgo cluster, it produced SNe 1901B, 1914A, 1959E, 1979C, and 2006X in roughly the last century and shows significant signal of star formation at present (Kanpen et al. 1993, 1996).

sources.summary: The paper finds Herschel detects dust in M 100 to about the optical radius and regular disk edge; its intermediate H I deficiency suggests outer-disk stripping by the cluster environment.

quotes.quote: We detect dust emission traced by all three SPIRE bands out to at least the optical radius defined by R25 for both galaxies (see maps for 3 5 detections in the nominal maps, and profs for the deeper, radially averaged profiles from the smoothed maps). Compared to , the dust can be found almost out to the edge of the regular disk for M 100. This is not entirely surprising, since M 100 is an intermediate deficient Haynes1984, Cayatte1994 galaxy ( def = 0.35 ) and thus its outer disk has probably been already stripped by the interaction with the cluster environment Boselli2006 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4321 as having an inner central bar, a larger main bar, several ring or spiral structures, asymmetric arms, and an outer logarithmic spiral.

quotes.quote: NGC 4321: The inner part of NGC 4321 is occupied by a central bar inside 13 , a main bar to around 67 and several ring/spiral structures. A set of prominent spiral arms starts at the end of the bar but these are asymmetric (with a much stronger southern arm) and tightly wound, in a similar way to those of NGC 1300. Close to 99 , a logarithmic spiral can be traced to 134 \ with the parameters ( a_2 , i ) = (0.5, 29.1 ).

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 4321's spiral and bar regions, notes a contaminated eastern stellar-continuum extinction map, and finds unusually high central dust mass where an AGN may affect estimates.

quotes.quote: We give positions for each pointing, including those mosaicked across the spiral and bar regions of NGC 3627 and NGC 4321.
We also note that the A _ V,s map for NGC 4321 on the east is omitted as those data were observed close to sunrise and the continuum light gradient is contaminated.

NGC 4321 exhibits particularly high dust mass surface densities in the very central region, however it is known to have an AGN which may affect the dust grain size distribution and resulting dust mass estimates.

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 4321 with galaxies whose cold-dust temperature maps have strong radial dependence, heating-temperature similarity, and emissivity distributions varying little with radius.

quotes.quote: When both parameters are fitted simultaneously, the cold dust temperature maps for 10 galaxies (NGC 1097, NGC 1512, NGC 3351, NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 4569, NGC 4579, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, and NGC 7331) exhibit a strong radial dependence.

As might be expected, we see a strong similarity between the heating maps and the T_c maps, particularly for NGC 1512, NGC 3351, NGC 4321, NGC 4579, NGC 4736, and NGC 5055, similar to the correlation between resolved 24 m emission and the cold dust temperature found for NGC 891 hughes2014 .

The Old Stellar Population and Submillimeter Slope Variations We find an almost universal decrease in the measured as a function of radius for our sources (the notable exceptions are NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 4579, and NGC 4736, where the distribution of does not vary greatly with radius).

sources.summary: The paper reports CO-only dominance, strong central overlap, and bar-linked spiral-arm offsets in NGC 4321.

quotes.quote: Of the detected sightlines, CO only sightlines (dark blue) are the most common detections in more than half of our targets (namely NGC 0628, NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 4535, NGC 5194).
The overlap in the central regions appears strong for the barred galaxies NGC 3351, NGC 3627, NGC 4321, and NGC 4535.
The spiral arms emanating from the stellar bars in NGC 4321 and NGC 4535 also exhibit offsets along significant arm segments.

sources.summary: The paper reports unusual central radio behavior in NGC 4321, a radio-bright northeastern disk half, and streaming-dominated cosmic-ray transport in its disk.

quotes.quote: Galaxy central region We realized that the radio properties of the central few kpc of NGC 4321 behave differently than those of the galactic disk.
The northeastern half of the galactic disk of NGC 4321 is radio-bright, whereas the southwestern half is radio-dim.
Streaming dominates in the disks of the perturbed Virgo spiral galaxies NGC 4321, NGC 4303, NGC 4254, NGC 4501, and NGC 4654.

sources.summary: For NGC4321, the paper discusses large separation scales along a spiral arm, a low value at co-rotation, and an inner-bin cloud-lifetime elevation.

quotes.quote: A similarly larger separation scale between individual CO peaks ( 400 ) is indeed observed by Henshaw2019 along the southern spiral arm of NGC4321.
In a less extreme way, the low value of \ measured at the co-rotation radius in NGC4321 likely results from a similar effect, but at a later stage of the star formation cycle.
For NGC4321 we note an elevation (by 60 per cent) of the molecular cloud lifetime in the inner bin compared to the galactic average.

sources.summary: The paper measures M100's UV morphology, treating its radial profile as intermediate, noting nuclear UV light likely from star formation, and finding it becomes undetectable when redshifted.

quotes.quote: Whereas M33 and M101 exhibit a rather linear profile, as expected for exponential disks, M51 and M81 have a non-monotonic distribution and M100 may be viewed as an intermediate case.
On the other hand, there is some UV light in the core of M100 which seems to be produced within a nuclear star-formation region (e.g Ryder Knappen ).
The first point to note is that M33, M81 and M100 become undetectable if they are redshifted in the HST V-band (z=1.68) without any other modifications.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4321 as one of the two dustiest sample galaxies; weak line detections yield upper limits on flux/EW and lower limits on integrated oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: In every object except NGC 3351 and NGC 4321, the two dustiest galaxies in the sample, we detect the , , and emission lines with >3 significance. In NGC 3351 and NGC 4321, however, we derive an upper limit to the flux and EW, which translates into a lower limit on the oxygen abundance.

We plot the integrated ``observed'', ``reddening-corrected'', and ``EW'' abundances using open circles, filled circles, and open squares, respectively, and the lower limits to the integrated abundances in NGC 3351 and NGC 4321 as arrows ( sec:intoh and Table  table:intoh ).

sources.summary: The paper uses M100 as a well-studied example whose molecular gas is concentrated in the inner disk, likely shaped by radial inflow, dynamical friction, and bar interaction.

quotes.quote: The best studied example is M100 , for which a high-resolution radial I_ CO profile was obtained with the BIMA and Nobeyama Observatory interferometers. Most of the gas in this galaxy is in the molecular state, and is concentrated in an inner region with a diameter of 2 3 kpc (although the gas also remains mostly molecular at larger distances from the center). The radial motion of the gas toward the center could be due to both dynamical friction acting on giant molecular clouds and to the interaction of gas with a currently or previously existing bar (recall that more than half of our galaxies have appreciable bars).

sources.summary: NGC 4321 is described as a Virgo grand-design spiral previously studied in radio continuum, H i, and CO, with beam-limited radio contrast and H i truncated near the optical disk.

quotes.quote: This classical grand-design spiral, also located in the Virgo Cluster, has been the target of numerous studies, e.g. in the radio continuum (van der Hulst ), H i (Knapen et al. ), and in CO (Cepa et al. ). The rather elongated synthesised beam reduces contrast in our radio continuum map. The H i emission is again (as in other Virgo spirals) truncated near the edge of the optical disk.

gmw_00000347.name: Leo A
gmw_00000347.overview: Leo A is an isolated, gas-rich dwarf irregular galaxy located in the Local Group [arXiv:1712.06594, arXiv:1810.07472]. It is characterized by an extremely low metallicity [arXiv:1805.04323, arXiv:1810.07472] and a delayed star formation history, with the majority of its stars forming within the last 8 billion years [arXiv:1805.04323, arXiv:2310.06785].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Leo A Cepheids with SMC period-luminosity templates, finds insignificant period dependence in distances, and adopts a distance modulus of 24.59 +/- 0.03.

quotes.quote: Dolphin:etal:02 determined the mean V and R magnitudes for the first 66 unambiguous classical Cepheids in Leo A, of which the authors classified 19 as P0 and 38 as P1 pulsators. The P0 Cepheids have periods of between 0.86 and 2.13 days, and the P1 Cepheids of between 0.46 and 1.22 days. These are the shortest-period Cepheids known. The data can be described well by shifting the templet relations of SMC in apparent magnitude.

It must therefore be assumed that the internal absorption is negligible, which seems plausible in the light of the preceding dwarf galaxies. t The individual distances of the P0 and P1 Cepheids follow directly from a comparison of their V^ 0 magnitudes with the corresponding calibrated P-L relations of SMC. The dependence of the resulting distances on P is insignificant as seen in Fig. fig:08 b . The mean distance modulus, read at median period, is (m-M)^ P0 =24.67 0.05 and (m-M)^ P1 =24.55 0.02 . We adopt the number-weighted mean of (m-M)^ 0 =24.59 0.03 .

sources.summary: The paper studies Leo A as a metal-poor Local Group dwarf irregular, measuring variable stars, distance, sparse ancient population, and metallicity evolution for use in variable-star luminosity calibration.

quotes.quote: Leo A, a small star forming dwarf galaxy discovered by zwi42 and located about 790 kpc away e.g. hereinafter D02 dol02 , is among the most metal-poor galaxies of the Local Group (LG). Its current metallicity is A _ O =12+log(O/H)=7.30 0.05 (i.e. \ [ Fe/H ] 1.5, or 3 solar assuming A _ O, =8.87 0.07; gre93 ) from the spectroscopy of three \ regions and a planetary nebula ski89,van06 . tol98 drew attention to the relative lack of an ancient stellar population in Leo A, and this was supported by the discovery of the rarity of RR Lyrae stars by dol02 .

We have calculated the distance to Leo A using the TRGB luminosity and various methods based on the photometric and pulsational properties of the Cepheids and RR Lyrae stars. Our best distance based on the TRGB method is (m M) _0= 24.57 0.13, corresponding to 820 50 kpc. This distance is in good agreement, within the uncertainties, with that obtained from the RR Lyrae stars ((m M) _0 =24.48 0.12) and literature values. The distance obtained from the Cepheid PL relations, however, is somewhat larger than these values, (m M) _0 =24.70 0.10. This may indicate that the short-period Cepheids of Leo A are intrinsically fainter than their equivalent in IC 1613 and the Magellanic Clouds.

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo A as a dwarf example with stellar mass like Pegasus but lower inferred potential depth, arguing SIDM halos can host it consistently.

quotes.quote: Consider, for example, the two galaxies Pegasus ( 1 kpc) and Leo A ( 500 pc) in Figure fig:rotationcurves . Both of these galaxies have about the same stellar mass M_ 10^7 . According to the expectations of abundance matching GarrisonKimmelTBTF , each of these galaxies should reside within a V_ max 40 halo. Instead, their central densities are such that, if their dark matter structure follows the CDM-inspired NFW form, they need to have drastically different potential well depths: V_ max 30 and 12 for Pegasus and Leo A, respectively see Figure 12 of GarrisonKimmelTBTF .

That is, in SIDM, just like CDM, we would naively expect both Pegasus and Leo A to reside in halos with V_ max = 40 , like Pippin and Merry. In SIDM, unlike in CDM, the predicted density profiles allow this to happen self-consistently. In the right-hand panel of Figure fig:rotationcurves we see that both Pegasus and Leo A could be hosted by Merry with /m = 10 . In the left-hand panel, both galaxies are consistent with the /m = 50 line.

sources.summary: The paper describes Leo A as an isolated dwarf irregular with delayed star formation, a small ancient population, low gas metallicity, and stellar/gas metallicities suggesting slow chemical evolution.

quotes.quote: Using HST/WFPC2 photometry, tol98 first noticed that Leo A conspicuously lacked a prominent ancient ( >10 Gyr) population. For that reason, it stood out among the Local Group and challenged the idea that all dwarf galaxies are old.

It was not until col07 obtained HST imaging down to the old MSTO that the fraction of ancient stars was conclusively determined to be less than 10 . The SFH that col07 \ derived shows that most of the star formation happened more recently than 6 Gyr ago, and it ramped up from then until 2 Gyr ago. The present star formation rate (SFR) is measured as 9.3 10^ 5 M_ yr ^ 1 from H imaging and 6.0 10^ 4 M_ yr ^ 1 from ultraviolet (UV) imaging kar13 .

A peculiarly low H ii region metallicity O/H = -1.31 0.10 , vanzee06 and consequently low effective yield lee06 also make Leo A stand out among dIrrs. The previously measured average stellar metallicity Fe/H = -1.58 , kir13b is not far below the gas-phase metallicity. The potential similarity in metallicity between stars and gas possibly corroborates the slow evolution in metallicity observed by col07 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes bright-red and RGB stellar populations in Leo A, finding different radial scale lengths, peculiar and dust-enshrouded stars, and evidence for internally driven structural evolution.

quotes.quote: 4mu^ We analysed a population of bright-red (BR) stars in the dwarf irregular galaxy Leo A by using multicolour photometry data obtained with the Subaru/Suprime-Cam ( B , V , R , I , H ) and HST/ACS ( F475W F814W ) instruments. In order to separate the Milky Way (MW) and Leo A populations of red stars, we developed a photometric method, which enabled us to study the spatial distribution of BR stars within the Leo A galaxy. We found a significant difference in the scale-length (S-L) of radial distributions of the ``young" and ``old" red giant branch (RGB) stars -- 0 82 0 04 and 1 53 0 03 , respectively.

Distinct structural components with notable differences in age and spatial extent imply an outside-in star formation or efficient star migration to the outskirts of the Leo A galaxy in early stages of its evolution. Together with previously identified stellar populations at 5' < a < 8' ( ), this might indicate that even isolated small size and low mass galaxies, like Leo A, have a non-trivial star formation history and structure evolution scenario driven solely by internal processes of stellar evolution. We also identified a group of 13 peculiar objects fully overlapping with the RGB stars in the I vs. V-I diagram (Fig. fig1 ), yet forming a separate sequence in diagrams with the B passband (Fig. fig1 b d). However, this sequence is not apparent in the HST/ACS data. Photometry data of these stars are not contaminated by neighboring objects or defects. The peculiar objects are projected on the Leo A galaxy (Fig. fig2 b), and are expected to be genuine members.

sources.summary: The paper studies Leo A young populations, recent star-formation history, low-mass clusters, low metallicity, gas richness, and present-day star-formation tracers.

quotes.quote: We have studied young stellar populations and star clusters in the dwarf irregular galaxy Leo A using multicolor ( B , V , R , I , H ) photometry data obtained with the Subaru Suprime-Cam and two-color photometry results measured on archival HST/ACS F475W F814W frames. The analysis of the main sequence (MS) and blue supergiant (BSG -- ``blue loop'') stars enabled us to study the star formation history in the Leo A galaxy during the last 200 Myr. Also, we have discovered 5 low-mass ( 400 M _ ) star clusters within the ACS field. This finding, taking into account a low metallicity environment and a yet-undetected molecular gas in Leo A, constrains star formation efficiency estimates and scenarios.

Leo A is an isolated dwarf irregular galaxy in the Local Group. It is a gas-rich ( ; ) dark-matter-dominated stellar system ( ; ) of low metallicity ( ; ). The present-day star formation activity is indicated by few H ii regions, while the existence of an old stellar population is proved by the detection of RR Lyr stars ( ; ).
By employing HST/ACS resolved stellar photometry data, we discovered 5 low-mass ( 400 M _ ) star clusters residing in the central part of the Leo A galaxy (Fig. fig3 ).

sources.summary: Leo A is described as an isolated, gas-rich, low-mass dwarf irregular galaxy where HST photometry finds four additional low-mass star clusters, bringing its known cluster sample to five.

quotes.quote: Leo A is an isolated gas-rich dwarf irregular galaxy of low stellar mass and metallicity residing at the outskirts of the Local Group. Ages of the stellar populations in Leo A range from 10 Myr to 10 Gyr. So far, only one star cluster has been discovered in this galaxy. Our aim is to search for star cluster candidates in the Leo A galaxy. We performed photometry of star-like objects on HST ACS archive observation frames in the F475W and F814W passbands and studied the spatial clustering of the Leo A stars down to the turn-off point of the old stellar populations.

We report the discovery of four star clusters in the Leo A galaxy. This means that now a sample of five star clusters is known in Leo A. Two clusters are of a young age ( 20 Myr; overlapping with H II regions) and low in mass ( 400 M_ and 150 M_ ), the other three clusters are older ( 100 Myr) and also of low mass ( 300 M_ ).

sources.summary: The paper derives precise multi-epoch HST proper motions for Leo A and argues its late star formation is unusual but not caused by a past Milky Way or M31 interaction.

quotes.quote: We have also used multi-epoch \ data to obtain more accurate PM estimates for Leo A and Sag DIG. In summary, we used three epochs of data with a total time baseline of 15 years for Leo A, whereas only two epochs were used with a time baseline of 9 years for Sag DIG. For each dwarf galaxy, we create high-resolution stacked images with pixel scales of 0.025 mas using images from the reference epoch, i.e., the first-epoch (2005) data for Leo A and the second-epoch (2020) data for Sag DIG.

The exception to this pattern is Leo A. Instead, Leo A has actually assembled 85 of its stellar mass in the last 6 Gyrs, with a main maximum centered on 5 Gyrs look-back time Cole2007,Skillman_2014,Ruiz-Lara_2018 . This unusual SFH could be evidence of Leo A being a backsplash galaxy from M31, with the enhancement in star formation being caused by the compression of Leo A's gas reservoir by the hot gas halo of M31. However, we find in this work that such a previous interaction is not consistent with the measured PMs. Our measurements cannot discriminate between these scenarios, but we can rule out that a previous interaction with either the MW or M31 is the cause of the unusual late star formation in Leo A.

sources.summary: The review describes Leo A as an isolated Local Group dwarf with delayed star formation, a recent SFR peak, ancient stars, and unusually young stellar mass fractions.

quotes.quote: Leo A (DDO 69), is one of the most isolated galaxies in the Local Group mat98 , 800 kpc from the Milky Way and 1200 kpc from M31. It was suggested by tol98 that Leo A had formed the vast majority of its stars within the past few Gyr, making it the most likely candidate yet to be a genuinely young galaxy. This candidacy was quashed when dol02+ discovered a small number of RR Lyrae type variables in Leo A, proving the presence of ancient (age > 10 Gyr) stars.

However, deep HST imaging by col07 showed conclusively that Leo A formed 90 of its stellar mass more recently than 7 Gyr ago, corresponding to a redshift of z 1. The SFR appeared to peak between 1--3 Gyr ago and then declined, with a second episode of star formation a few hundred Myr ago. This made Leo A unique among known galaxies in having such a remarkably high fraction of stars younger than 10 Gyr. In the SFH derived by col07 , the average SFR of Leo A from 2--5 Gyr ago was 4--5 times the previous long-term average SFR.

sources.summary: The paper studies Leo A as an isolated, extremely young Local Group dwarf, comparing integrated-light and CMD star-formation histories for its low-metallicity stellar population.

quotes.quote: We compare SFHs recovered from both techniques in Leo A, a Local Group dwarf galaxy whose majority of stars formed during the last 8 Gyrs. This complements our previous findings in a field in the Large Magellanic Cloud bar, where star formation has been on-going since early epochs though at varying rates. We have used GTC/OSIRIS in long-slit mode to obtain a high-quality integrated light spectrum by scanning a selected region within Leo A, for which a CMD reaching the old main mequence turn-off (oMSTO) is available from HST. We compared the SFH obtained from the two datasets, using state-of-art methods of integrated light ( STECKMAP ) and resolved stellar population analysis. In the case of the CMD, we computed the SFH both from a deep CMD (observed with HST/ACS), and from a shallower one (archival data from HST/WFPC2). The agreement between the SFHs recovered from the oMSTO CMD and from full spectrum fitting is remarkable, particularly regarding the time evolution of the star formation rate. The overall extremely low metallicity of Leo A is recovered up to the last 2 Gyrs, when some discrepancies appear.

Leo A is an isolated galaxy with a well-reported population of young and massive stars 1984AJ.....89.1160D, 1996ApJ...462..684T . Using an HST CMD reaching the oMSTO, 2007ApJ...659L..17C see also 2015ApJ...811L..18G found that around 80 of the star formation in Leo A occurred within the last 8 Gyr of evolution, with a peak on the Star Formation Rate (SFR) between 1 to 3 Gyr ago and an almost constant low metallicity (Z Throughout the paper we use Z to denote the metallicity of the stellar component of Leo A (unless expressed otherwise).

Leo A is one of the few close dwarf galaxies for which old main sequence stars can be resolved via HST photometry allowing for the observation of a deep CMD. At the same time, despite Leo A's low surface brightness, the light collecting power of the new generation of giant telescopes makes possible to obtain a high-quality integrated spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper describes Leo A as a low-luminosity, gas-rich blue dwarf irregular with delayed star formation, where most observed-field star formation occurred within the last 8 Gyr.

quotes.quote: Late-type Dwarf Galaxies Leo A and IC 1613 are the two dIrr galaxies that are still actively forming stars today. Compared to the other types of dwarfs, their metallicities increase continuously and have reached a relatively higher value in the present day. Leo A is one of the least luminous gas-rich galaxies we know. It is a small, blue galaxy conspicuous for its population of young, massive stars, with estimated ages ranging from 10^7 to 10^8 yr.

Leo A presents the most striking example of delayed SF cole07 . The SFH derived by the CMD fitting method is shown in panel (a) of Figure Fig:dIrr (cyan). Within the ACS/WFC field of view, 90 of Leo A's SF occurred more recently than 8 Gyr ago.

sources.summary: The paper identifies DDO 69, alias Leo A, as a slow-rotator prototype whose H I distribution has higher-density ring or ridge regions, central depression, and an extended low-column-density halo.

quotes.quote: The second sample contains dwarf galaxies with comparable rotational and random velocities. This sample, having Leo A as a prototype, is consistent with an extrapolation of the classical Tully-Fisher relation to lower luminosities. These dwarf galaxies are not particularly rich in gas, but their mass is dominated by baryonic matter within their HI radius.

The HI distribution of the slow rotators DDO 63, DDO 69, DDO 125, and DDO 165 is characteristized by a small number of regions with higher HI column density, usually in a ring or a ridge, around the edge of the optical galaxy. A smaller HI column density is observed near the centre. DDO 63, DDO 69 and DDO 125 appear to have an extended HI halo with a low HI column density. DDO 69 (Young Lo 1996) is a representative example.

sources.summary: Leo A is compared to CHVCs as a Local Group dwarf irregular with narrow-line H I clumps in a diffuse halo, CNM within WNM, similar velocity fields, and comparable gas and stellar masses.

quotes.quote: The comparison with the Local Group dwarf irregulars, Leo A and Sag DIG, studied at high resolution by Young and Lo ( , ) is particularly apt. Both of these objects display the same general morphology and kinematics as the CHVCs: high column density clumps characterized by narrow linewidths (about 8 km s ^ 1 FWHM) and angular sizes of a few arcmin embedded in a diffuse halo of lower column density gas with broader linewidths (about 20 km s ^ 1 FWHM) extending over 10's of arcmin. Young and Lo reach the same conclusion presented here regarding the phase content of the dwarf irregulars, namely that cool condensations of CNM (with T_ k 100 K) are found within WNM envelopes (with T_ k 8000 K). The peak brightness temperatures detected from the CNM in Leo A, 73 K, are almost identical to those we see in the compact clumps of CHVC 125+41 207. The velocity fields of both Leo A and Sag DIG are also very similar to those of the CHVCs.

Unlike the CHVCs, these galaxies contain comparable gas and stellar masses, each corresponding to about 10 ^8 M _ in the case of Leo A and about 10 ^7 M _ in Sag DIG. Young and Lo also comment on a small region of about 2.5 arcmin diameter within Leo A which they suggest might be the expansion or contraction signature of an shell, comparable to the five cases of line--splitting within CHVCs which we describe above.

sources.summary: The paper highlights LeoA among the lowest-luminosity nearby XMPs, describing this low-surface-brightness subset as unusually gas-rich and inefficient at converting H i gas into stars.

quotes.quote: We call attention to the six sources with the lowest g band luminosity -- UGC2684, DD053, LeoA, SextansB, UGCA292 and GR8. These are all nearby (D < 6 Mpc), low-surface brightness galaxies, that also turn out to be XMPs.

We find an anti-correlation between the M _ H i /L _g ratio and the luminosity, whereby fainter XMPs are more gas-rich than brighter XMPs. The result suggests that the less luminous sources have converted a smaller fraction of their H i gas into stars. However, our sources deviate from the observed correlation found for dwarf galaxies (cyan line; bottom; Fig. 5; Staveley-Smith, Davies Kinman 1992) particularly at low luminosities, which correspond to the low-surface brightness, nearby galaxies UGC2684, DD053, LeoA, SextansB, UGCA292 and GR8. In this low luminosity regime, the XMP galaxies appear to be 10 to 100 times more gas-rich than typical dwarf galaxies.

sources.summary: DDO 69 is one of five low-metallicity dwarf irregulars mapped in far-infrared lines; its southern field shows a bright emission peak and northwest weak emission.

quotes.quote: We present far-infrared spectral line observations of five galaxies from the \ sample: DDO 69, DDO 70, DDO 75, DDO 155, and WLM. While most studies of dwarfs focus on bright systems or starbursts due to observational constraints, our data extend the observed parameter space into the regime of low surface brightness dwarf galaxies with low metallicities and moderate star formation rates. Our targets were observed with \ at the \ 158 m, \ 63 m, \ 88 m, and \ 122 m emission lines using the PACS Spectrometer. These high-resolution maps allow us for the first time to study the far-infrared properties of these systems on the scales of larger star-forming complexes.

DDO 69 has a bright \ peak in the south of the field of view, with emission extending to the south and west. The weak \ peak is roughly cospatial with the western portion of the , and weak \ is detected 30 \ to the northwest.

sources.summary: The paper presents near-infrared observations of Leo A, describing it as a metal-poor, gas-rich Local Group dwarf irregular with young-to-intermediate populations and evolved-star dust production.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: RA = 09 h 59 m 26.1 s and Dec = + 30 ^ 44 m 55.5 s for Leo A

quotes.quote: Leo A (also named Leo III or DDO 69) is an isolated, dwarf irregular galaxy in the Local Group, at a distance of about 790 kpc Bernard2013, Dolphin2002 . This gas-rich galaxy Young1996 contains both young and old populations Cole2007 and is thought to be a dark-matter-dominated ( > 80 ) stellar system Brown2007 . Leo A is metal poor ( [ Fe/H ] 2.1 to 1.2 ; Kirby2017 ), and the majority of its stellar population is of a young to intermediate age, forming within the last 5--8 Gyr.

We identify 32 stars in Leo A and 101 stars in Sextans A with a DPR >10^ 11 M_ yr ^ 1 , confirming that metal-poor stars can form substantial amounts of dust. We also find tentative evidence for oxygen-rich dust formation at low metallicity, contradicting previous models that suggest oxygen-rich dust production is inhibited in metal-poor environments. The total rates of dust injection into the interstellar medium of Leo A and Sextans A are (8.2 1.8) 10^ 9 M_ yr ^ 1 and (6.2 0.2) 10^ 7 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper treats Leo A as an extreme delayed-star-formation dwarf whose late peak and gas richness can be qualitatively reproduced by reionization-sensitive stochastic mass assembly models.

quotes.quote: Extreme examples are Leo A and Aquarius which reached the peak of star formation at z<1 (more than 6 Gyr after the Big Bang). This fact seemingly challenges the CDM cosmological framework because the dark matter halos of these galaxies on average should assemble the majority of their masses before z 2 (<3 Gyr after the Big Bang).

These ``reborn'' model galaxies show very similar delayed star formation histories to those of Leo A and Aquarius.
